# Human–computer interactions predict mental health

**MAILA — a MAchine learning framework for Inferring Latent mental states from digital Activity**

<!-- --- -->

**Veith Weilnhammer**$^{1,2, *}$, **Jefferson Ortega**$^{3}$, **David Whitney**$^{1,3,4}$

$^{1}$ Helen Wills Neuroscience Institute, University of California Berkeley, USA  
$^{2}$ Max Planck UCL Centre for Computational Psychiatry and Ageing Research, London, UK  
$^{3}$ Department of Psychology, University of California Berkeley, USA  
$^{4}$ Vision Science Group, University of California Berkeley, USA  
$^{*}$ Corresponding author. Email: veith.weilnhammer@gmail.com  

\vspace{2em}

Scalable assessments of mental illness remain a critical roadblock toward accessible and equitable care. Here, we show that everyday human–computer interactions encode mental health with biomarker accuracy. We introduce MAILA, a MAchine-learning framework for Inferring Latent mental states from digital Activity. We trained MAILA on 18,200 cursor and touchscreen recordings labeled with 1.3 million mental-health self-reports collected from 9,500 participants. MAILA tracks dynamic mental states along 13 clinically relevant dimensions, resolves circadian fluctuations and experimental manipulations of arousal and valence, achieves near-ceiling accuracy at the group level, captures information that is only partially reflected in verbal self-report, and improves the ability of large language models to infer user mental health. By extracting signatures of psychological function that have so far remained untapped, MAILA establishes human–computer interactions as a new modality for scalable digital phenotyping and a foundation for context-aware artificial intelligence.

\newpage

```{r}
#| label: grid_id_settings
#| include: false

grid_id = 5 
movement_features = mod(grid_id,6) * 10
```

```{r}
#| label: Graphical Abstract
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"

#knitr::include_graphics("cursor_mind_files/Graphical_Abstract_illustration.png")
```

\newpage
# Introduction

Mental illness is the leading cause of disability worldwide [@gbd_2019_mental_disorders_collaborators_global_2022; @vigo_estimating_2016]. Despite their impact, symptoms often go undetected for years [@ghio_duration_2014; @pablo_what_2024]. Delayed access to care increases the risk of poor outcomes [@kraus_prognosis_2019].

Language, the medium through which mental health is commonly expressed and understood, is not sufficient to close the gap between symptom onset and access to care. Mental illness can make it difficult to recognize and articulate the experiences that give rise to distress [@preece_alexithymia_2024]. Feelings of shame, stigma, and language barriers may prevent people from reaching out [@clement_what_2015; @miteva_impact_2022]. In support systems with limited resources, shared moments of communication are often difficult to achieve [@keynejad_who_2018]. While fluent in conversation and, to some extent, reflective of human cognition [@binz_foundation_2025], large language models still lack the contextual understanding and interpretability required for responsible deployment [@dohnany_technological_2025; @galatzer-levy_generative_2025; @weilnhammer_vulnerability-amplifying_2026].

Efforts to develop more accessible and efficient mental health care are therefore expanding from language-based assessments, such as interviews and questionnaires, to non-verbal markers, including polygenic risk scores [@lewis_polygenic_2020; @murray_could_2021; @sanchez-roige_emerging_2020], neuroimaging [@schmaal_subcortical_2016; @kambeitz_detecting_2017; @marek_reproducible_2022], wearable sensors [@lyall_association_2018; @abd-alrazaq_systematic_2023; @liu_digital_2025; @xie_jets_2025; @kim_codas_2026], sleep monitoring [@thapa_multimodal_2026],  cognitive tasks [@open_science_collaboration_estimating_2015], and digital behaviors [@eichstaedt_facebook_2018; @kelley_using_2022; @mirea_cognitive_2025]. Human–computer interactions like cursor and touchscreen activity are of particular interest, because they are generated by virtually every consumer-grade device, recorded continuously at zero cost, and independent of language, introspection, and social expectations [@freeman_doing_2018]. Establishing a systematic mind-body connection in these digital behaviors will allow mental states, and their changes, to be decoded every time a person uses a computer, tablet, or smartphone [@jain_digital_2015; @insel_digital_2017; @wainberg_challenges_2017; @barrett_digitising_2017; @freeman_doing_2018; @topol_high-performance_2019; @onnela_opportunities_2021; @kelley_using_2022; @hauser_promise_2022; @koutsouleris_promise_2022; @galatzer-levy_machine_2023; @mirea_cognitive_2025; @picard_affective_1997].

The idea that mental states are expressed in movement is supported by centuries of research on facial expression, posture, gait, and gestures [@darwin_expression_1998; @ekman_emotional_2004; @de_gelder_why_2009]. According to motor-control theory, actions rely on internal models that are continuously shaped by ongoing affective and cognitive processes [@wolpert_unifying_2003; @shadmehr_error_2010]. Human–computer interactions, like other forms of motor behavior [@darwin_expression_1998; @ekman_emotional_2004; @de_gelder_why_2009; @wolpert_unifying_2003; @shadmehr_error_2010], are therefore expected to encode signatures of mental states, including those central to mental health.

So far, however, the extent to which human–computer interactions reflect mental states remains an open question. Previous attempts have been limited by small, homogeneous samples that restrict statistical power and external validity [@schoemann_using_2021]. Many have focused on narrowly defined features that may overlook the high-dimensional nature of human–computer interactions [@freihaut_tracking_2021; @de_angel_digital_2022]. In addition, prior work has mostly targeted binary diagnostic traits rather than the dynamic and continuous fluctuations in mental health that matter most in psychology, medicine, and neuroscience [@insel_research_2010; @kotov_paradigm_2018].

Here, we introduce MAILA, a machine learning framework for inferring latent mental states from digital activity, and the MAILA dataset, a large-scale collection of human–computer interactions annotated with self-reports about mental health. Our results demonstrate that cursor movements and touchscreen activity, two universal components of human–computer interactions, reflect the mental state of the person behind the screen. MAILA enables reliable and dynamic decoding of psychological function from routine digital behavior, setting a new benchmark in the cost-efficiency, scalability, and ecological validity of mental-health assessment [@opel_transforming_2026].

```{r}
#| label: demographics
#| include: false
library(dplyr)

read_with_source <- function(path, source) {
  df <- read.csv(path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- source
  df
}

##
## Step 1: Read demographics data
##
demographics_sample_1 <- read.csv(
  file.path(root, 'experiments', 'bsi_mhc_5', 'demographics', 'demographics_bsi_mhc_5.csv')
)

demographics_sample_2 <- read.csv(
  file.path(root, 'experiments', 'bsi_mhc_4_cb', 'demographics', 'demographics_bsi_mhc_4_cb.csv')
)

demographics_subset_1 <- demographics_sample_1 %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant.id, Started.at, Completed.at, Time.taken, Completion.code, Age, Sex,
                Nationality, Language, `Ethnicity.simplified`, `Country.of.residence`, `Student.status`, `Employment.status`, Total.approvals) %>%
  mutate(sample = 1)

demographics_subset_2 <- demographics_sample_2 %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant.id, Started.at, Completed.at, Time.taken, Completion.code, Age, Sex,
                Nationality, Language, `Ethnicity.simplified`, `Country.of.residence`, `Student.status`, `Employment.status`, Total.approvals) %>%
  mutate(sample = 2)

# Load data
error_dem = rbind(read_with_source(file.path(root, 'public_mental_health', 'error_demo', 'test_error_demo.csv'), "baseline"),
                  read_with_source(file.path(root, 'public_mental_health', 'error_demo', 'retest_error_demo.csv'), "follow-up"),
                  read_with_source(file.path(root, 'public_mental_health', 'error_demo', 'quest_error_demo.csv'), "survey"),
                  read_with_source(file.path(root, 'public_mental_health', 'error_demo', 'beads_error_demo.csv'), "game"),
                  read_with_source(file.path(root, 'public_mental_health', 'error_demo', 'touch_error_demo.csv'), "touch"),
                  read_with_source(file.path(root, 'public_mental_health', 'error_demo', 'touch_draw_error_demo.csv'), "touch-d")
)

error_dem = subset(error_dem, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))

error_dem = subset(error_dem, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))
demographics <- subset(error_dem, Dimension %in% c("PC1"))

demographics$Age <- as.numeric(demographics$Age)

summary_demographics <- ddply(
  demographics[!is.na(demographics$Age), ],
  .(Source),
  summarise,
  n = sum(!is.na(Age)),
  n_nationality = length(unique(Nationality)),
  mean_age = mean(Age, na.rm = TRUE),
  sem_age = sd(Age, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(Age))),
  fraction_female = mean(Sex == 'Female', na.rm = TRUE)
)

age_range <- range(demographics$Age, na.rm = TRUE)

started_at_sample_1 <- as.POSIXct(demographics_subset_1$Started.at,
                                  format = '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%OSZ', tz = 'UTC')
started_at_sample_2 <- as.POSIXct(demographics_subset_2$Started.at,
                                  format = '%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%OSZ', tz = 'UTC')

time_differences <- abs(started_at_sample_2 - started_at_sample_1)
mean_time_elapsed <- mean(as.numeric(time_differences), na.rm = TRUE)
sem_time_elapsed <- sd(as.numeric(time_differences), na.rm = TRUE) /
  sqrt(summary_demographics$n[summary_demographics$Source == "follow-up"])

between_samples <- data.frame(
  mean_time_elapsed = mean_time_elapsed,
  sem_time_elapsed = sem_time_elapsed
)

# Gender counts
gender_counts <- table(demographics$Sex)
n_female <- gender_counts["Female"]
n_male <- gender_counts["Male"]
n_other <- gender_counts["Prefer not to say"]


exclude_vals <- c("CONSENT_REVOKED", "DATA_EXPIRED", "")
diversity_summary <- data.frame(
  Variable = c("Nationality", "Language", "Employment", "Student", "Ethnicity", "Location", "Age_min", "Age_max"),
  Unique_values = c(
    length(unique(setdiff(demographics$Nationality, exclude_vals))),
    length(unique(setdiff(demographics$Language, exclude_vals))),
    length(unique(setdiff(demographics$`Employment.status`, exclude_vals))),
    length(unique(setdiff(demographics$`Student.status`, exclude_vals))),
    length(unique(setdiff(demographics$`Ethnicity.simplified`, exclude_vals))),
    length(unique(setdiff(demographics$`Country.of.residence`, exclude_vals))),
    min(demographics$Age, na.rm = TRUE),
    max(demographics$Age, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
)
```

```{r}
#| label: questionnaires
#| echo: false
#| include: true

library(readxl)
library(plyr)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)

questionnaires <- read_excel(
  file.path(root, 'experiments', 'bsi_mhc_5', 'conditions_3b.xlsx')
)

questionnaire_summary <- ddply(
  questionnaires,
  .(Questionnaire),
  summarise,
  total_questions = length(Question),
  total_dimensions = length(unique(Dimension))
)
n_bsi_questions <- questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'BSI', ]$total_questions
n_bsi_dims <- questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'BSI', ]$total_dimensions
n_mhc_questions <- questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'MHC-SF', ]$total_questions
n_mhc_dims <- questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'MHC-SF', ]$total_dimensions

n_total_questions <- nrow(questionnaires)
```

# Results

We recorded cursor and touchscreen activity during a variety of digital activities and at multiple times in 9,500 unique participants who answered  `r nrow(questionnaires)` questions about their current psychological distress and wellbeing. We projected each participant into two spaces, one defined by patterns of human–computer interaction, and one defined by self-reported mental health, and trained MAILA to map from one to the other (Figure 1).

```{r}
#| label: Figure_1
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 1. Decoding mental states from digital behavior.** MAILA predicts mental states from cursor and touchscreen activity, two integral components of everyday interactions with computers and handheld devices. Each participant (N = 9,500 total) is represented in two spaces: (i), the space of digital behavior $X^{N \\times C}$, where $C$ denotes features of human–computer interaction identified by unsupervised representation learning (illustrated here for three features $x_1$–$x_3$, left), and (ii), the space of mental health $Y^{N \\times Q}$, where $Q$ denotes self-reported dimensions describing mental states (illustrated here with three example items $y_1$–$y_3$, right).  By training support vector regression to map from $X$ to $Y$, MAILA predicts each participant's mental-health profile from their movement fingerprint."


knitr::include_graphics("cursor_mind_files/Figure_1.png")
```

## The space of human–computer interaction

```{r}
#| label: movement_data_summary
#| include: true

##
## datapoints cursor
##
cluster_data <- read.csv(file.path(root, "movement_matrix", "sampled_movements.csv"))
cluster_data$Cluster <- as.factor(cluster_data$Cluster + 1)

movement_matrix_path <- file.path(root, "movement_matrix", "movement_feature_matrix.csv")
mental_health_matrix_path <- file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "mental_health_matrix.csv")

movement_matrix <-data.frame(read.csv(movement_matrix_path))
mental_health_matrix <- data.frame(read.csv(mental_health_matrix_path))

# Load data
datapoints_time <- read.csv(file.path(root, "experiments", "bsi_mhc_5", "summary_results", "experiment_bsi_mhc_5", "datapoints_time.csv"))
av_compression = mean_ci_95(datapoints_time$data_points*2/length(unique(cluster_data$Cluster)), na.rm = TRUE)$mean
ci_compression = mean_ci_95(datapoints_time$data_points*2/length(unique(cluster_data$Cluster)), na.rm = TRUE)$error

sampling_rate_cursor <- read.csv(file.path(root, "hardware", "sampling_rate_group_df_bsi_mhc_5_PID_ID_FILE.csv"))$sampling_rate

##
## datapoints touch
##
sampling_rate_touch <- read.csv(file.path(root, "hardware", "touchscreens.csv"))$freq_avg_hz
datapoints_touch_time <- read.csv(file.path(root, "hardware", "touchscreens.csv"))$n_samples


sampling_rate_cursor_median <- round(median(sampling_rate_cursor, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
sampling_rate_cursor_within_5hz <- round(mean(abs(sampling_rate_cursor - sampling_rate_cursor_median) <= 5, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1)

sampling_rate_touch_median <- round(median(sampling_rate_touch, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
sampling_rate_touch_within_5hz <- round(mean(abs(sampling_rate_touch - sampling_rate_touch_median) <= 5, na.rm = TRUE) * 100, 1)

sampling_rate_within_5hz <- mean(c(sampling_rate_touch_within_5hz, sampling_rate_cursor_within_5hz))


##
## datapoints across
##
across_datapoints = c(datapoints_time$data_points, datapoints_touch_time)
```

We tracked cursor movements in 4,000 participants from the general population who completed a web interface designed to mimic everyday computer use. 2,000 of the 4,000 baseline participants repeated the assessment at a later time. Among the follow-up participants, 600 completed an additional non-mental-health survey, and another 600 played an interactive decision-making game. Separately, we recorded touchscreen activity in 5,500 participants who completed a creative drawing task and a mobile version of the web interface. Among these, 1,000 self-identified as living with depression, and 500 reported living with obsessive–compulsive disorder (OCD). A final validation sample of 500 participants completed the GAD-7 [@spitzer_brief_2006] and PHQ-9 [@kroenke_phq-9_2001] in their standard format in addition to the touch-based MAILA tasks.

MAILA uses unsupervised representation learning to encode each participant’s cursor or touchscreen activity as a distribution over a data-driven library of movement motifs. We segmented each recording, containing on average `r mean_ci_95(across_datapoints)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(across_datapoints)$error` screen-normalized coordinates, into partially overlapping windows of `r length(unique(cluster_data$Time_Step))` consecutive samples. A long short-term memory autoencoder, pretrained on naturalistic human–computer interactions [@kilic_bogazici_2021], transforms each segment into a `r movement_features`-dimensional movement embedding. We pooled the embeddings across all participants N and grouped them into C = `r length(unique(cluster_data$Cluster))` K-means clusters, each representing a distinct, recurring pattern of human–computer interaction. MAILA then computes, for individual participants, the proportion of segments assigned to each cluster (Figures S2–S5).

This process transforms the recorded cursor or touchscreen activity into a $\mathrm{X^{N \times C}}$ feature matrix. Each row in X defines a participant’s location in the space of digital behavior, that is, a C-dimensional distribution over recurring patterns of human–computer interaction.

## The space of mental health

```{r}
#| label: mental_health_summary
#| include: true

# Load required libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2) 
library(dplyr)
library(viridis)
library(stringr)
# 
# reports <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'mental_health_matrix', 'results_quest_test.csv'))
selected_columns <- c(
  "Anxiety", "Phobic.Anxiety", "Somatization", "Obsession.Compulsion", "Depression", "Interpersonal.Sensitivity",
  "Psychoticism", "Paranoid.Ideation", "Hostility", "Clinically.Important", 
  "Emotional.Wellbeing", "Social.Wellbeing", "Psychological.Wellbeing"
)

selected_columns_clean <- str_replace_all(selected_columns, "\\.", " ")

##
## overview true data
##
cor_matrix_true <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "corr_matrix_true.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
tsne_coords_true <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "tsne_true.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

tsne_coords_true_nodes <- tsne_coords_true %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = rownames(.),
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, X, Y)

drop_dims <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")
tsne_coords_true_nodes <- tsne_coords_true_nodes %>%
  filter(!Dimension %in% drop_dims)

cor_long_true <- cor_matrix_true %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Dimension1 = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Dimension1, names_to = "Dimension2", values_to = "Correlation") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension1 = str_replace_all(Dimension1, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish(),
    Dimension2 = str_replace_all(Dimension2, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

cor_long_true_domains <- cor_long_true %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Domain1 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension1, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
    Domain2 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension2, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress")
  )

cor_long_true_domains <- cor_long_true_domains %>%
  filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

domain_true_summary <- cor_long_true_domains %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = case_when(
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Distress"   ~ "Within Distress",
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Wellbeing"  ~ "Within Wellbeing",
    Domain1 != Domain2                           ~ "Between Domains"
  )) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$mean,
    error_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$error,
    n = sum(!is.na(Correlation)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

##
## overview predicted data
##
cor_matrix_pred <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "corr_matrix_predicted.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
tsne_coords_pred <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "tsne_predicted.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

tsne_coords_pred_nodes <- tsne_coords_pred %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = rownames(.),
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, X, Y)

drop_dims <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")
tsne_coords_pred_nodes <- tsne_coords_pred_nodes %>%
  filter(!Dimension %in% drop_dims)

cor_long_pred <- cor_matrix_pred %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Dimension1 = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Dimension1, names_to = "Dimension2", values_to = "Correlation") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension1 = str_replace_all(Dimension1, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish(),
    Dimension2 = str_replace_all(Dimension2, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

cor_long_pred_domains <- cor_long_pred %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Domain1 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension1, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
    Domain2 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension2, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress")
  )

cor_long_pred_domains <- cor_long_pred_domains %>%
  filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

domain_pred_summary <- cor_long_pred_domains %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = case_when(
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Distress"   ~ "Within Distress",
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Wellbeing"  ~ "Within Wellbeing",
    Domain1 != Domain2                           ~ "Between Domains"
  )) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$mean,
    error_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$error,
    n = sum(!is.na(Correlation)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: mental_health_change_summary
#| include: true
long_change_path <- file.path(root, 'experiments', 'bsi_mhc_5_4_cb', 'long_questionnaire_bsi_mhc_5_4_cb.csv')

long_change <- read.csv(long_change_path)

# Fix the name for Obsession-Compulsion in long_change
long_change <- long_change %>%
  mutate(Dimension = str_replace(Dimension, "Obsession-Compulsion", "Obsession Compulsion"))

# Filter out unwanted numeric dimensions and extra variables
long_change <- long_change %>%
  filter(Dimension %in% selected_columns_clean)

# Convert to factor after ensuring only valid dimensions remain
long_change$Dimension <- factor(long_change$Dimension, levels = selected_columns_clean)

long_change_summary <- ddply(
  long_change, .(Dimension), summarise,
  mean_change = mean(abs(Test - Retest), na.rm = TRUE),
  sd_change = sd(abs(Test - Retest), na.rm = TRUE),
  ci_error = mean_ci_95(abs(Test - Retest), na.rm = TRUE)$error,
  ci_lower = mean_ci_95(abs(Test - Retest), na.rm = TRUE)$lower,
  ci_upper = mean_ci_95(abs(Test - Retest), na.rm = TRUE)$upper,
  correlation = cor(Test, Retest, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
)


##
## overview true data
##
cor_matrix_true_change <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "corr_matrix_true_change.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
tsne_coords_true_change <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "tsne_true_change.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

tsne_coords_true_nodes_change <- tsne_coords_true_change %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = rownames(.),
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, X, Y)

drop_dims <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")
tsne_coords_true_nodes_change <- tsne_coords_true_nodes_change %>%
  filter(!Dimension %in% drop_dims)

cor_long_true_change <- cor_matrix_true_change %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Dimension1 = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Dimension1, names_to = "Dimension2", values_to = "Correlation") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension1 = str_replace_all(Dimension1, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish(),
    Dimension2 = str_replace_all(Dimension2, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

cor_long_true_domains_change <- cor_long_true_change %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Domain1 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension1, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
    Domain2 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension2, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress")
  )

cor_long_true_domains_change <- cor_long_true_domains_change %>%
  filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

domain_true_summary_change <- cor_long_true_domains_change %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = case_when(
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Distress"   ~ "Within Distress",
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Wellbeing"  ~ "Within Wellbeing",
    Domain1 != Domain2                           ~ "Between Domains"
  )) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$mean,
    error_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$error,
    n = sum(!is.na(Correlation)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

##
## overview predicted data
##
cor_matrix_pred_change <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "corr_matrix_predicted_change.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
tsne_coords_pred_change <- read.csv(file.path(root, "mental_health_matrix", "tsne_predicted_change.csv"), row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)

tsne_coords_pred_nodes_change <- tsne_coords_pred_change %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = rownames(.),
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, X, Y)

drop_dims <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")
tsne_coords_pred_nodes_change <- tsne_coords_pred_nodes_change %>%
  filter(!Dimension %in% drop_dims)

cor_long_pred_change <- cor_matrix_pred_change %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Dimension1 = rownames(.)) %>%
  pivot_longer(-Dimension1, names_to = "Dimension2", values_to = "Correlation") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension1 = str_replace_all(Dimension1, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish(),
    Dimension2 = str_replace_all(Dimension2, "[-\\.]", " ") %>% str_squish()
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

cor_long_pred_domains_change <- cor_long_pred_change %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Domain1 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension1, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
    Domain2 = ifelse(str_detect(Dimension2, "Wellbeing"), "Wellbeing", "Distress")
  )

cor_long_pred_domains_change <- cor_long_pred_domains_change %>%
  filter(Dimension1 != Dimension2)

domain_pred_summary_change <- cor_long_pred_domains_change %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Type = case_when(
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Distress"   ~ "Within Distress",
    Domain1 == Domain2 & Domain1 == "Wellbeing"  ~ "Within Wellbeing",
    Domain1 != Domain2                           ~ "Between Domains"
  )) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Type) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$mean,
    error_r = mean_ci_95(Correlation, na.rm = TRUE)$error,
    n = sum(!is.na(Correlation)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: validation
#| include: true
##
## Within dataset validation
##
validation_within =  read.csv(file.path(root, 'validation', 'validation_within_summary.csv'))

##
## Validation across time (test-retest) 
##
val_test_retest =  read.csv(file.path(root, 'validation', 'test_retest_group.csv'))
val_test_retest_summary =  read.csv(file.path(root, 'validation', 'test_retest_group_summary.csv'))

change_versus_delay =  read.csv(file.path(root, 'experiments', 'bsi_mhc_5_6a_cb_q_beads', 'long_questionnaire_bsi_mhc_5_6a_cb_beads_time.csv'))

change_versus_delay$abs_change <- abs(change_versus_delay$Retest - change_versus_delay$Test)

# in delay into weeks-
change_versus_delay$week <- floor(change_versus_delay$retest_delay_days / 7)
change_versus_delay$week[change_versus_delay$week >8 ] = 8

# Compute per-dimension stats per week
change_by_week_dim <- ddply(change_versus_delay, .(week, Dimension), summarise,
                            mean_change = mean(abs(Retest - Test), na.rm = TRUE),
                            sem_change = sd(abs(Retest - Test), na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(Retest - Test))),
                            n = length(Test),
                            cor_r = cor(Test, Retest, use = "complete.obs"))

STAT.change_over_time = summary(lmer(abs_change ~ week  + (1 | Dimension) + (1 | PID), data = change_versus_delay))


##
## No prediction from raw response positions
##
interface_data = read.csv(file.path(root, 'validation', 'data_interface.csv'))

ecc_data = read.csv(file.path(root, 'validation', 'eccentricity_interface.csv'))
ecc_data$Type = "Eccentricity"

pos_data = read.csv(file.path(root, 'validation', 'position_interface.csv'))
pos_data$Type = "Position"

ecc_pos_data = rbind(ecc_data, pos_data)
```

We annotated all recorded human–computer interactions with self-reports of psychological distress and wellbeing as two distinct but related domains of mental health [@westerhof_mental_2010]. To this end, we devised a novel, continuous-response interface aligned with the Brief Symptom Inventory (BSI [@derogatis_brief_1983]) and the Mental Health Continuum–Short Form (MHC-SF  [@keyes_evaluation_2008]). We assessed distress on `r questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'BSI',]$total_questions` items across `r questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'BSI',]$total_dimensions` subscales, including depression, anxiety, phobic anxiety, somatization, interpersonal sensitivity, psychoticism, paranoid ideation, hostility, and clinically relevant features. We quantified wellbeing on `r questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'MHC-SF',]$total_questions` items across `r questionnaire_summary[questionnaire_summary$Questionnaire == 'MHC-SF',]$total_dimensions` subscales, covering emotional, social, and psychological experiences (Table S1; Figure S6). 

We organized the continuous self-reports in a $\mathrm{Y^{N \times Q}}$ mental health matrix, where Q represents individual items, dimension-specific scores, and global questionnaire scores, scaled from 0 to 1. Each row in this matrix defines a location in the space of psychological distress and wellbeing, that is, a Q-dimensional description of the experiences that define mental health. 

```{r}
#| label: phq_gad_external_validation
#| include: false

library(dplyr)
library(readr)

# questionnaire totals
phq_gad_df <- read.csv(
  "/Users/veithweilnhammer/Github/cursor_mind/generalization/generalize_to_phq9_gad7/general_phq9_gad7.csv"
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Participant_ID = as.numeric(user_id)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID, GAD7_total, PHQ9_total)

# true MAILA dimension scores
maila_true_df <- read.csv(
  "/Users/veithweilnhammer/Github/cursor_mind/validation/touchscreen_reports_phq9_gad7.csv"
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Participant_ID = ID
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID, Anxiety, Depression)

# merge
validation_df <- phq_gad_df %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(maila_true_df, by = "Participant_ID")

# Spearman correlations
STAT.gad_anxiety <- cor.test(
  validation_df$GAD7_total,
  validation_df$Anxiety,
  method = "spearman",
  use = "complete.obs"
)

STAT.phq_depression <- cor.test(
  validation_df$PHQ9_total,
  validation_df$Depression,
  method = "spearman",
  use = "complete.obs"
)
```


Our ground truth assessment achieved an internal consistency of `r mean(subset(validation_within, metric == 'cronbach_bsi' | metric == 'cronbach_mhc')$value)` (Cronbach’s $\alpha$), with mental health profiles that spanned the full continuum from distress to wellbeing at an average inter-quartile range of `r subset(validation_within, metric == 'item_iqr_mean')$value` ± `r subset(validation_within, metric == 'item_iqr_ci95')$value`. Test-retest correlations reached `r mean(subset(change_by_week_dim, week == 0 & (Dimension == "BSI" | Dimension == "MHC-SF") )$cor_r)` for follow-up intervals shorter than one week, and declined to `r mean(subset(change_by_week_dim, week == 8 & (Dimension == "BSI" | Dimension == "MHC-SF") )$cor_r)` after eight weeks, indicating reliable measurements with sensitivity to mental health changes that accumulated over time. Standard-format PHQ-9 and GAD-7 scores correlated with the corresponding MAILA dimensions of depression and anxiety at R = `r STAT.phq_depression$estimate` (p = `r STAT.phq_depression$p.value`) and R = `r STAT.gad_anxiety$estimate` (p = `r STAT.gad_anxiety$p.value`), respectively, providing strong external validation against established questionnaires (see Methods).

## Linking human–computer interactions and mental health

Together, $\mathrm{X^{N \times C}}$ and $\mathrm{Y^{N \times Q}}$ form a paired representation of digital behavior and mental health. We trained support vector regression machines to predict self-reported mental states from patterns of human–computer interaction, and evaluated model performance on held-out participants using 5-fold cross-validation and generalization of frozen models to independent datasets. 

## Human–computer interactions predict mental health

```{r}
#| label: hci_regression_mental_health
#| include: false

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(RColorBrewer)
library(gridExtra)
# If using hex: library(hexbin)

files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "regression", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "baseline"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "retest", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "follow-up"),
    list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "questionnaire", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "survey"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "beads", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "game"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "quest", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "touch"),
   list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "draw", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "touch-d")
)

predictions_df_all <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()

overall_dimensions <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")
standard_dimensions <- setdiff(unique(predictions_df_all$Dimension), overall_dimensions)
ordered_standard_dimensions <- intersect(selected_columns_clean, standard_dimensions)
ordered_standard_dimensions <- c(overall_dimensions[1], selected_columns_clean[1:10],
                                 overall_dimensions[2], selected_columns_clean[11:13])

custom_blues  <- rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(9, "Blues")[5:9])(11))
custom_greens <- rev(colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(9, "Greens")[5:9])(4))
wellbeing_dims     <- grep("Wellbeing", ordered_standard_dimensions, value = TRUE)
non_wellbeing_dims <- setdiff(ordered_standard_dimensions, wellbeing_dims)
blue_colors  <- setNames(custom_blues,  non_wellbeing_dims)
green_colors <- setNames(custom_greens, wellbeing_dims)
custom_colors <- c(blue_colors, green_colors)

read_with_source <- function(path, source) {
  df <- read.csv(path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- source
  df
}

# Real metrics
real_cursor <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"regression","summary_regression.csv"), "baseline") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, 
         AUC_ROC_10, AUC_ROC_25, AUC_ROC_50, AUC_ROC_75, AUC_ROC_90, 
         p_nrmse, p_r, 
         p_auc_10, p_auc_25, p_auc_50, p_auc_75, p_auc_90,  Source) |> distinct()

real_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","retest","original","summary_regression.csv"), "follow-up") |>
    dplyr::select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, 
         AUC_ROC_10, AUC_ROC_25, AUC_ROC_50, AUC_ROC_75, AUC_ROC_90, 
         p_nrmse, p_r, 
         p_auc_10, p_auc_25, p_auc_50, p_auc_75, p_auc_90,  Source) |> distinct()

real_game <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","beads","original","summary_regression.csv"), "game") |>
    dplyr::select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, 
         AUC_ROC_10, AUC_ROC_25, AUC_ROC_50, AUC_ROC_75, AUC_ROC_90, 
         p_nrmse, p_r, 
         p_auc_10, p_auc_25, p_auc_50, p_auc_75, p_auc_90,  Source) |> distinct()

real_survey <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","questionnaire","original","summary_regression.csv"), "survey") |>   
  dplyr::select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, 
         AUC_ROC_10, AUC_ROC_25, AUC_ROC_50, AUC_ROC_75, AUC_ROC_90, 
         p_nrmse, p_r, 
         p_auc_10, p_auc_25, p_auc_50, p_auc_75, p_auc_90,  Source) |> distinct()

real_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "quest", "original", "summary_regression.csv"), "touch") |>   
  dplyr::select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, 
         AUC_ROC_10, AUC_ROC_25, AUC_ROC_50, AUC_ROC_75, AUC_ROC_90, 
         p_nrmse, p_r, 
         p_auc_10, p_auc_25, p_auc_50, p_auc_75, p_auc_90,  Source) |> distinct()

real_touch_draw <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "draw", "original", "summary_regression.csv"), "touch-d") |>   
  dplyr::select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, 
         AUC_ROC_10, AUC_ROC_25, AUC_ROC_50, AUC_ROC_75, AUC_ROC_90, 
         p_nrmse, p_r, 
         p_auc_10, p_auc_25, p_auc_50, p_auc_75, p_auc_90,  Source) |> distinct()

real_joint_df <- bind_rows(real_cursor, real_retest, real_game, real_survey, real_touch_quest, real_touch_draw)


# Permuted
perm_rho_cursor  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"regression","summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "baseline")
perm_rho_retest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","retest","original","summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "follow-up")
perm_rho_game  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","beads","original","summary_permuted_rhos.csv"), "game")
perm_rho_survey  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","questionnaire","original","summary_permuted_rhos.csv"), "survey")
perm_rho_touch_quest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "quest", "original","summary_permuted_rhos.csv"), "touch")
perm_rho_touch_draw  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "draw", "original","summary_permuted_rhos.csv"), "touch-d")

permuted_rhos_df <- bind_rows(perm_rho_cursor, perm_rho_retest, perm_rho_game, perm_rho_survey, perm_rho_touch_quest, perm_rho_touch_draw)


permuted_rhos_df$Dimension <- factor(permuted_rhos_df$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
real_joint_df$Dimension      <- factor(real_joint_df$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

p_scatter_overall <- ggplot(
  predictions_df_all |> dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")),
  aes(x = True, y = Predicted)
) +
  geom_hex(bins = 100) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  facet_grid("Regression" ~ Dimension) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "True scores", y = "Predicted scores", subtitle = "A")


##
## z-scored
##
dims_to_show <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")

pred_all_z <- predictions_df_all %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_show) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    True_z      = as.numeric(scale(True)),
    Predicted_z = as.numeric(scale(Predicted))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

x_grid_z <- tibble(True_z = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 400))

fits_z <- pred_all_z %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    m  <- lm(Predicted_z ~ True_z, data = .x)
    pr <- as_tibble(predict(m, newdata = x_grid_z, interval = "confidence", level = 0.95))
    bind_cols(x_grid_z, pr)  # columns: True_z, fit, lwr, upr
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

p_scatter_overall_z <- ggplot() +
  # ultra-light point underlay (colored by Dimension, optional)
  geom_point(
    data = pred_all_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = Predicted_z, color = Dimension),
    size = 0.01, alpha = 0.01
  ) +
  # 95% CI ribbon (per Dimension)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = fits_z,
    aes(x = True_z, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.20, color = NA, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # mean OLS line
  geom_line(
    data = fits_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = fit, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4, inherit.aes = FALSE, alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  # per-Dimension dashed lines at facet means (≈ 0 after z-scoring)
  geom_vline(
    xintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  # 45° reference
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_grid("Regression" ~ Dimension) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  labs(x = "True (z)", y = "Predicted (z)", subtitle = "B")

## Long format for metrics
perm_rho_long <- permuted_rhos_df |>
  dplyr::rename(Value = Permuted_R) |>
  dplyr::mutate(Metric = "r") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Value, Metric, Source)

permuted_combined <- bind_rows(perm_rho_long) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r"), ordered = TRUE),
    Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                           levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing"))
  )

# Real (KEEP Source → different shapes)
joint_long <- real_joint_df |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c("R", "NRMSE"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
  dplyr::mutate(Metric = recode(Metric, "R" = "r", "NRMSE" = "e"),
         Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r","e"), ordered = TRUE),
         Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                                levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing")))

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
p_metrics_permuted <- ggplot() +
  # pooled null violins (transparent outline)
  geom_violin(
    data = permuted_combined %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 1, bw = 0.00001, color = NA
  ) +

  # 1) BASELINE — solid markers (fill matches outline color)
  geom_point(
    data = joint_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r", Source %in% c( "baseline", "touch", "touch-d")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +

  # 2) FOLLOW-UP & GAME — hollow markers (white fill, colored outline)
  geom_point(
    data = joint_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r", Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  # Shape mapping (fillable shapes)
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23, "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25)) +
  # Dimension colors (outline + baseline fill)
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +

  facet_grid( ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "R", title = "A" , subtitle = "MAILA predicts inter-individual differences in mental health") +
   theme(
    legend.position   = "top",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),   # narrower keys
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),   # tighter horizontal spacing
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8), # slightly smaller text
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  ylim(-0.05, 0.3) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1))

p_metrics_permuted

##
## STATS
##
R_cursor = subset(real_cursor, !Dimension %in% c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress"))$R
STAT.R_cursor = t.test(R_cursor)

R_retest = subset(real_retest, !Dimension %in% c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress"))$R
STAT.R_retest = t.test(R_retest)

R_game = subset(real_game, !Dimension %in% c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress"))$R
STAT.R_game = t.test(R_game)

R_survey = subset(real_survey, !Dimension %in% c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress"))$R
STAT.R_survey = t.test(R_survey)

R_touch_quest = subset(real_touch_quest, !Dimension %in% c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress"))$R
STAT.R_touch_quest = t.test(R_touch_quest)

R_touch_draw = subset(real_touch_draw, !Dimension %in% c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress"))$R
STAT.R_touch_draw = t.test(R_touch_draw)

##
## time between test and retest
##
gen_time_original_regression_data = subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "follow-up")
gen_time_original_regression_data$week <- floor(gen_time_original_regression_data$DaysBetween / 7)
gen_time_original_regression_data$week[gen_time_original_regression_data$week >= 8] = 8

error_by_time <- ddply(gen_time_original_regression_data, .(week, Dimension), summarise,
                            mean_error = mean(abs(True - Predicted), na.rm = TRUE),
                            sem_error = sd(abs(True - Predicted), na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(sum(!is.na(True - Predicted))),
                            n = length(True),
                            cor_r = cor(True, Predicted, use = "complete.obs"))

STAT.change_over_time = summary(lmer(abs(True-Predicted) ~ DaysBetween  + (1 | Dimension) + (1 | PID), data = gen_time_original_regression_data))

##
## Absolute change over time
##
long_df_items_only = read.csv(file.path(root, 'experiments', 'bsi_mhc_5_6a_cb_q_beads', 'long_questionnaire_bsi_mhc_5_6a_cb_beads_time_items_only.csv'))

##
## errors between calibration and generalization
##
nrmse_participant_og_time <- read.csv(file.path(root,  'generalization', 'retest', 'original', "paired_errors_generalization_vs_calibration.csv"))
summary_nrmse_participant_og_time <- ddply(nrmse_participant_og_time, .(Dimension), summarise,
                         difference = mean(NRMSE_Gen-NRMSE_Test)/mean(NRMSE_Test))

STAT.error_test_time = summary(lmer((NRMSE_Test-NRMSE_Gen) ~ 1 + (1 | Dimension), data = nrmse_participant_og_time))

nrmse_participant_og_game <- read.csv(file.path(root,  'generalization', 'beads', 'original', "paired_errors_generalization_vs_calibration.csv"))
summary_nrmse_participant_og_game <- ddply(nrmse_participant_og_game, .(Dimension), summarise,
                         difference = mean(NRMSE_Gen-NRMSE_Test)/mean(NRMSE_Test))

STAT.error_test_game = summary(lmer((NRMSE_Test-NRMSE_Gen) ~ 1 + (1 | Dimension), data = nrmse_participant_og_game))

nrmse_participant_og_survey <- read.csv(file.path(root,  'generalization', 'questionnaire', 'original', "paired_errors_generalization_vs_calibration.csv"))
summary_nrmse_participant_og_survey <- ddply(nrmse_participant_og_survey, .(Dimension), summarise,
                         difference = mean(NRMSE_Gen-NRMSE_Test)/mean(NRMSE_Test))

STAT.error_test_survey = summary(lmer((NRMSE_Test-NRMSE_Gen) ~ 1 + (1 | Dimension), data = nrmse_participant_og_survey))

##
## prognostic
##
trait_prediction <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'changes', 'pca', 'prognosis_pca.csv'))
```

```{r}
#| label: hci_auc_mental_health
#| include: false

auc_cols <- grep("^AUC_ROC_\\d+$", names(real_joint_df), value = TRUE)

joint_auc_long <- real_joint_df |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Source, dplyr::all_of(auc_cols)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols      = dplyr::all_of(auc_cols),
    names_to  = "Percentile",
    values_to = "AUC"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Percentile   = as.integer(sub("^AUC_ROC_", "", Percentile)),
    Questionnaire = factor(
      ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
      levels = c("Distress", "Wellbeing")
    )
  )

joint_auc_long2 <- joint_auc_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    FillKey = ifelse(Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey"),
                     "white", as.character(Dimension))
  )

pd_src <- position_dodge(width = 0.8)  # real dodge

p_auc_jitter <- ggplot(
  joint_auc_long2,
  aes(
    x     = factor(Percentile),
    y     = AUC,
    color = Dimension,
    shape = Source,
    group = Source
  )
) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = FillKey), position = pd_src, size = 1, stroke = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23, "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("white" = "white", custom_colors), guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = "Percentile cutoff (%)", y = "AUC", subtitle = "C") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.4, 0.9)) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "none",   # shows only Source legend (shapes)
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  )

# Real metrics
real_roc_cursor      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "regression",      "summary_roc_curves.csv"),                                  "baseline")
real_roc_retest      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "generalization",  "retest", "original",        "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "follow-up")
real_roc_game        <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "generalization",  "beads",  "original",        "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "game")
real_roc_survey      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "generalization",  "questionnaire", "original", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "survey")
real_roc_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "touch_regression","general","quest","original","summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "touch")
real_roc_touch_draw  <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "touch_regression","general","draw", "original","summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "touch-d")

real_joint_roc_df <- bind_rows(
  real_roc_cursor,
  real_roc_retest,
  real_roc_game,
  real_roc_survey,
  real_roc_touch_quest,
  real_roc_touch_draw
)

real_joint_roc_df <- real_joint_roc_df %>%
  mutate(
    Type = if_else(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress")
  )

grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.001)

loess_by_dim_src <- real_joint_roc_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fpr) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, Source) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    df <- dplyr::distinct(.x, fpr, tpr)
    fit <- stats::loess(
      tpr ~ fpr, data = df,
      span = 0.25, degree = 1, surface = "direct",
      control = loess.control(trace.hat = "approx")
    )
    t_hat <- predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(fpr = grid))
    t_hat <- pmin(pmax(t_hat, 0), 1)
    t_hat <- cummax(replace(t_hat, is.na(t_hat), 0))
    tibble::tibble(fpr = grid, tpr_hat = t_hat)
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

loess_mean_ci <- loess_by_dim_src %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, fpr) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n        = dplyr::n(),
    mean_tpr = mean(tpr_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tpr   = sd(tpr_hat,   na.rm = TRUE),
    se_tpr   = sd_tpr / sqrt(n),
    ci_low   = pmax(0, mean_tpr - 1.96 * se_tpr),
    ci_high  = pmin(1, mean_tpr + 1.96 * se_tpr),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

p_roc_loess <- ggplot() +
  # 95% CI ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = loess_mean_ci,
    aes(x = fpr, ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.2, color = NA
  ) +
  # mean curve
  geom_line(
    data = loess_mean_ci,
    aes(x = fpr, y = mean_tpr, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_grid("Classification" ~ Dimension) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  labs(
    x        = "False positive rate",
    y        = "True positive rate",
    subtitle = "C"
  )
p_roc_loess

# Permuted
perm_auc_cursor      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "regression",      "summary_permuted_aucs.csv"),                                  "baseline")
perm_auc_retest      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "generalization",  "retest", "original",        "summary_permuted_aucs.csv"),     "follow-up")
perm_auc_game        <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "generalization",  "beads",  "original",        "summary_permuted_aucs.csv"),     "game")
perm_auc_survey      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "generalization",  "questionnaire", "original", "summary_permuted_aucs.csv"),     "survey")
perm_auc_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "touch_regression","general","quest","original","summary_permuted_aucs.csv"),     "touch")
perm_auc_touch_draw  <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "touch_regression","general","draw", "original","summary_permuted_aucs.csv"),     "touch-d")

permuted_aucs_df <- bind_rows(

)

permuted_aucs_df$Dimension <- factor(permuted_aucs_df$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

permuted_aucs_df <- permuted_aucs_df |>
  mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
p_aucs_permuted <- ggplot() +

    data = summary_joint_auc_long %>% dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = avg_AUC, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23, "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  facet_grid("Classification" ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "AUC", subtitle = "A") +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "left",
    legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "lines"),   # reduce spacing between legend rows
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  ylim(0.45, 0.65) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(ncol = 1))
 

pd_src<-  position_dodge(width = 0.8)  # real dodge
pA <- ggplot(joint_auc_long2,
       aes(x = factor(Percentile), y = AUC,
           color = Dimension, shape = Source, group = Source)) +
  geom_point(aes(fill = FillKey), position = pd_src, size = 1, stroke = 0.6) +
```
