```{r}
#| label: tsne_embeddings
#| include: false

cor_threshold <- 0.1
make_edges <- function(cor_long, coords_nodes, source_label, cor_threshold, drop_dims) {
  cor_long %>%
    filter(Correlation > cor_threshold) %>%
    filter(!Dimension1 %in% drop_dims, !Dimension2 %in% drop_dims) %>%
    left_join(coords_nodes %>% dplyr::rename(X1 = X, Y1 = Y),
              by = c("Dimension1" = "Dimension")) %>%
    left_join(coords_nodes %>% dplyr::rename(X2 = X, Y2 = Y),
              by = c("Dimension2" = "Dimension")) %>%
    mutate(Source = source_label)
}

make_nodes <- function(coords_nodes, source_label, drop_dims) {
  coords_nodes %>%
    filter(!Dimension %in% drop_dims) %>%
    mutate(Source = source_label)
}

edges_true <- make_edges(cor_long_true, tsne_coords_true_nodes, "Ground Truth", cor_threshold, drop_dims)
edges_pred <- make_edges(cor_long_pred, tsne_coords_pred_nodes, "MAILA", cor_threshold, drop_dims)
edges_all  <- bind_rows(edges_true, edges_pred)

nodes_true <- make_nodes(tsne_coords_true_nodes, "Ground Truth", drop_dims)
nodes_pred <- make_nodes(tsne_coords_pred_nodes, "MAILA", drop_dims)
nodes_all  <- bind_rows(nodes_true, nodes_pred)

legend_position <- "none"
tile_aspect_ratio <- 1
viridis_palette_option <- "D"
alpha_range <- c(0, 1)
dot_size <- 1
line_alpha <- 0.2
line_width_range <- c(0.1, 1)

p_mental_tsne_facets <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = edges_all,
    aes(x = X1, y = Y1, xend = X2, yend = Y2, linewidth = abs(Correlation)/2),
    color = "gray60", alpha = line_alpha
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = nodes_all,
    aes(x = X, y = Y, fill = Dimension),
    size = dot_size, shape = 21, color = "white", stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = nodes_all,
    aes(x = X, y = Y, label = Dimension),
    size = 1.2, box.padding = 0.5, point.padding = 0.1, min.segment.length = 0,
    force = 5, segment.color = "gray50", segment.size = 0.3, max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Source, nrow = 2, scales = 'free') +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = alpha_range, guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth_continuous(range = line_width_range/2, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = legend_position,
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(x = "t-SNE dimension 1", y = "t-SNE dimension 2", subtitle = "D")
```

```{r}
#| label: correlation_mila_ground_truth
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"
# --- canonical order you want on axes ---
ordered_dims <- selected_columns_clean

# helper to clean names, drop summary dims, and long-ify
prep_cor_long <- function(cor_matrix, label,
                          drop_dims = c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress")) {
  cor_matrix %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    rownames_to_column("Var1") %>%
    pivot_longer(-Var1, names_to = "Var2", values_to = "Correlation") %>%
    filter(!Var1 %in% drop_dims, !Var2 %in% drop_dims) %>%
    mutate(
      Var1 = str_replace_all(Var1, "[\\.-]", " ") %>% str_squish(),
      Var2 = str_replace_all(Var2, "[\\.-]", " ") %>% str_squish(),
      Var1 = factor(Var1, levels = ordered_dims),
      Var2 = factor(Var2, levels = ordered_dims),
      Source = label
    )
}

# data
cor_long_true <- prep_cor_long(cor_matrix_true, "Ground Truth")
cor_long_pred <- prep_cor_long(cor_matrix_pred, "MAILA")
cor_matrix_diff <- cor_matrix_pred - cor_matrix_true
cor_long_diff   <- prep_cor_long(cor_matrix_diff, "Difference")

# facet (True vs MAILA) with viridis
p_corr_structure <- bind_rows(cor_long_true, cor_long_pred) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "r", limits = c(-1, 1),
                       option = viridis_palette_option) +
  facet_wrap(~ Source, nrow = 2) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_dims) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(ordered_dims)) +
  labs(title = "A", subtitle = "MAILA recovers the structure of mental health")

# difference plot with diverging scale centered at 0
v_diff <- max(abs(cor_long_diff$Correlation), na.rm = TRUE)

p_corr_structure_diff <- ggplot(cor_long_diff, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    name = "r (Pred − True)",
    low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",
    limits = c(-v_diff, v_diff), midpoint = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ "Difference", nrow = 1) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_dims) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(ordered_dims)) +
  labs(subtitle = "B")

##
## STATS
##
common_dims <- intersect(rownames(cor_matrix_true), rownames(cor_matrix_pred))
cor_true_flat <- cor_matrix_true[common_dims, common_dims]
cor_pred_flat <- cor_matrix_pred[common_dims, common_dims]

# Extract upper triangle (excluding diagonal)
get_upper <- function(m) {
  m[upper.tri(m, diag = FALSE)]
}

x <- get_upper(as.matrix(cor_true_flat))
y <- get_upper(as.matrix(cor_pred_flat))

# Compute Spearman correlation
STAT.cor_result_structure <- cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)

abs_true <- abs(get_upper(as.matrix(cor_matrix_true)))
abs_pred <- abs(get_upper(as.matrix(cor_matrix_pred)))

# --- Paired t-test (MAILA vs Ground Truth) ---
STAT.abs_corr_ttest <- t.test(abs_pred, abs_true, paired = TRUE, alternative = "two.sided")

# --- RMSE between correlation matrices (upper triangles) ---
STAT.rmse_corr_structure_error <- sqrt(mean((x - y)^2, na.rm = TRUE))/2

# --- Permutation test for RMSE ---
n_perm <- 1000
rmse_perm <- numeric(n_perm)
for (i in seq_len(n_perm)) {
  y_perm <- sample(y)                       # permute MAILA correlations
  rmse_perm[i] <- sqrt(mean((x - y_perm)^2, na.rm = TRUE))/2
}

STAT.rmse_corr_structure_pvalue <- mean(rmse_perm <= STAT.rmse_corr_structure_error)


##
## corr between correlation structures
##
clean_dim <- function(x) x %>% str_replace_all("[\\.-]", " ") %>% str_squish()

get_upper_pairs <- function(mat) {
  idx <- which(upper.tri(mat, diag = FALSE), arr.ind = TRUE)
  tibble(
    Var1  = rownames(mat)[idx[, 1]],
    Var2  = colnames(mat)[idx[, 2]],
    value = mat[idx]
  )
}

drop_dims_raw <- c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress")
drop_dims     <- clean_dim(drop_dims_raw)

scatter_corr_df <- get_upper_pairs(as.matrix(cor_true_flat)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(True = value) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    get_upper_pairs(as.matrix(cor_pred_flat)) %>% dplyr::rename(Predicted = value),
    by = c("Var1", "Var2")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = clean_dim(Var1),
    Pair      = paste(Var1, "-", Var2)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    !Dimension %in% drop_dims,
    Dimension %in% ordered_dims
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension  = factor(Dimension, levels = ordered_dims),
    r_true = True,
    r_pred = Predicted,
    r_mean = (r_true + r_pred) / 2
  )

scatter_corr_structure <- ggplot(scatter_corr_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = r_true)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85, size = 1.2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(
    x = "Ground-truth correlation (R)",
    y = "MAILA correlation (R)",
    color = "Recovery of correlation coefficients (R)",
    title = "B", subtitle = NULL
  ) +
  xlim(-1, 1) + ylim(-1, 1) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(
    option = viridis_palette_option,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    guide = guide_colorbar(
      direction = "horizontal",
      barheight = unit(0.25, "lines"),
      barwidth  = unit(8, "lines"),     # <-- shorten legend bar (try 6–10)
      title.position = "top",
      label.position = "bottom"
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.box.just = "center",         # optional: center the shorter bar
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0),
    legend.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: classification_ocd_mdd
#| include: false
auc_roc_general_quest_ocd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_quest_general_vs_ocd_results.csv"))
auc_roc_general_draw_ocd  <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_draw_general_vs_ocd_results.csv"))
auc_roc_general_quest_mdd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_quest_general_vs_mdd_results.csv"))
auc_roc_general_draw_mdd  <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_draw_general_vs_mdd_results.csv"))

auc_roc_general <- rbind(
  auc_roc_general_quest_ocd,
  auc_roc_general_draw_ocd,
  auc_roc_general_quest_mdd,
  auc_roc_general_draw_mdd
)

# --- Clean up labels: make facet labels exactly "Obsession Compulsion" and "Depression" ---
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- gsub("mdd", "Depression", auc_roc_general$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- gsub("ocd", "Obsession Compulsion", auc_roc_general$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- gsub("general", "General", auc_roc_general$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- gsub("_vs_", " - ", auc_roc_general$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- gsub("_", " ", auc_roc_general$comparison_mode)

# collapse "General - X" -> "X" so facets show only the disorder label
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- gsub("^General\\s*-\\s*", "", auc_roc_general$comparison_mode)

auc_roc_general$task <- gsub("quest", "interface", auc_roc_general$task)

# Factor order
auc_roc_general$comparison_mode <- factor(
  auc_roc_general$comparison_mode,
  levels = c("Obsession Compulsion", "Depression")
)

# Colors keyed to the NEW labels (so they work)
cols_two <- unname(custom_colors[c(5, 6)])
names(cols_two) <- c("Obsession Compulsion", "Depression")

library(dplyr)

anchor_map <- tibble::tibble(
  task = c("interface", "draw"),
  anchor_fpr = c(0.6, 0.75)
)

auc_anchor <- auc_roc_general %>%
  left_join(anchor_map, by = "task") %>%
  group_by(task, comparison_mode) %>%
  slice_min(order_by = abs(fpr - anchor_fpr), n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup()

auc_roc_general_selfreport_quest_mdd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_quest_general_vs_mdd_selfreports_results.csv"))
auc_roc_general_selfreport_draw_mdd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_draw_general_vs_mdd_selfreports_results.csv"))
auc_roc_general_selfreport_quest_ocd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_quest_general_vs_ocd_selfreports_results.csv"))
auc_roc_general_selfreport_draw_ocd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "auc_roc_classify_draw_general_vs_ocd_selfreports_results.csv"))


# --- Add self-report ROC curves as dashed lines (same plot styling) ---

# 1) Bind self-report ROC data
auc_roc_general_selfreport <- rbind(
  #auc_roc_general_selfreport_quest_ocd,
  auc_roc_general_selfreport_draw_ocd,
  #auc_roc_general_selfreport_quest_mdd,
  auc_roc_general_selfreport_draw_mdd
)

# 2) Match label cleaning exactly
auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- gsub("mdd", "Depression", auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- gsub("ocd", "Obsession Compulsion", auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- gsub("general", "General", auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- gsub("_vs_", " - ", auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- gsub("_", " ", auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- gsub("^General\\s*-\\s*", "", auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode)
auc_roc_general_selfreport$task <- gsub("quest", "interface", auc_roc_general_selfreport$task)

auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode <- factor(
  auc_roc_general_selfreport$comparison_mode,
  levels = c("Obsession Compulsion", "Depression")
)

# 3) Add a "source" flag so we can dash only the self-report curves
auc_roc_general$source <- "MAILA"
auc_roc_general_selfreport$source <- "Self-report"

# 4) Combine and plot:
#    - MAILA curves: solid (existing)
#    - Self-report curves: dashed
# 4) Combine and plot:
plot_auc_mdd <- ggplot(
  rbind(auc_roc_general, auc_roc_general_selfreport),
  aes(
    x = fpr, y = tpr,
    color = comparison_mode,
    linetype = source,
    group = interaction(comparison_mode, task, source)
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.85) +

  # Anchor markers (MAILA only) — FIX: inherit x/y from main aes
  geom_point(
    data = auc_anchor %>% dplyr::mutate(source = "MAILA"),
    aes(shape = task, fill = comparison_mode),
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, alpha = 1
  ) +

  # Small "self-report" label in each facet
  geom_text(
    data = dplyr::distinct(
      auc_roc_general_selfreport,
      comparison_mode
    ) %>% dplyr::mutate(fpr = 0.45, tpr = 0.85, label = "ceiling"),
    aes(x = fpr, y = tpr, label = label),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    hjust = 1, vjust = 0,
    size = 1.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  geom_abline(
    intercept = 0, slope = 1,
    linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "MAILA detects individuals who self-identify with mental illness",
    x = "False Positive Rate",
    y = "True Positive Rate"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols_two, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols_two, guide = "none") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(interface = 24, draw = 25), guide = "none") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(MAILA = "solid", `Self-report` = "dashed"), guide = "none") +
  facet_grid(~ comparison_mode) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )

plot_auc_mdd

##
## MDD
##

# Classifcation
summary_general_vs_mdd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_general_vs_mdd_summary_metrics.csv"))
summary_general_vs_mdd_global <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_general_vs_mdd_concat_summary_metrics.csv"))

# Effect of severity and medication on classification
raw_general_vs_mdd_draw <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_draw_general_vs_mdd_results.csv"))
raw_general_vs_mdd_draw$type = "draw"
raw_general_vs_mdd_quest <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_questionnaire_general_vs_mdd_results.csv"))
raw_general_vs_mdd_quest$type = "quest"

raw_general_vs_mdd = rbind(raw_general_vs_mdd_draw, raw_general_vs_mdd_quest)
raw_general_vs_mdd$correct = as.numeric(raw_general_vs_mdd$predicted_label == raw_general_vs_mdd$true_label)

class_mdd_general_sev_med <- glmer(
  correct ~ severity * med_status + (1 | participant_id),
  data = raw_general_vs_mdd[raw_general_vs_mdd$severity != "general",],
  family = binomial
)
STAT.class_mdd_general_sev_med = summary(class_mdd_general_sev_med)


##
## OCD
##

# Classifcation
summary_general_vs_ocd <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_general_vs_ocd_summary_metrics.csv"))
summary_general_vs_ocd_global <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_general_vs_ocd_concat_summary_metrics.csv"))

# Effect of severity and medication on classification
raw_general_vs_ocd_draw <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_draw_general_vs_ocd_results.csv"))
raw_general_vs_ocd_draw$type = "draw"
raw_general_vs_ocd_quest <- read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "classify_questionnaire_general_vs_ocd_results.csv"))
raw_general_vs_ocd_quest$type = "quest"

raw_general_vs_ocd = rbind(raw_general_vs_ocd_draw, raw_general_vs_ocd_quest)
raw_general_vs_ocd$correct = as.numeric(raw_general_vs_ocd$predicted_label == raw_general_vs_ocd$true_label)

class_ocd_general_sev_med <- glmer(
  correct ~ severity * med_status + (1 | participant_id),
  data = raw_general_vs_ocd[raw_general_vs_ocd$severity != "general",],
  family = binomial
)
STAT.class_ocd_general_sev_med = summary(class_ocd_general_sev_med)

##
## JOINT
##
raw_general_vs_ocd$disorder <- "OCD"
raw_general_vs_mdd$disorder <- "MDD"
df_combined_clinical_sev_med <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  raw_general_vs_ocd,
  raw_general_vs_mdd
)

class_disorders_sev_med <- glmer(
  correct ~ disorder * severity * med_status + 
    (1 | participant_id),
  data   = subset(df_combined_clinical_sev_med, severity != "general"),
  family = binomial
)

STAT.class_disorders_sev_med <- summary(class_disorders_sev_med)

##
## Improvement by repeated measures
##
rm_auc_clinical = c(
  subset(summary_general_vs_ocd, context == 'combined')$auc - subset(summary_general_vs_ocd, (context == 'draw' | context == 'questionnaire'))$auc, 
  subset(summary_general_vs_mdd, context == 'combined')$auc - subset(summary_general_vs_mdd, (context == 'draw' | context == 'questionnaire'))$auc)/
  min(c(
    subset(summary_general_vs_mdd, (context == 'draw' | context == 'questionnaire'))$auc), 
    subset(summary_general_vs_ocd, (context == 'draw' | context == 'questionnaire'))$auc
    )
##
## Improvement of self-reports by MAILA
##
improve_language_ocd = 100 * (subset(summary_general_vs_ocd_global, feature_mode == 'concat' & context == "combined")$auc - subset(summary_general_vs_ocd, context == "combined")$auc)/subset(summary_general_vs_ocd, context == "combined")$auc

improve_language_mdd = 100 * (subset(summary_general_vs_mdd_global, feature_mode == 'concat' & context == "combined")$auc - subset(summary_general_vs_mdd, context == "combined")$auc)/subset(summary_general_vs_mdd, context == "combined")$auc

```

```{r}
#| label: regression_ocd_mdd
#| include: false

# Real metrics
clinical_real_touch_all <- read_with_source(
  file.path(root, "touch_regression", "global", "all", "original", "clinical_summary_regression.csv"),
  "touch-all"
)

# Rename subgroup label: "clinical" -> "self-identifying"
clinical_real_touch_all$group <- dplyr::recode(clinical_real_touch_all$group, clinical = "self-identifying")

# Keep desired ordering for legend / shapes
clinical_real_touch_all$group <- factor(
  clinical_real_touch_all$group,
  levels = c("global", "general", "self-identifying")
)

clinical_real_touch_all$Dimension <- factor(
  clinical_real_touch_all$Dimension,
  levels = ordered_standard_dimensions
)

clinical_perm_touch_all <- read_with_source(
  file.path(root, "touch_regression", "global", "all", "original", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),
  "touch-all"
)

clinical_perm_touch_all$Dimension <- factor(
  clinical_perm_touch_all$Dimension,
  levels = ordered_standard_dimensions
)

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)

clinical_metrics_r <- ggplot() +
  geom_violin(
    data = clinical_perm_touch_all,
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Permuted_R, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 0.8, bw = 0.005, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = clinical_real_touch_all[clinical_real_touch_all$group %in% c("global", "general", "self-identifying"), ],
    aes(x = Dimension, y = rho, color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = group),
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(
    x = NULL, y = "R",
    shape = NULL, color = NULL, title = NULL,
    subtitle = "MAILA recovers inter-individual differences among people who self-identify with mental illness"
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  ylim(-0.05, 0.25) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 3))
```

```{r}
#| label: spectrum_ocd_mdd
#| include: false
diagnosis_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "touch_regression", "global", "all", "original", "diagnosis.csv"))
diagnosis_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(diagnosis_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
diagnosis_mila$Dimension <- factor(diagnosis_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

diagnosis_mila$collapsed_group = gsub("MDD", "Depression", diagnosis_mila$collapsed_group)
# order the employment categories (match your rename in Python)
diagnosis_order <- c("General", "Depression", "OCD")
diagnosis_mila$collapsed_group <- factor(diagnosis_mila$collapsed_group , levels = diagnosis_order)

# ensure Type is a nice factor
diagnosis_mila$Type <- factor(diagnosis_mila$Type, levels = c("True","Predicted"))

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.75)

errorbar_diagnosis = ggplot(diagnosis_mila , aes(x = collapsed_group, y = Mean, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95),
    width = 0.1, position = pd, linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(position = pd, size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~Type, scales = "fixed") +
  labs(
    x = "Self-identifying subgroups",
    y = "Mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    title = "D",
    subtitle = "MAILA recovers symptom patterns in self-identifying groups"
  ) + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )


# Wide format: True and Predicted in columns
scatter_diagnosis_df <- diagnosis_mila %>%
  select(Dimension, collapsed_group, Type, Mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Mean) %>%
  drop_na(True, Predicted)

scatter_diagnosis = ggplot(scatter_diagnosis_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
   stat_cor(
    aes(x = True, y = Predicted),          # re-specify without Dimension
    method = "spearman",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,                   # prevents using color=Dimension
    label.x.npc = "left",
    label.y.npc = "top",
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "True mean (z)",
    y = "Predicted mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    title = " ",
    subtitle = " "
  ) + xlim(-0.5, 0.5) + ylim(-0.5, 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )

STAT.corr_diagnosis = cor.test(scatter_diagnosis_df$True, scatter_diagnosis_df$Predicted, method = 'spearman')


##
## Generalization
##
transfer_general_clinical = read.csv(file.path(root, "clinical", "generalizaion_general_clinical.csv"))

##
## predictions
##
global_clinical = read.csv(file.path(root, "touch_regression", "global/all/original/individual_predictions_with_diagnostic_groups.csv"))

STAT.depression_diagn = summary(lm(Predicted ~ collapsed_group, 
             data = subset(global_clinical, Dimension == "Depression")))

STAT.ocd_diagn = summary(lm(Predicted ~ collapsed_group, 
             data = subset(global_clinical, Dimension == "Obsession-Compulsion")))
```

```{r}
#| label: distress_transitions
#| include: false

# Load transition results
transition_results <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/major_distress_transitions_detailed.csv"))

# Load AUC results
auc_results <- read.csv(file.path(root,"changes/distress_transitions_auc_per_dimension.csv"))
auc_distress_dimensions = auc_results[!grepl('Wellbeing', auc_results$Dimension) & !grepl('Overall', auc_results$Dimension),]

permuted_aucs <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/distress_transitions_permuted_aucs.csv"))

# Ensure cleaned column names for all factors
permuted_aucs$Dimension <- str_replace_all(permuted_aucs$Dimension, "\\-", " ") 


# **Identify overall dimensions and standard dimensions**
overall_dimensions <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")
standard_dimensions <- setdiff(unique(permuted_aucs$Dimension), overall_dimensions)

# **Ensure standard dimensions follow selected_columns_clean order**
ordered_standard_dimensions <- intersect(selected_columns_clean, standard_dimensions)
ordered_standard_dimensions = c(overall_dimensions[1], selected_columns_clean[1:10], overall_dimensions[2], selected_columns_clean[11:13])

# Set factor levels: Overall dimensions first, then ordered standard dimensions
permuted_aucs$Dimension <- factor(
  permuted_aucs$Dimension, 
  levels = c(ordered_standard_dimensions)
)
```

```{r}
#| label: comorbid_transitions
#| include: false
comorbidity_df <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/comorbidity_scores.csv"))
comorbidity_corr = cor.test(comorbidity_df$Comorbidity_Change_Actual, comorbidity_df$Comorbidity_Change_Predicted, method = "spearman")

# Load permutation results
permuted_corrs <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/comorbidity_permutations.csv"))

perm_p_comorbidity <- mean(permuted_corrs$Permuted_Corr >= comorbidity_corr$estimate)

# Combine Actual and Predicted into a long format for faceting
comorbidity_long <- comorbidity_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID, 
         Actual_Test = Above_Threshold_Test, 
         Actual_Retest = Above_Threshold_Retest, 
         Predicted_Test = Above_Predicted_Threshold_Test, 
         Predicted_Retest = Above_Predicted_Threshold_Retest) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Actual_Test, Actual_Retest, Predicted_Test, Predicted_Retest),
    names_to = c("Type", "Time"),
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "Comorbidity"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = "Time",
    values_from = "Comorbidity"
  )

```

```{r}
#| label: axis_transitions
#| include: false

# Load entropy scores
entropy_df <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/entropy_scores.csv"))

# Load permutation results
permuted_entropy <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/entropy_permutations.csv"))


# Compute observed Spearman correlation
entropy_corr <- cor.test(entropy_df$Entropy_Change_Actual,
                         entropy_df$Entropy_Change_Predicted,
                         method = "spearman")

# Calculate the permuted p-value
perm_p_entropy <- mean(abs(permuted_entropy$Permuted_Corr) >= abs(entropy_corr$estimate))

# Combine Actual and Predicted into a long format for faceting
entropy_long <- entropy_df %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID,
         Actual_Test = Actual_Entropy_Test,
         Actual_Retest = Actual_Entropy_Retest,
         Predicted_Test = Predicted_Entropy_Test,
         Predicted_Retest = Predicted_Entropy_Retest) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Actual_Test, Actual_Retest, Predicted_Test, Predicted_Retest),
    names_to = c("Type", "Time"),
    names_sep = "_",
    values_to = "Entropy"
  ) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = "Time",
    values_from = "Entropy"
  )
```

Established biomarkers of mental health, such as polygenic risk scores [@lewis_polygenic_2020; @murray_could_2021; @sanchez-roige_emerging_2020], neuroimaging [@schmaal_subcortical_2016; @kambeitz_detecting_2017; @marek_reproducible_2022], wearable sensors [@lyall_association_2018; @abd-alrazaq_systematic_2023; @liu_digital_2025; @xie_jets_2025; @kim_codas_2026], sleep monitoring [@thapa_multimodal_2026],  cognitive tasks [@open_science_collaboration_estimating_2015], and digital behaviors [@eichstaedt_facebook_2018; @kelley_using_2022; @mirea_cognitive_2025], typically yield correlations below R = 0.2 with inter-individual differences in psychological function [@open_science_collaboration_estimating_2015; @marek_reproducible_2022; @kelley_using_2022] and reach an area under the curve (AUC) between 0.55 and 0.75 when classifying diagnoses such as depression [@kambeitz_detecting_2017; @eichstaedt_facebook_2018; @lyall_association_2018; @kim_codas_2026], anxiety [@liu_digital_2025; @xie_jets_2025], or schizophrenia [@lewis_polygenic_2020; @murray_could_2021; @abd-alrazaq_systematic_2023]. 

MAILA matched the performance of existing psychiatric biomarkers with signals that can be extracted at zero marginal cost from any consumer-grade device: Based on cursor movement alone, MAILA predicted overall levels of distress (R = `r subset(real_cursor, Dimension == "Overall Distress")$R`, p = `r subset(real_cursor, Dimension == "Overall Distress")$p_r`, rank correlation relative to $\mathrm{10^6}$ randomly permuted baselines) and wellbeing (R = `r subset(real_cursor, Dimension == "Overall Wellbeing")$R`, p = `r subset(real_cursor, Dimension == "Overall Wellbeing")$p_r`) in held-out participants. Predictions extended to inter-individual differences on all 13 subdimensions of mental health, including depression, anxiety, phobic anxiety, somatization, interpersonal sensitivity, psychoticism, paranoid ideation, hostility, clinically relevant features, and emotional, social, and psychological wellbeing (R = `r mean_ci_95(R_cursor)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(R_cursor)$error`, p = `r STAT.R_cursor$p.value`, across dimensions, 4,000 participants; Figure 2A). The model achieved an equivalent level of performance when evaluated on touch-based interactions with phones and tablets (R = `r mean_ci_95(R_touch_quest)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(R_touch_quest)$error`, p = `r STAT.R_touch_quest$p.value`, 3,500 participants). 

MAILA’s data-driven representations of digital behavior distinguished individuals who self-identified as living with depression (AUC = `r summary_general_vs_mdd[summary_general_vs_mdd$context == "combined", ]$auc`, 1,000 participants) and obsessive–compulsive disorder (AUC =  `r summary_general_vs_ocd[summary_general_vs_ocd$context == "combined", ]$auc`, 500 participants) from those who did not endorse these labels [@sanchez-roige_emerging_2020] (3,500 participants; Figure 2B). The model performed comparably to classifiers trained directly on all available self-reports in the MAILA dataset, which achieved an AUC = `r subset(summary_general_vs_mdd_global, feature_mode == 'selfreports' & context == "combined")$auc` for self-identified depression and `r subset(summary_general_vs_ocd_global, feature_mode == 'selfreports' & context == "combined")$auc` for obsessive–compulsive disorder (Figure 2B), and remained sensitive to inter-individual differences even in these subgroups (R = `r mean_ci_95(subset(clinical_real_touch_all, group == "self-identifying")$rho, na.rm = TRUE)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(clinical_real_touch_all, group == "self-identifying")$rho, na.rm = TRUE)$error`; Figure S7).

## Human–computer interactions track changes in mental health

```{r}
#| label: change_visuals
#| include: false
#| 
files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "changes", 'retest', "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "follow-up"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "changes", 'questionnaire', "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "survey"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "changes", 'beads', "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "game")
)

predictions_df_change <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()

# Real metrics
real_change_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "retest", "original","summary_regression.csv"), "follow-up") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
real_change_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "questionnaire", "original","summary_regression.csv"), "survey") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
real_change_beads <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "beads", "original","summary_regression.csv"), "game") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
real_change = rbind(real_change_retest, real_change_quest, real_change_beads)

# Permuted
perm_rho_change_retest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "retest", "original", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "follow-up")
perm_rho_change_quest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "questionnaire", "original", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "survey")
perm_rho_change_beads  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "beads", "original", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "game")
perm_rho_change = rbind(perm_rho_change_retest, perm_rho_change_quest, perm_rho_change_beads)

perm_rho_change$Dimension <- factor(perm_rho_change$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
real_change$Dimension      <- factor(real_change$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)


p_scatter_change <- ggplot(
  predictions_df_change |> dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")),
  aes(x = True, y = Predicted)
) +
  geom_hex(bins = 75) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, ncol = 2) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.75, 0.75), breaks = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-0.75, 0.75), breaks = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "True change", y = "Predicted change", subtitle = "A")


##
## z-scored (changes)
##
dims_to_show <- c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")

# 1) z-score ΔTrue / ΔPredicted within each Dimension
pred_change_z <- predictions_df_change %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_show) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    True_z      = as.numeric(scale(True)),
    Predicted_z = as.numeric(scale(Predicted))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 2) common x-grid and per-dimension OLS + 95% CI (mean) in z-space
x_grid_z_change <- tibble::tibble(True_z = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 400))

fits_change_z <- pred_change_z %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    m  <- stats::lm(Predicted_z ~ True_z, data = .x)
    pr <- tibble::as_tibble(stats::predict(m, newdata = x_grid_z_change,
                                           interval = "confidence", level = 0.95))
    dplyr::bind_cols(x_grid_z_change, pr)  # cols: True_z, fit, lwr, upr
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 3) plot (z-scored changes)
p_scatter_change_z <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = pred_change_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = Predicted_z, color = Dimension),
    size = 0.01, alpha = 0.05
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = fits_change_z,
    aes(x = True_z, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.20, color = NA, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = fits_change_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = fit, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4, inherit.aes = FALSE, alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, ncol = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = grid::unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  labs(
    x = "True change (z)",
    y = "Predicted change (z)",
    subtitle = "A"
  )


perm_rho_change_long <- perm_rho_change |>
  dplyr::rename(Value = Permuted_R) |>
  mutate(Metric = "r") |>
  select(Dimension, Value, Metric, Source)

permuted_rho_change_combined <- bind_rows(perm_rho_change_long) |>
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r"), ordered = TRUE),
    Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                           levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing"))
  )

real_change_long <- real_change |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c("R", "AUC_ROC", "NRMSE"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
  mutate(Metric = recode(Metric, "R" = "r", "AUC_ROC" = "AUC", "NRMSE" = "e"),
         Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r","e"), ordered = TRUE),
         Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                                levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing")))

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
p_change_permuted <- ggplot() +
  geom_violin(
    data = permuted_rho_change_combined %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 1, bw = 0.001, color = NA
  ) +
  # 2) FOLLOW-UP & GAME — hollow markers (white fill, colored outline)
  geom_point(
    data = real_change_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  # Shape mapping (fillable shapes)
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23)) +
  # Dimension colors (outline + baseline fill)
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
 geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_grid("Regression" ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = 'r', subtitle = "B") +
  ylim(-0.1,0.7) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),   # narrower keys
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),   # tighter horizontal spacing
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8), # slightly smaller text
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1)) 
```

```{r}
#| label: change_rocs
#| include: false
#| 
# Real metrics
real_roc_retest_change     <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "changes", "retest", "original", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "follow-up")
real_roc_game_change        <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "changes", "beads", "original", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "game")
real_roc_survey_change      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "changes", "questionnaire", "original", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "survey")

real_joint_roc_change_df <- bind_rows(
  real_roc_retest_change,
  real_roc_game_change,
  real_roc_survey_change,
)

real_joint_roc_change_df <- real_joint_roc_change_df %>%
  mutate(
    Type = if_else(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress")
  )

grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.001)

loess_by_dim_src_change <- real_joint_roc_change_df %>%
  #dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% c("Overall Distress", "Overall Wellbeing")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fpr) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, Source, Type) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    df <- dplyr::distinct(.x, fpr, tpr)  # avoid dup fpr rows
    fit <- stats::loess(
      tpr ~ fpr, data = df,
      span = 0.25, degree = 1, surface = "direct",
      control = loess.control(trace.hat = "approx")
    )
    t_hat <- predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(fpr = grid))
    t_hat <- pmin(pmax(t_hat, 0), 1)
    t_hat <- cummax(replace(t_hat, is.na(t_hat), 0))
    tibble::tibble(fpr = grid, tpr_hat = t_hat)
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

loess_mean_ci_change <- loess_by_dim_src_change %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, Type, fpr) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n        = dplyr::n(),
    mean_tpr = mean(tpr_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tpr   = sd(tpr_hat,   na.rm = TRUE),
    se_tpr   = sd_tpr / sqrt(n),
    ci_low   = pmax(0, mean_tpr - 1.96 * se_tpr),
    ci_high  = pmin(1, mean_tpr + 1.96 * se_tpr),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

p_roc_loess_change <- ggplot() +
  # 95% CI ribbon
  # geom_ribbon(
  #   data = loess_mean_ci_change,
  #   aes(x = fpr, ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high, fill = Dimension),
  #   alpha = 0.2, color = NA
  # ) +
  # mean curve
  geom_line(
    data = loess_mean_ci_change,
    aes(x = fpr, y = mean_tpr, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_grid( ~ Type) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  labs(
    x        = "False positive rate",
    y        = "True positive rate",
    title = "C",
    subtitle = "MAILA detects mental-health changes within individuals"
  )
p_roc_loess_change
```

#| label: load_vector_alignment

# Load observed metrics
observed_vector <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/vector", "observed_vector_alignment.csv"))

# Load individual participant results
individual_rhos <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/vector", "individual_vector_correlations.csv"))
cosine_similarities <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/vector", "individual_cosine_similarities.csv"))

# Load permuted values
permuted_vector <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/vector", "permuted_rhos.csv"))
permuted_cosines <- read.csv(file.path(root, "changes/vector", "permuted_cosines.csv"))

# For reporting
p_rho <- observed_vector$p_value_rho
mean_cosine <- mean_ci_95(cosine_similarities$Cosine_Similarity, na.rm = TRUE)$mean
p_cosine <- observed_vector$p_value_cosine

```{r}
#| label: tsne_embeddings_change
#| include: false

cor_threshold <- 0.1

edges_true_change <- make_edges(cor_long_true_change, tsne_coords_true_nodes_change, "Ground Truth", cor_threshold, drop_dims)
edges_pred_change <- make_edges(cor_long_pred_change, tsne_coords_pred_nodes_change, "MAILA", cor_threshold, drop_dims)
edges_all_change  <- bind_rows(edges_true_change, edges_pred_change)

nodes_true_change <- make_nodes(tsne_coords_true_nodes_change, "Ground Truth", drop_dims)
nodes_pred_change <- make_nodes(tsne_coords_pred_nodes_change, "MAILA", drop_dims)
nodes_all_change  <- bind_rows(nodes_true_change, nodes_pred_change)

legend_position <- "none"
tile_aspect_ratio <- 1
viridis_palette_option <- "D"
alpha_range <- c(0, 1)
dot_size <- 1
line_alpha <- 0.2
line_width_range <- c(0.1, 1)

p_mental_tsne_facets_change <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = edges_all_change,
    aes(x = X1, y = Y1, xend = X2, yend = Y2, linewidth = abs(Correlation)/2),
    color = "gray60", alpha = line_alpha
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = nodes_all_change,
    aes(x = X, y = Y, fill = Dimension),
    size = dot_size, shape = 21, color = "white", stroke = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_text_repel(
    data = nodes_all_change,
    aes(x = X, y = Y, label = Dimension),
    size = 1.2, box.padding = 0.5, point.padding = 0.1, min.segment.length = 0,
    force = 5, segment.color = "gray50", segment.size = 0.3, max.overlaps = Inf
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Source, nrow = 2, scales = 'free') +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = alpha_range, guide = "none") +
  scale_linewidth_continuous(range = line_width_range/2, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = legend_position,
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", size = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", size = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(x = "t-SNE dimension 1", y = "t-SNE dimension 2", subtitle = "D")
```

```{r}
#| label: correlation_mila_ground_truth_changes
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"


# data
cor_long_true_change <- prep_cor_long(cor_matrix_true_change, "Ground Truth")
cor_long_pred_change <- prep_cor_long(cor_matrix_pred_change, "MAILA")
cor_matrix_diff_change <- cor_matrix_pred_change - cor_matrix_true_change
cor_long_diff_change   <- prep_cor_long(cor_matrix_diff_change, "Difference")

# facet (True vs MAILA) with viridis
p_corr_structure_change <- bind_rows(cor_long_true_change, cor_long_pred_change) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(name = "r", limits = c(-1, 1),
                       option = viridis_palette_option) +
  facet_wrap(~ Source, nrow = 1) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_dims) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(ordered_dims)) +
  labs(subtitle = "C")

# difference plot with diverging scale centered at 0
v_diff_change <- max(abs(cor_long_diff_change$Correlation), na.rm = TRUE)

p_corr_structure_diff_change <- ggplot(cor_long_diff_change, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    name = "r (Pred − True)",
    low = "#4575B4", mid = "white", high = "#D73027",
    limits = c(-v_diff_change, v_diff_change), midpoint = 0
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    axis.title = element_blank(),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ "Difference", nrow = 1) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = ordered_dims) +
  scale_y_discrete(limits = rev(ordered_dims)) +
  labs(subtitle = "B")

##
## STATS
##
common_dims <- intersect(rownames(cor_matrix_true_change), rownames(cor_matrix_pred_change))
cor_true_flat_change <- cor_matrix_true_change[common_dims, common_dims]
cor_pred_flat_change <- cor_matrix_pred_change[common_dims, common_dims]

# Extract upper triangle (excluding diagonal)
get_upper <- function(m) {
  m[upper.tri(m, diag = FALSE)]
}

x <- get_upper(as.matrix(cor_true_flat_change))
y <- get_upper(as.matrix(cor_pred_flat_change))

# Compute Spearman correlation
STAT.cor_result_structure_change <- cor.test(x, y, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE)

abs_true_change <- abs(get_upper(as.matrix(cor_matrix_true_change)))
abs_pred_change <- abs(get_upper(as.matrix(cor_matrix_pred_change)))

# --- Paired t-test (MAILA vs Ground Truth) ---
STAT.abs_corr_ttest_change <- t.test(abs_pred_change, abs_true_change, paired = TRUE, alternative = "two.sided")

# --- RMSE between correlation matrices (upper triangles) ---
STAT.nrmse_corr_structure_error_change <- sqrt(mean((x - y)^2, na.rm = TRUE)) / 2

# --- Permutation test for NRMSE ---
n_perm <- 1000
nrmse_perm <- numeric(n_perm)

for (i in seq_len(n_perm)) {
  y_perm <- sample(y)   # permute MAILA correlations
  nrmse_perm[i] <- sqrt(mean((x - y_perm)^2, na.rm = TRUE)) / 2
}

STAT.nrmse_corr_structure_pvalue_change <- 
  mean(nrmse_perm <= STAT.nrmse_corr_structure_error_change)

##
## corr between correlation structures (changes)
##

drop_dims_raw <- c("Overall Wellbeing", "Overall Distress")
drop_dims     <- clean_dim(drop_dims_raw)

scatter_corr_df_change <- get_upper_pairs(as.matrix(cor_true_flat_change)) %>%
  dplyr::rename(True = value) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    get_upper_pairs(as.matrix(cor_pred_flat)) %>% dplyr::rename(Predicted = value),
    by = c("Var1", "Var2")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = clean_dim(Var1),
    Pair      = paste(Var1, "-", Var2)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(
    !Dimension %in% drop_dims,
    Dimension %in% ordered_dims
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension  = factor(Dimension, levels = ordered_dims),
    r_true = True,
    r_pred = Predicted,
    r_mean = (r_true + r_pred) / 2
  )

scatter_corr_structure_change <- ggplot(scatter_corr_df_change, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = r_true)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.85, size = 1.2) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.3) +
  labs(
    x = "Ground-truth correlation (R)",
    y = "MAILA correlation (R)",
    color = "r (ground-truth)",
    title = "D"
  ) +
  xlim(-1, 1) + ylim(-1, 1) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = viridis_palette_option, limits = c(-1, 1)) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: absolute_changes
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"

absolute_changes_dimension <- long_df_items_only %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, PID) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    rel_change = 100 * abs(Test - Retest),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

absolute_changes <- absolute_changes_dimension %>%
  dplyr::group_by(PID) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    av_change = mean(rel_change),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

median_change <- median(absolute_changes$av_change)
iqr_change    <- IQR(absolute_changes$av_change)
```

```{r}
#| label: change_prediction_wihtout_baseline_labels
#| include: false

real_change_retest_cv <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "retest", "original", "cv_baseline", "summary_regression.csv"), "follow-up") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
real_change_beads_cv <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "beads", "original", "cv_baseline", "summary_regression.csv"), "game") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
real_change_survey_cv <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "questionnaire", "original", "cv_baseline", "summary_regression.csv"), "survey") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
real_change_cv = rbind(real_change_retest_cv, real_change_survey_cv, real_change_beads_cv)

perm_rho_change_retest_cv  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "retest", "original", "cv_baseline", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "follow-up")
perm_rho_change_beads_cv  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "beads", "original", "cv_baseline", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "game")
perm_rho_change_survey_cv  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "questionnaire", "original", "cv_baseline", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "survey")
perm_rho_change_cv = rbind(perm_rho_change_retest_cv, perm_rho_change_survey_cv, perm_rho_change_beads_cv)

real_change_cv$Dimension      <- factor(real_change_cv$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
perm_rho_change_cv$Dimension <- factor(perm_rho_change_cv$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

STAT.real_change_cv = t.test(real_change_cv$R)



perm_rho_change_cv_long <- perm_rho_change_cv |>
  dplyr::rename(Value = Permuted_R) |>
  mutate(Metric = "r") |>
  select(Dimension, Value, Metric, Source)

permuted_rho_change_cv_combined <- bind_rows(perm_rho_change_cv_long) |>
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r"), ordered = TRUE),
    Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                           levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing"))
  )

real_change_cv_long <- real_change_cv |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c("R", "AUC_ROC", "NRMSE"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
  mutate(Metric = recode(Metric, "R" = "r", "AUC_ROC" = "AUC", "NRMSE" = "e"),
         Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r","e"), ordered = TRUE),
         Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                                levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing")))

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
p_change_cv_permuted <- ggplot() +
  geom_violin(
    data = permuted_rho_change_cv_combined %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 1, bw = 0.001, color = NA
  ) +
  ylim(-0.05, 0.3) +
  geom_point(
    data = real_change_cv_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  # Shape mapping (fillable shapes)
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23)) +
  # Dimension colors (outline + baseline fill)
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
 geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_grid("Change correlation without baseline labels" ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = 'r', subtitle = NULL) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),   # narrower keys
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),   # tighter horizontal spacing
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8), # slightly smaller text
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1)) 
```

Detecting changes in mental health is central to early intervention and personalized care [@jacobson_digital_2019; @saragosa-harris_real-world_2022; @kelley_using_2022; @schurr_dynamic_2024]. Most existing psychiatric biomarkers, however, are used as static predictors of risk or diagnostic status [@lewis_polygenic_2020; @murray_could_2021; @sanchez-roige_emerging_2020; @kambeitz_detecting_2017]. To address this gap, we calibrated MAILA on cursor movements from 4,000 participants recruited at baseline and applied the frozen model, without any retraining or adaptation, to 2,000 participants who repeated the experiment `r min(long_df_items_only$retest_delay_days, na.rm = TRUE)` to `r max(long_df_items_only$retest_delay_days, na.rm = TRUE)` days later. Between baseline and follow-up, participants reported median mental-health changes of `r median(absolute_changes$av_change)`% (inter-quartile range: `r iqr_change`%) relative to the full response range.

In contrast to the analyses above, which assessed the ability to predict psychological distress and wellbeing at a single point in time, we asked whether MAILA could decode within-person changes in mental health from cursor movements alone. Based on the recorded cursor movements alone, MAILA discriminated improved from worsened mental health at an average AUC of `r mean_ci_95(real_change$AUC_ROC)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(real_change$AUC_ROC)$error` (Figure 2C). Models trained at baseline successfully predicted inter-individual differences at follow-up (R = `r mean_ci_95(R_retest)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(R_retest)$error`, p = `r STAT.R_retest$p.value`; Figure 2A), and errors did not increase with the interval between recordings (p = `r 1 - STAT.change_over_time$coefficients[2,5]/2`). 

```{r}
#| label: circadian_pca
#| echo: false
#| include: false

circadian_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "pca", "results", "circadian.csv"))
#circadian_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(circadian_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
#circadian_mila$Dimension <- factor(circadian_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
circadian_mila$Type <- factor(circadian_mila$Type, levels = c("True","Predicted"))

errorbar_circadian = ggplot(
  circadian_mila,
  aes(x = LocalHour, y = Mean,
      color = Dimension,
      linetype = Type,
      group = interaction(Dimension, Type))
) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95, fill = Dimension),
              alpha = 0.25, colour = NA, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_line(size = 0.25, alpha = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 12), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette) +
  labs(
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Mean (z)",
    linetype = NULL,
    color = "Dimension",
    subtitle = "C"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  guides(
    color = "none",
    linetype = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.0, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )

errorbar_circadian

circadian_binned_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "pca", "results", "circadian_binned.csv"))
#circadian_binned_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(circadian_binned_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
#circadian_binned_mila$Dimension <- factor(circadian_binned_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

# Wide format: True and Predicted in columns
scatter_circadian_df <- circadian_binned_mila[circadian_binned_mila$Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"),] %>%
  select(Dimension, LocalHour, Type, Mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Mean) %>%
  drop_na(True, Predicted)

scatter_circadian = ggplot(scatter_circadian_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
   stat_cor(
    aes(x = True, y = Predicted),          # re-specify without Dimension
    method = "spearman",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,                   # prevents using color=Dimension
    label.x.npc = "left",
    label.y.npc = "top",
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(
    x = "True mean (z)",
    y = "Predicted mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    subtitle = " "
  ) + xlim(-0.3, 0.3) + ylim(-0.3, 0.3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )
STAT.cor_circadian = cor.test(scatter_circadian_df$True, scatter_circadian_df$Predicted, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
#| label: circadian_original
#| echo: false
#| include: false

circadian_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "original", "results", "circadian.csv"))
circadian_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(circadian_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
circadian_mila$Dimension <- factor(circadian_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
circadian_mila$Type <- factor(circadian_mila$Type, levels = c("True","Predicted"))
circadian_mila$Questionnaire <- ifelse(
  grepl("Wellbeing", circadian_mila$Dimension),
  "Wellbeing",
  "Distress"
)

errorbar_circadian = ggplot(
  subset(circadian_mila, Dimension %in% c("Depression", "Emotional Wellbeing")),
  aes(x = LocalHour, y = Mean,
      color = Dimension,
      group = interaction(Dimension, Type))
) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95, fill = Dimension),
              alpha = 0.25, colour = NA, show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_line(size = 0.25, alpha = 1) +
  facet_wrap(~ Type, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 12), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  labs(
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Mean (z)",
    linetype = NULL,
    color = "Dimension",
    title = "D",
    subtitle = "MAILA recovers established circadian fluctuations in \npositive and negative affect from digital behavior"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  guides(
    color = "none",
    linetype = guide_legend(nrow = 1)
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.0, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )

##
## Scatter
##
circadian_binned_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "original", "results", "circadian_binned.csv"))
circadian_binned_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(circadian_binned_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
circadian_binned_mila$Dimension <- factor(circadian_binned_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

# Wide format: True and Predicted in columns
scatter_circadian_df <- circadian_binned_mila %>%
  filter(Dimension != "Pooled") %>%
  select(Dimension, LocalHour, Type, Mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Mean) %>%
  drop_na(True, Predicted)

scatter_circadian = ggplot(scatter_circadian_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
   stat_cor(
    aes(x = True, y = Predicted),          # re-specify without Dimension
    method = "spearman",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,                   # prevents using color=Dimension
    label.x.npc = "left",
    label.y.npc = "top",
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(
    x = "True hourly mean (z)",
    y = "Predicted hourly mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    subtitle = "Hour-to-hour correlation \nacross dimensions at lag = 0",
    title = " "
  ) + xlim(-0.2, 0.2) + ylim(-0.2, 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )
STAT.cor_circadian = cor.test(scatter_circadian_df$True, scatter_circadian_df$Predicted, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")


##
## Lagcorr
##
lagcorr_df = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "original", "results", "circadian_lag.csv"))
lagcorr_df <- lagcorr_df %>% filter(Dimension != "Pooled")
lagcorr_df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(lagcorr_df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
lagcorr_df$Dimension <- factor(lagcorr_df$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
lagcorr_df$Questionnaire <- ifelse(
  grepl("Wellbeing", lagcorr_df$Dimension),
  "Wellbeing",
  "Distress"
)

# 1) Summarize mean ± 95% CI across Dimensions per Questionnaire × LagHours
lagcorr_summary <- lagcorr_df %>%
  group_by(Questionnaire, LagHours) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    Mean  = mean_ci_95(R)$mean,
    CI95  = mean_ci_95(R)$error,
    .groups = "drop"
  )


# Plot: lag (hours) vs correlation, facet by Dimension
p_lagcorr <- ggplot(
  lagcorr_df,
  aes(x = LagHours, y = R, color = Dimension, group = Dimension)
) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.5, alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = lagcorr_summary %>% filter(Questionnaire == "Distress"),
    aes(x = LagHours, ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill  = custom_colors["Overall Distress"],
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = lagcorr_summary %>% filter(Questionnaire == "Distress"),
    aes(x = LagHours, y = Mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = custom_colors["Overall Distress"],
    linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = lagcorr_summary %>% filter(Questionnaire == "Wellbeing"),
    aes(x = LagHours, ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    fill  = custom_colors["Overall Wellbeing"],
    alpha = 0.18
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = lagcorr_summary %>% filter(Questionnaire == "Wellbeing"),
    aes(x = LagHours, y = Mean),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    color = custom_colors["Overall Wellbeing"],
    linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Questionnaire, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = "Lag (hours)",
    y = "R: MAILA ~ ground truth",
    title = "E",
    subtitle =  "Temporal tuning between MAILA \nand ground-truth peaks lag = 0"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "top",
    legend.key.height = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(1.0, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  )
p_lagcorr
```

```{r}
#| label: SF16_circadian_naturalstic
#| echo: false
#| include: true
#| cache: true
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"


per_user_path <- file.path(root, "generalization", "naturalistic", "original_by_user_hour.csv")
group_path    <- file.path(root, "generalization", "naturalistic", "original_by_group_hour.csv")

user_hour_long <- read.csv(per_user_path) %>%
  mutate(
    user      = as.factor(user),
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  ) %>%
  drop_na(LocalHour, Dimension, Mean)

group_hour <- read.csv(group_path) %>%
  mutate(
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "\\-", " "),
    Type      = factor(Type)   # typically "Predicted" in this file
  ) %>%
  drop_na(LocalHour, Dimension, Mean, CI95)

dims_to_plot <- c("Depression", "Emotional Wellbeing")
if (length(dims_to_plot)) {
  user_hour_long <- user_hour_long %>% filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_plot)
  group_hour     <- group_hour     %>% filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_plot)
}

# Optional: consistent ordering (if you have a global vector)
if (exists("ordered_standard_dimensions")) {
  user_hour_long$Dimension <- factor(user_hour_long$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
  group_hour$Dimension     <- factor(group_hour$Dimension,     levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
}

# Colors (use your palette if present)
if (exists("custom_colors")) {
  scale_col <- scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors)
  scale_fill <- scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors)
} else {
  scale_col <- scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
  scale_fill <- scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Dark2")
}

# Base size
base_sz <- if (exists("base_font_size")) base_font_size else 13

p_nat_circadian <- ggplot() +
  # faint individual user curves
  # geom_line(
  #   data = user_hour_long,
  #   aes(x = LocalHour, y = Mean, group = interaction(user, Dimension), color = Dimension),
  #   linewidth = 0.25, alpha = 0.125, show.legend = FALSE
  # ) +
  # group mean ± 95% CI (ribbon)
  geom_ribbon(
    data = group_hour,
    aes(x = LocalHour, ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95, fill = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.25, colour = NA, show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  # group mean line
  geom_line(
    data = group_hour,
    aes(x = LocalHour, y = Mean, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.25, alpha = 1
  ) +
  # If you have both True and Predicted in group_hour and want the original facet-by-Type look, use:
  # facet_wrap(~ Type, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed")
  # Otherwise facet by Dimension (as we’re plotting per-dimension curves):
  #facet_wrap(~ Dimension, nrow = 1) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(0, 12), limits = c(0, 24)) +
  scale_col +
  scale_fill +
  labs(
    x = "Time of day (h)",
    y = "Mean (z)",
    title = "F",
    subtitle = "Predictions from open-ended \nhuman–computer interactions"
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~"Predicted (External)") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_sz) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "none",
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  )



method_cor <- "spearman"  # or "pearson"
mila_true_hour <- circadian_mila %>%
  filter(Type == "True") %>%
  transmute(
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "\\-", " "),
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    Mean_true = as.numeric(Mean)
  )


ext_pred_hour <- group_hour %>%
  # keep predicted if present; otherwise this filter is harmless if only "Predicted" exists
  filter(is.na(Type) | Type == "Predicted") %>%
  transmute(
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "\\-", " "),
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    Mean_pred_ext = as.numeric(Mean)
  )

joined_hour <- inner_join(mila_true_hour, ext_pred_hour, by = c("Dimension", "LocalHour")) %>%
  drop_na(Mean_true, Mean_pred_ext)


circadian_corr_by_dim <- joined_hour %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::summarize(
    n_hours = n(),
    rho = suppressWarnings(cor(Mean_true, Mean_pred_ext, method = method_cor, use = "complete.obs")),
    p_value = suppressWarnings(cor.test(Mean_true, Mean_pred_ext, method = method_cor)$p.value),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(rho))


ext_binned_path <- file.path(root, "generalization", "naturalistic", "original_by_group_hour_binned.csv")
ext_binned <- read.csv(ext_binned_path) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = str_replace_all(Dimension, "\\-", " "),
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    Predicted = as.numeric(Mean)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension != "Pooled") %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, LocalHour, Predicted)


mila_true_binned <- circadian_binned_mila %>%
  dplyr::filter(Type == "True", Dimension != "Pooled") %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Dimension = Dimension,
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    True = as.numeric(Mean)
  )

scatter_circadian_ext_df <- dplyr::inner_join(
  mila_true_binned,
  ext_binned,
  by = c("Dimension", "LocalHour")
) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(True, Predicted)

scatter_circadian_ext_df <- scatter_circadian_ext_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_plot)

scatter_circadian_ext <- ggplot(scatter_circadian_ext_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  stat_cor(
    aes(x = True, y = Predicted),
    method = "spearman",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    label.x.npc = "left",
    label.y.npc = "top",
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(
    x = "MAILA cohort: true mean by hour (z)",
    y = "External dataset: predicted mean by hour (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    subtitle = " "
  ) + xlim(-0.2, 0.2) + ylim(-0.2, 0.2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )

```

Self-reports in the MAILA dataset, grouped by the local time at which participants started the task, followed established systematic fluctuations in mental health: participants reported higher emotional wellbeing in the early morning and increasing depression-related distress as the day progressed, consistent with a morning peak in positive affect and a gradual rise in negative affect toward nightfall [@golder_diurnal_2011; @costa-gomes_public_2026]. MAILA’s predictions closely tracked time-of-day effects across all mental-health dimensions (R = `r STAT.cor_circadian$estimate`, p = `r STAT.cor_circadian$p.value`; Figure 2D). Temporal tuning curves, obtained by shifting predicted and true diurnal signals, peaked at zero lag, indicating that cursor movements capture shared circadian dynamics in mental state (Figure 2E).

To test whether naturalistic, open-ended human–computer interactions encode similar time-of-day effects, we applied frozen MAILA models to cursor movements from a public dataset of 19 users, who jointly contributed 2,550 hours and 160,000 sessions spanning web browsing, file management, office applications, coding, and entertainment [@kilic_bogazici_2021]. Strikingly, with only these everyday digital behaviors as input, MAILA reproduced the same diurnal pattern within individual users, suggesting that human–computer interactions reflect more positive affect in the morning (correlation with the hour-by-hour average self-reports in the MAILA dataset: R = `r subset(circadian_corr_by_dim, Dimension == "Emotional Wellbeing")$rho`, p = `r subset(circadian_corr_by_dim, Dimension == "Emotional Wellbeing")$p_value`) and increasing negative affect toward nightfall (R = `r subset(circadian_corr_by_dim, Dimension == "Depression")$rho`, p = `r subset(circadian_corr_by_dim, Dimension == "Depression")$p_value`; Figure 2F). In this external dataset, predictions evolved smoothly across successive sessions, consistent with slow natural fluctuations in mental state [@kuppens_emotional_2010], while preserving the expected covariance structure across dimensions (e.g., higher distress co-occurring with lower wellbeing [@caspi_all_2018]; Figure S8). 

```{r}
#| label: Figure_2
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 2. Human–computer interactions predict mental health.** **A.** Correlations (Spearman R) between MAILA predictions and self-reports across 13 mental-health dimensions. Violin plots show null distributions from 1,000,000 label permutations; filled markers indicate observed correlations from 5-fold cross-validation (general population data; baseline: N = 4,000; touch interface: N = 3,500; touch drawing: N = 3,500); open markers indicate correlations when frozen models were applied to independent datasets (follow-up, N = 2,000; survey, N = 600; game, N = 600). **B.** ROC curves for distinguishing participants who self-identified as living with obsessive–compulsive disorder (N = 500, left) or depression (N = 1,000, right) from those who did not endorse these labels (general population, N = 3,500). Upward and downward triangles indicate performance based on touch-based interface interactions and drawings, respectively. The dashed line shows the corresponding self-report–based classifier (using all 67 questionnaire items) as an empirical upper bound. **C.** ROC curves for classifying improvement versus worsening from baseline to follow-up (N = 2,000) using MAILA's continuous predictions. **D.** Ground truth and predictions for depression (negative affect; blue) and emotional wellbeing (positive affect; green) across the day in the MAILA dataset (shaded: 95% confidence intervals). **E.** Lagged correlations between predicted and true diurnal profiles across all dimensions (left; thin lines: individual dimensions; thick lines: averages) peaked at 0 lag, indicating that MAILA captures shared circadian structure in mental state. Predicted versus true group means across time-of-day bins (right). **F.** Applying frozen MAILA models to open-ended cursor activity from an external dataset reproduced the same diurnal fluctuations across 19 individuals with repeated sessions at varying times of day."

lay1 <- rbind(c(1,1,1,2,2), c(1,1,1,3,3), c(6,6,5,4,7))
grid.arrange(p_metrics_permuted, 
             plot_auc_mdd, p_roc_loess_change,
             scatter_circadian, p_lagcorr, errorbar_circadian, p_nat_circadian,
             layout_matrix = lay1, heights = c(1,1,1.2), widths = c(0.75,0.75,1.2,1,0.9))


g <- arrangeGrob(p_metrics_permuted, 
             plot_auc_mdd, p_roc_loess_change,
             scatter_circadian, p_lagcorr, errorbar_circadian, p_nat_circadian,
             layout_matrix = lay1, heights = c(1,1,1.2), widths = c(0.75,0.75,1.2,1,0.9))

ggsave("cursor_mind_files/rev_Figure_2.svg", g, width = 9, height = 5, units = "in", device = "svg")
```

## MAILA generalizes across contexts

```{r}
#| label: generalization_ground_truth_og
#| include: false
#| cache: true
library(readr)

# Load data
quest <- read.csv(file.path(root, "generalization/ground_truth_retest_vs_quest.csv"))
beads <- read.csv(file.path(root, "generalization/ground_truth_retest_vs_beads.csv"))

# Keep only numeric columns (drop ID column)
quest_numeric <- quest[sapply(quest, is.numeric)]
beads_numeric <- beads[sapply(beads, is.numeric)]

# Compute pairwise correlations (matching first and second halves)
half_q <- ncol(quest_numeric) / 2
half_b <- ncol(beads_numeric) / 2

corr_quest <- mapply(function(i) cor(quest_numeric[[i]], quest_numeric[[i + half_q]], use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
                     1:half_q)
corr_beads <- mapply(function(i) cor(beads_numeric[[i]], beads_numeric[[i + half_b]], use = "pairwise.complete.obs"),
                     1:half_b)


##
## Gen: Quest to survey
##
gen_survey_within_regression_data <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'generalization', 'questionnaire', 'within', 'enhanced_regression_results.csv'))

STAT.prediction_within_survey = t.test(subset(gen_survey_within_regression_data, Questionnaire == "Control")$Real_Rho)

##
## Gen: Quest to game
##
gen_game_within_regression_data <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'generalization', 'beads', 'within', 'enhanced_regression_results.csv'))

STAT.prediction_within_game = t.test(gen_game_within_regression_data$Real_Rho)
```

In everyday digital environments, users typically navigate between central content and peripheral controls along horizontal, vertical, and diagonal paths [@kilic_bogazici_2021]. We trained MAILA on cursor and touchscreen activity recorded while participants engaged with a custom web interface that replicated such naturalistic human–computer interactions by placing self-report elements at random central locations and navigation elements at fixed corner positions (Figures S1, S9-S10). 

We designed the interface so that cursor and touchscreen trajectories carried no trivial spatial information about the content of participants' responses.
