In the baseline and follow-up experiments, participants answered items such as “How much are you distressed by feeling fearful?” or "To what extent do you feel happy?" on a continuous scale from “Not at all” to “Very much”. Questions appeared in random order, and responses were given by moving a cursor or dragging a dot onto a response line whose start and endpoint were independently randomized on every trial. MAILA received the full cursor or touchscreen trajectory as input; the model was never told which pixels corresponded to the chosen response, which item was on screen at any moment, or in what order items were answered. Control analyses confirmed that raw pointer coordinates alone could not decode self-reports above chance (Figure S11).

```{r}
#| label: generalization_ground_truth_prediction_corr
#| include: false
#| cache: true
#| 
##
## correlation between contexts
##

# follow-up vs. survey
df_rm_corr_survey <- inner_join(
  subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "follow-up") %>% select(Participant_ID, Dimension, Predicted_quest = Predicted),
  subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "survey")  %>% select(Participant_ID, Dimension, Predicted_draw  = Predicted),
  by = c("Participant_ID", "Dimension")
)

df_rm_corr_survey_results <- df_rm_corr_survey %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n   = sum(complete.cases(Predicted_quest, Predicted_draw)),
    r   = stats::cor(Predicted_quest, Predicted_draw, use = "complete.obs"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Dimension)

STAT.cor_rm_survey_results = t.test(df_rm_corr_survey_results$r)

# follow-up vs. game
df_rm_corr_game <- inner_join(
  subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "follow-up") %>% select(Participant_ID, Dimension, Predicted_quest = Predicted),
  subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "game")  %>% select(Participant_ID, Dimension, Predicted_draw  = Predicted),
  by = c("Participant_ID", "Dimension")
)

df_rm_corr_game_results <- df_rm_corr_game %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n   = sum(complete.cases(Predicted_quest, Predicted_draw)),
    r   = stats::cor(Predicted_quest, Predicted_draw, use = "complete.obs"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Dimension)

STAT.cor_rm_game_results = t.test(df_rm_corr_game_results$r)
```

Having established MAILA's sensitivity to dynamic mental states within the training interface, we next evaluated the model's robustness across a diverse set of human–computer interactions labeled with mental-health self-reports. To this end, we trained models on cursor movements recorded at baseline and then applied frozen MAILA models, without retraining or adaptation, to two separate subgroups recruited at follow-up. Each subgroup included 600 participants who used the web interface for a task unrelated to mental health: one completed a non-psychological survey; the other played an interactive decision-making game [@so_jumping_2016] (Table S2; Figures S1-S2). Responses in both tasks carried no above-chance information about the participants’ mental health (Figure S12). Human–computer interactions recorded in these contexts were not predictive of the non-psychological survey (R = `r mean_ci_95(subset(gen_survey_within_regression_data, Questionnaire == "Control")$Real_Rho)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(gen_survey_within_regression_data, Questionnaire == "Control")$Real_Rho)$error`, p = `r STAT.prediction_within_survey$p.value`) or gameplay behavior (R = `r mean_ci_95(gen_game_within_regression_data$Real_Rho)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(gen_game_within_regression_data$Real_Rho)$error`, p = `r STAT.prediction_within_game$p.value`, Table S2). Any ability to decode psychological distress or wellbeing must therefore arise from how people moved the cursor in a new behavioral and cognitive context, rather than from how they responded to specific survey questions or game events.

```{r}
#| label: errors_distribution_shift
#| include: false
#| cache: true

error_shift_survey = (mean(subset(real_joint_df, Source == "survey")$NRMSE) - mean(subset(real_joint_df, Source == "baseline")$NRMSE))/mean(subset(real_joint_df, Source == "baseline")$NRMSE) * 100

error_shift_game = (mean(subset(real_joint_df, Source == "game")$NRMSE) - mean(subset(real_joint_df, Source == "baseline")$NRMSE))/mean(subset(real_joint_df, Source == "baseline")$NRMSE) * 100
```

Despite the shift in context, MAILA's decoding errors remained within the baseline distribution, increasing by `r mean_ci_95(100*summary_nrmse_participant_og_survey$difference)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(100*summary_nrmse_participant_og_survey$difference)$error`% when frozen models were applied to human–computer interactions recorded during non-mental-health survey completion and decreasing by `r -mean_ci_95(100*summary_nrmse_participant_og_game$difference)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(100*summary_nrmse_participant_og_game$difference)$error`%  for models applied to gameplay behavior (Figure S12). MAILA remained sensitive to inter-individual differences (survey: R = `r mean_ci_95(R_survey)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(R_survey)$error`, p = `r STAT.R_survey$p.value`; game: R = `r mean_ci_95(R_game)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(R_game)$error`, p = `r STAT.R_game$p.value`; Figure 2A) and changes in mental health with only the out-of-context data as input (survey: AUC = `r mean_ci_95(subset(real_change, Source == "survey")$AUC, na.rm = TRUE)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(real_change, Source == "survey")$AUC, na.rm = TRUE)$error`; game: AUC = `r mean_ci_95(subset(real_change, Source == "game")$AUC, na.rm = TRUE)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(real_change, Source == "game")$AUC, na.rm = TRUE)$error`; Figure 2C). At the time of the survey and game experiments, each participant also repeated the mental-health task used for training at baseline. Relative to this context, MAILA produced consistent mental-health estimates for the same held-out individuals (survey: R = `r mean_ci_95(df_rm_corr_survey_results$r)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(df_rm_corr_survey_results$r)$error`, p = `r STAT.cor_rm_survey_results$p.value`; game: R = `r mean_ci_95(df_rm_corr_game_results$r)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(df_rm_corr_game_results$r)$error`, p = `r STAT.cor_rm_game_results$p.value`).

```{r}
#| label: touch_draw_vs_touch_quest
#| include: false
#| cache: true
# Load predictions (unchanged) ----------
files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "repeated_measures", "repeated_measures_error_tidy.csv"), source = "touch"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "repeated_measures", "repeated_measures_error_tidy_pair_1_2.csv"), source = "interface + survey"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "repeated_measures", "repeated_measures_error_tidy_pair_1_3.csv"), source = "interface + game")
)

errors_rm <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()


eps <- 1e-6

df_plot <- errors_rm %>%
  transmute(
    Source,
    x = pmax(as.numeric(avg_of_errors), eps),
    y = pmax(as.numeric(error_of_average), eps)
  ) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(x, y)

relative_reduction_rm <- 100 * ((df_plot$y - df_plot$x)/df_plot$x)
STAT.relative_reduction_rm = t.test(relative_reduction_rm)

by_source <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(Source) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_single = mean(x),
    mean_repeated = mean(y),
    reduction_pct = 100 * (mean_single - mean_repeated) / mean_single,
    n = length(x)
)

eps <- 1e-4  # lower bound for log scale (can't be 0)
p_hex_rm <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = x, y = y)) +
  geom_hex(bins = 100) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  scale_x_continuous(
    #trans = "log10",
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01),
    limits = c(0, 0.5)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    #trans = "log10",
    labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01),
    limits = c(0, 0.5)
  ) +
   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "e: 1 context",
    y = "e: 2 contexts",
    subtitle = "F"
  )




# inner join on Participant_ID + Dimension
df_rm_corr <- inner_join(
  subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "touch") %>% dplyr::select(Participant_ID, Dimension, Predicted_quest = Predicted),
  subset(predictions_df_all, Source == "touch-d")  %>% dplyr::select(Participant_ID, Dimension, Predicted_draw  = Predicted),
  by = c("Participant_ID", "Dimension")
)

cor_rm_results <- df_rm_corr %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n   = sum(complete.cases(Predicted_quest, Predicted_draw)),
    r   = stats::cor(Predicted_quest, Predicted_draw, use = "complete.obs"),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(Dimension)

STAT.cor_rm_results = t.test(cor_rm_results$r)
```

```{r}
#| label: reliability_metrics
#| include: false
# Real metrics
reliability_cursor <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"reliability", "reliability_summary_test.csv"), "baseline") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, R,  Source) |> distinct()
reliability_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"reliability", "reliability_summary_retest.csv"), "follow-up") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, R,  Source) |> distinct()
reliability_survey <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"reliability", "reliability_summary_questionnaire.csv"), "survey") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, R,  Source) |> distinct()
reliability_game <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"reliability", "reliability_summary_beads.csv"), "game") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, R,  Source) |> distinct()
reliability_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"reliability", "reliability_summary_touch_quest.csv"), "touch") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, R,  Source) |> distinct()
  reliability_touch_draw <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"reliability", "reliability_summary_touch_draw.csv"), "touch-d") |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, R,  Source) |> distinct()

reliability_joint_df <- bind_rows(reliability_cursor, reliability_retest, reliability_survey, reliability_game, reliability_touch_quest, reliability_touch_draw)

av_reliability_joint_df <- ddply(reliability_joint_df, .(Dimension, Source), summarise, 
                      R = mean(R, na.rm = TRUE))

# Permuted
reliability_perm_rho_cursor  <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "reliability", "permuted_corrs_test.csv"),  "baseline")
reliability_perm_rho_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "reliability", "permuted_corrs_retest.csv"),  "follow-up")
reliability_perm_rho_survey <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "reliability", "permuted_corrs_questionnaire.csv"),  "survey")
reliability_perm_rho_game <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "reliability", "permuted_corrs_beads.csv"),  "game")
reliability_perm_rho_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "reliability", "permuted_corrs_touch_quest.csv"),  "touch")
reliability_perm_rho_touch_draw <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "reliability", "permuted_corrs_touch_draw.csv"),  "touch-d")

reliability_permuted_rhos_df <- bind_rows(reliability_perm_rho_cursor, reliability_perm_rho_retest, reliability_perm_rho_survey, reliability_perm_rho_game,
  reliability_perm_rho_touch_quest, reliability_perm_rho_touch_draw )


reliability_permuted_rhos_df$Dimension <- factor(reliability_permuted_rhos_df$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
reliability_permuted_rhos_df <- reliability_permuted_rhos_df |>
  mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(
      ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
      levels = c("Distress", "Wellbeing")
    )
  )

av_reliability_joint_df$Dimension      <- factor(av_reliability_joint_df$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
av_reliability_joint_df <- av_reliability_joint_df |>
  mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(
      ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
      levels = c("Distress", "Wellbeing")
    )
  )

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
p_reliability_metrics_permuted <- ggplot() +
  geom_violin(
    data = reliability_permuted_rhos_df,
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Permuted_R, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 1, bw = 0.00001, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = av_reliability_joint_df %>% dplyr::filter(Source %in% c( "baseline", "touch", "touch-d")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = R, color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = av_reliability_joint_df %>% dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = R, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23, "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  facet_grid("Subset 1 ~ Subset 2 (random 50/50 split)" ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "R", subtitle = "MAILA's observed split-half correlations exceed the permuted null across all dimensions") +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),   # narrower keys
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),   # tighter horizontal spacing
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8), # slightly smaller text
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1))
```

To test whether MAILA can generalize beyond rigid user interfaces, we asked all touchscreen participants to complete a series of prompted drawings on their phones or tablets before starting the questionnaire. Each prompt, for example, “Draw a spaceship” or “Draw the digits 036”, specified only what to draw, but not how (see Table S3 for all prompts and Figure S13 for example drawings). When trained and tested on these free-form, creative digital behaviors, MAILA predicted overall distress (R = `r subset(real_touch_draw, Dimension == "Overall Distress")$R`, p = `r subset(real_touch_draw, Dimension == "Overall Distress")$p_r`), wellbeing (R = `r subset(real_touch_draw, Dimension == "Overall Wellbeing")$R`, p = `r subset(real_touch_draw, Dimension == "Overall Wellbeing")$p_r`), and their subdimensions (R = `r mean_ci_95(R_touch_draw)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(R_touch_draw)$error`, p = `r STAT.R_touch_draw$p.value`). Despite relying on entirely different interaction modes, independent models trained on the structured touchscreen interface and the drawing behavior converged on correlated predictions for the same held-out participants (R = `r mean_ci_95(cor_rm_results$r)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(cor_rm_results$r)$error`, p = `r STAT.cor_rm_results$p.value`). Errors decreased by `r -mean_ci_95(relative_reduction_rm)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(relative_reduction_rm)$error`% (p = `r STAT.relative_reduction_rm$p.value`) when predictions from the two touchscreen recordings were combined, indicating that repeated measurements across contexts improve MAILA’s accuracy [@gillan_smartphones_2021]. Across all recorded human–computer interactions, predictions derived from random splits of each participant’s data correlated at R = `r mean_ci_95(av_reliability_joint_df$R)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(av_reliability_joint_df$R)$error`, demonstrating a level of reliability that exceeds many experimental markers of psychological function [@karvelis_individual_2023] (Figure S14).

Together, these results confirm that human–computer interactions encode context-invariant and robust signatures of mental health that generalize across tasks, cognitive context, and user interfaces. While predictive performance decreases under out-of-context deployment, MAILA remained sensitive to both inter-individual differences and within-person change across all tasks and interfaces.

## MAILA predicts mental health across demographics

```{r}
#| label: pca_vs_demo
#| include: false
#| out.width: "100%"

files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "regression", "errors_base_vs_demo_pca.csv"), source = "baseline"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "retest", "pca", "errors_base_vs_demo_pca.csv"), source = "follow-up"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "beads", "pca", "errors_base_vs_demo_pca.csv"), source = "game"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "questionnaire", "pca", "errors_base_vs_demo_pca.csv"), source = "survey"),
    list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "quest", "pca", "errors_base_vs_demo_pca.csv"), source = "touch"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "draw", "pca", "errors_base_vs_demo_pca.csv"), source = "touch-d")
)

demo_errors_df_all_pca <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()

demo_errors_df_all_pca = subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))

p_hex_mila_vs_demo <- ggplot(subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
  aes(x = NRMSE_base, y = NRMSE_demo)) +
  geom_hex(bins = 100) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
 scale_x_continuous(
  labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01),
  limits = c(0, 0.5),
  breaks = seq(0, 0.5, by = 0.25)   # ticks every 0.1
) +
scale_y_continuous(
  labels = label_number(accuracy = 0.01),
  limits = c(0, 0.5),
  breaks = seq(0, 0.5, by = 0.25)   # ticks every 0.1
) +
   geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, , linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "MAILA errors",
    y = "Demographics errors",
    subtitle = "A"
  ) + facet_wrap(~Dimension, ncol = 1, scales = 'free')


STAT.MILA_vs_demo = summary(lmer(NRMSE_base - NRMSE_demo ~ 1 + (1 | Source), data = subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))))
STAT.MILA_PC1_vs_demo = summary(lmer(NRMSE_base - NRMSE_demo ~ 1 + (1 | Source), data = subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC1"))))
STAT.MILA_PC2_vs_demo = summary(lmer(NRMSE_base - NRMSE_demo ~ 1 + (1 | Source), data = subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC2"))))
STAT.MILA_PC3_vs_demo = summary(lmer(NRMSE_base - NRMSE_demo ~ 1 + (1 | Source), data = subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC3"))))

MILA_vs_demo = ddply(
  subset(demo_errors_df_all_pca, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
  .(Dimension),
  summarise,
  mean_rel_change = 100 * mean_ci_95((NRMSE_base - NRMSE_demo), na.rm = TRUE)$mean / mean(NRMSE_demo),
  mean_rel_error  = 100 * mean_ci_95((NRMSE_base - NRMSE_demo), na.rm = TRUE)$error / mean(NRMSE_demo)
)

compare_mila_baseline = read_with_source(file.path(root,"regression", "original", "compare_demo_mila.csv"), "baseline")
compare_mila_retest = read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization", "retest", "original", "compare_demo_mila.csv"), "follow-up")
compare_mila_survey = read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization", "questionnaire", "original", "compare_demo_mila.csv"), "survey")
compare_mila_game = read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization", "beads", "original", "compare_demo_mila.csv"), "game")
compare_mila_touch_quest = read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "quest/original", "compare_demo_mila.csv"), "touch")
compare_mila_touch_draw = read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "draw/original", "compare_demo_mila.csv"), "touch-d")
compare_mila_joint = rbind(compare_mila_baseline, compare_mila_retest, compare_mila_survey, compare_mila_game, compare_mila_touch_quest, compare_mila_touch_draw)

compare_mila_joint$Dimension <- str_replace_all(compare_mila_joint$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
compare_mila_joint$Dimension      <- factor(compare_mila_joint$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

STAT.MILA_plus_demo = t.test(compare_mila_joint$dNRMSE)

plot_delta <- compare_mila_joint %>%
  mutate(
    dR   = 100 * (R_mila_demo   - R_demo)   / R_demo,
    dAUC = 100 * (AUC_mila_demo - AUC_demo) / AUC_demo,
    de   = 100 * (NRMSE_mila_demo - NRMSE_demo) / NRMSE_demo
  ) %>%
  select(Dimension, Source, dR, dAUC, de) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(dR, dAUC, de),
    names_to = "metric",
    values_to = "delta"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    metric = factor(
      metric,
      levels = c("dR", "dAUC", "de"),
      labels = c("Δr", "ΔAUC", "Δe")
    ),
    Questionnaire = factor(
      ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
      levels = c("Distress", "Wellbeing")
    )
  )
plot_delta$Dimension <- factor(plot_delta$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

p_demo_plus_mila_vs_demo_only = ggplot() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = subset(plot_delta, Source %in% c("baseline", "touch", "touch-d") & metric == "Δe"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = delta, color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = Source),
    alpha = 0.8, size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = subset(plot_delta, Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey") & metric == "Δe"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = delta, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    alpha = 0.8, size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(
    "baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21,
    "game" = 22, "survey" = 23,
    "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25
  )) +
  facet_grid("MAILA+D vs. D," ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  labs(x = "Dimension", y = "Δe (%)", subtitle = "B") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
  )


files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "regression/original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values_demo_only.csv"), source = "baseline"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "retest", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values_demo_only.csv"), source = "follow-up"),
    list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "questionnaire", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values_demo_only.csv"), source = "survey"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "beads", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values_demo_only.csv"), source = "game"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "quest", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values_demo_only.csv"), source = "touch"),
   list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "draw", "original", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values_demo_only.csv"), source = "touch-d")
)

predictions_df_demo_only <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()

library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
prep_pred_df <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Dimension = stringr::str_replace_all(Dimension, "\\-", " ")) %>%
    dplyr::select(Participant_ID, Dimension, True, Predicted, Source) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(Participant_ID, Dimension, Source) %>%
    dplyr::summarise(
      True = mean(True, na.rm = TRUE),
      Predicted = mean(Predicted, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}

A <- prep_pred_df(predictions_df_demo_only) %>% dplyr::rename(True_A = True, Pred_A = Predicted)
B <- prep_pred_df(predictions_df_all)       %>% dplyr::rename(True_B = True, Pred_B = Predicted)

AB <- dplyr::inner_join(A, B, by = c("Participant_ID", "Dimension", "Source"))

safe_spear <- function(x, y) {
  if (sum(complete.cases(x, y)) < 2) return(NA_real_)
  suppressWarnings(cor(x, y, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"))
}

per_dim <- AB %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, Source) %>%
  dplyr::reframe(                       
    n_pairs = sum(complete.cases(True_A, True_B, Pred_A, Pred_B)),
    r_true  = safe_spear(True_A, True_B),
    r       = safe_spear(Pred_A, Pred_B)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(
      ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
      levels = c("Distress", "Wellbeing")
    )
  )

cor_summary <- bind_rows(per_dim)

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
p_mila_vs_demo <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = cor_summary %>% dplyr::filter(Source %in% c( "baseline", "touch", "touch-d")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = r, color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = Source),
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = cor_summary %>% dplyr::filter(
      Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = r, color = Dimension, shape = Source),

    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23, "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  facet_grid("MAILA ~ D" ~ Questionnaire, scales = "free", space = "free_x") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "r", subtitle = "C") +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),   # narrower keys
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),   # tighter horizontal spacing
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8), # slightly smaller text
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1))


df_delta <- plot_delta %>%
  dplyr::filter(metric == "Δe") %>%
  transmute(
    Dimension, Source, Questionnaire,
    value = delta,                 # Δe (%)
    Metric = "Δe (%)",
    Contrast = "change in e (%): MAILA+D vs. D"
  )

df_r <- cor_summary %>%
  dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("baseline","follow-up","game","survey","touch","touch-d")) %>%
  transmute(
    Dimension, Source, Questionnaire,
    value = r,                   
    Metric = "r",
    Contrast = "R: MAILA vs. D"
  )

df_all <- bind_rows(df_delta, df_r)
df_all$Dimension = factor(df_all$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)

p_combined_mila_demo <- ggplot() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, color = "black") +

  geom_point(
    data = df_all %>% dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("baseline","touch","touch-d")),
    aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(Dimension), color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = Source),
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, alpha = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = df_all %>% dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("follow-up","game","survey")),
    aes(x = value, y = fct_rev(Dimension), color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, alpha = 0.8, fill = "white"
  ) +

  scale_shape_manual(values = c(
    "baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21,
    "game" = 22, "survey" = 23,
    "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25
  )) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +

  facet_grid(cols = vars(Contrast), rows = vars(Questionnaire),
             scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = "change in e (%) / r", y = "Dimension", subtitle = "B") +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1))

```

```{r}
#| label: demographics_stats
#| include: false
#| out.width: "100%"

if (!load_summary_data) {
  
# fit full model
m_full <- lmer(
  NRMSE ~ Dimension + Gender + Ethnicity.simplified +
    Country.of.birth + Country.of.residence +
    Nationality + Language + 
    Student.status + Employment.status + Age +
    (1 | Participant_ID),
  data = error_dem,
  REML = FALSE
)

anova_demographics <- anova(m_full, type = 3)
anova_demographics$Fvalue = anova_demographics$`F value`
anova_demographics$pvalue = anova_demographics$`Pr(>F)`
  
  if (save_summary_data) {
    print("saving ...")
    save(anova_demographics,
         file = "./Results/anova_summary_demographics.Rdata")
  }
} else {
  load("./Results/anova_summary_demographics.Rdata")
}
median_F = median(anova_demographics$`F value`[2:nrow(anova_demographics)])
```

Bias is a major concern when applying predictive models to people who differ in age, gender, or cultural background, since unequal performance across demographic groups is known to amplify existing disparities in care [@obermeyer_dissecting_2019; @omar_sociodemographic_2025]. Figures S15-S16 summarize the demographic composition of our sample. The MAILA dataset includes participants between `r subset(diversity_summary, Variable == "Age_min")$Unique_values` and `r subset(diversity_summary, Variable == "Age_max")$Unique_values` years of age, `r subset(diversity_summary, Variable == "Nationality")$Unique_values` nationalities and a balanced gender distribution (`r 100 * sum(demographics$Sex == "Female")/nrow(demographics)`% female, `r 100 * sum(demographics$Sex == "Male")/nrow(demographics)`% male), representing varied ethnicities and a range of employment and student statuses (Figures S15–S16). Demographic distributions were closely aligned between all subsets of the MAILA dataset (see Figure S16 for a visualization of age, gender, employment, and ethnicity across subsets). While no dataset can fully reflect global populations [@keyes_uk_2019], this diversity provides a meaningful foundation for testing whether MAILA’s predictions generalize fairly. We observed stable prediction errors across demographic groups (median F = `r median_F`; Figure S15), indicating that, within this sample, demographic factors do not systematically modulate inferences from human–computer interactions in a way suggestive of algorithmic bias.

```{r}
#| label: residual_preds
#| echo: false
#| cache: false
#| include: false

if (!load_summary_data) {
  

  pred <- predictions_df_all %>%
  mutate(
    Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID),
    Source = as.character(Source),
    Dimension = as.character(Dimension)
  )

dem <- error_dem %>%
  mutate(
    Participant_ID = as.character(Participant_ID),
    Source = as.character(Source),
    Dimension = as.character(Dimension)
  )

pred_ids <- pred %>% distinct(Source, Participant_ID)
dem_ids  <- dem  %>% distinct(Source, Participant_ID)

match_summary <- pred_ids %>%
  full_join(dem_ids, by = c("Source", "Participant_ID"), suffix = c("_pred", "_dem")) %>%
  mutate(
    in_pred = !is.na(Participant_ID),
    in_dem  = !is.na(Participant_ID)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Source) %>%
  summarise(
    n_pred = sum(in_pred),
    n_dem  = sum(in_dem),
    n_overlap = sum(in_pred & in_dem),
    frac_pred_matched = n_overlap / n_pred,
    frac_dem_matched  = n_overlap / n_dem,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

demog_cols <- c(
  "Age","Sex","Ethnicity.simplified","Country.of.birth","Country.of.residence",
  "Nationality","Language","Student.status","Employment.status","Time.taken", "Total.approvals"
)

demog <- dem %>%
  dplyr::select(Participant_ID, Source, any_of(demog_cols)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Participant_ID, Source) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(across(everything(), ~ dplyr::first(na.omit(.x))), .groups = "drop")


# helper: residualize y on covariates, return residuals
residualize <- function(df, y, covars){
  if (length(covars) == 0) return(df[[y]])
  f <- as.formula(paste0(y, " ~ ", paste(covars, collapse = " + ")))
  # drop rows with missing in model vars
  d <- df %>% select(all_of(c(y, covars))) %>% drop_na()
  if (nrow(d) < 5) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(df)))
  res <- rep(NA_real_, nrow(df))
  keep_idx <- which(complete.cases(df[, c(y, covars)]))
  res[keep_idx] <- residuals(lm(f, data = df[keep_idx, , drop = FALSE]))
  res
}

# analysis table (merged)
df_ana <- pred %>%
  left_join(demog, by = c("Participant_ID", "Source"))

# choose covariates that actually exist and have variability
covars_present <- intersect(demog_cols, names(df_ana))

# function to run per group
run_one <- function(d){
  d <- d %>% filter(is.finite(True), is.finite(Predicted))
  n_raw <- nrow(d)

  # raw spearman
  raw <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(d$True, d$Predicted, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))

  # pick covariates that are usable in THIS subset (not all NA; >1 level if factor)
  cov_ok <- covars_present[covars_present %in% names(d)]
  cov_ok <- cov_ok[purrr::map_lgl(cov_ok, \(v){
    x <- d[[v]]
    if (all(is.na(x))) return(FALSE)
    if (is.character(x) || is.factor(x)) return(dplyr::n_distinct(na.omit(x)) >= 2)
    if (is.numeric(x) || is.integer(x))  return(stats::sd(x, na.rm = TRUE) > 0)
    FALSE
  })]

  # residualize True and Predicted on demographics, then spearman
  d$True_res <- residualize(d, "True", cov_ok)
  d$Pred_res <- residualize(d, "Predicted", cov_ok)

  d2 <- d %>% filter(is.finite(True_res), is.finite(Pred_res))
  n_adj <- nrow(d2)

  if (n_adj >= 5){
    adj <- suppressWarnings(cor.test(d2$True_res, d2$Pred_res, method = "spearman", exact = FALSE))
    tibble(
      n_raw = n_raw,
      rho_raw = unname(raw$estimate),
      p_raw = raw$p.value,
      n_adj = n_adj,
      rho_adj = unname(adj$estimate),
      p_adj = adj$p.value,
      covars_used = paste(cov_ok, collapse = ", ")
    )
  } else {
    tibble(
      n_raw = n_raw,
      rho_raw = unname(raw$estimate),
      p_raw = raw$p.value,
      n_adj = n_adj,
      rho_adj = NA_real_,
      p_adj = NA_real_,
      covars_used = paste(cov_ok, collapse = ", ")
    )
  }
}

results_corr <- df_ana %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Source, Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~run_one(.x)) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_raw_fdr = p.adjust(p_raw, method = "fdr"),
    p_adj_fdr = p.adjust(p_adj, method = "fdr")
  )
  
  if (save_summary_data) {
    print("saving ...")
    save(results_corr,
         file = "./Results/residualized_summary_demographics.Rdata")
  }
} else {
  load("./Results/residualized_summary_demographics.Rdata")
}

plot_df <- results_corr %>%
  transmute(
    Source,
    Dimension,
    raw = rho_raw,
    adjusted = rho_adj
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(raw, adjusted),
    names_to = "Type",
    values_to = "rho"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Type = recode(Type, raw = "Raw", adjusted = "Adjusted"),
    Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Raw", "Adjusted"))
  )

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.55)

# make sure the color mapping keys match exactly
plot_df2 <- plot_df %>%
  mutate(
    Dimension = as.character(Dimension),
    Dimension_f = factor(Dimension, levels = names(custom_colors)),
    Type = factor(Type, levels = c("Raw", "Adjusted"))
  )

# fillable shapes like in your other figure
SHAPES_TYPE <- c("Raw" = 21, "Adjusted" = 22)

p_residualized_pred <- ggplot() +
  # 1) RAW — solid markers (fill matches outline color)
  geom_point(
    data = plot_df2 %>% filter(Type == "Raw"),
    aes(x = Dimension_f, y = rho, color = Dimension_f, fill = Dimension_f, shape = Type),
    position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  # 2) ADJUSTED — hollow markers (white fill, colored outline)
  geom_point(
    data = plot_df2 %>% filter(Type == "Adjusted"),
    aes(x = Dimension_f, y = rho, color = Dimension_f, shape = Type),
    position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.25, color = "black") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = SHAPES_TYPE) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors, guide = "none") +
  facet_wrap(~ Source, nrow = 1) +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "R (True vs Predicted)",
    subtitle = "MAILA's predictions correlate with the ground truth while controlling for all demographic factors"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "bottom",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.x  = unit(0.3, "lines"),
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8),
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  guides(shape = guide_legend(nrow = 1))

p_residualized_pred

# summaries for inline reporting (add after results_corr exists)
results_corr <- results_corr %>%
  mutate(
    delta_rho = rho_raw - rho_adj
  )

# overall mean ±95% CI drop
ci_drop <- mean_ci_95(results_corr$delta_rho)

# average raw/adjusted rho (across Source×Dimension tests)
ci_rho_raw <- mean_ci_95(results_corr$rho_raw)
ci_rho_adj <- mean_ci_95(results_corr$rho_adj)

STAT.adj <- t.test(results_corr$rho_adj, mu = 0)
```

Beyond differential error across groups, an additional concern is that MAILA may predict mental health only indirectly via confounding traits, because characteristics such as age, gender, cultural background, and the ability to engage with an online task may correlate with both human–computer interaction patterns and mental health. MAILA’s sensitivity to dynamic mental states argues against a purely trait-driven account, because static characteristics cannot explain fluctuations within the same individual over time (Figure 2). Consistent with this, the correlation between MAILA’s predictions and the ground truth remained significant after residualizing both measures with respect to all available demographic covariates (age, gender, ethnicity, country of birth/residence, nationality, language, student status, and employment status) and two proxies of digital literacy (task completion time and the number of prior successfully completed tasks unrelated to MAILA). The adjustment reduced predictive performance by `r ci_drop$mean * 100` ± `r ci_drop$error * 100`% to R = `r ci_rho_adj$mean` ± `r ci_rho_adj$error` while ground-truth correlations remained significant (across dimensions and datasets, T(`r STAT.adj$parameter`) = `r STAT.adj$statistic`, p = `r STAT.adj$p.value`; Figure S17).

## MAILA tracks group-level mental health

```{r}
#| label: simulations_public_mental_health
#| echo: false
#| include: true
simulation_results <- read.csv(file.path(root, "simulation/simulation_results_n_variable.csv"))


library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(pROC)

# Function to compute NNS for a given AUC and prevalence
compute_nns <- function(auc = 0.54, prevalence = 0.25, population_size = 100000) {
  
  # Convert AUC to d-prime (discriminability index)
  d_prime <- sqrt(2) * qnorm(auc)
  
  # Generate true population based on prevalence
  num_cases <- round(prevalence * population_size)
  num_controls <- population_size - num_cases
  
  # Generate case and control scores
  case_scores <- rnorm(num_cases, mean = d_prime, sd = 1)
  control_scores <- rnorm(num_controls, mean = 0, sd = 1)
  
  # Combine all scores
  all_scores <- c(case_scores, control_scores)
  
  # Create binary labels (1 = case, 0 = control)
  labels <- c(rep(1, num_cases), rep(0, num_controls))
  
  # Compute AUC-ROC curve
  roc_obj <- roc(labels, all_scores, direction = "<")  # Ensure controls < cases
  
  # Determine optimal threshold using Youden's J index
  best_threshold <- coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = "threshold", transpose = FALSE)[1]
  
  # Apply threshold to get predicted classifications
  predictions <- ifelse(all_scores >= best_threshold$threshold, 1, 0)
  
  # Compute confusion matrix elements
  tp <- sum(predictions == 1 & labels == 1)  # True positives
  fp <- sum(predictions == 1 & labels == 0)  # False positives
  tn <- sum(predictions == 0 & labels == 0)  # True negatives
  fn <- sum(predictions == 0 & labels == 1)  # False negatives
  
  # Compute Sensitivity & Specificity
  sensitivity <- tp / (tp + fn)
  specificity <- tn / (tn + fp)
  
  # Compute PPV (Positive Predictive Value)
  ppv <- (prevalence * sensitivity) / ((prevalence * sensitivity) + ((1 - prevalence) * (1 - specificity)))
  
  # Compute NNS (Number Needed to Screen)
  nns <- ifelse(ppv > 0, 1 / ppv, NA)  # Avoid division by zero
  
  # Return all screening statistics
  return(data.frame(
  AUC = auc,
  Prevalence = prevalence, 
  Sensitivity = sensitivity,
  Specificity = specificity, 
  PPV = ppv,
  NNS = nns,
  TP = tp,
  FP = fp,
  FP_per_TP = ifelse(tp > 0, fp / tp, NA)
))
}

# Exame: Compute NNS for AUC = 0.54 and Prevalence = 25%
screening_sim <- compute_nns(auc = 0.6, prevalence = 1/8)

##
## Empirical group-level correlations 
##
df_daily <- read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "daily_by_question.csv"))
df_daily$Day <- as.Date(df_daily$Day)

overall_pmh_correlation <- read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health/correlations_per_dimension_daily.csv"))

recov_corr_distress_mean = mean(overall_pmh_correlation[!grepl("Wellbeing", overall_pmh_correlation$Dimension),]$rho)
recov_corr_distress_sem = sd(overall_pmh_correlation[!grepl("Wellbeing", overall_pmh_correlation$Dimension),]$rho)/sqrt(length(sum(!grepl("Wellbeing", overall_pmh_correlation$Dimension))))

recov_corr_wellbeing_mean = mean(overall_pmh_correlation[grepl("Wellbeing", overall_pmh_correlation$Dimension),]$rho)
recov_corr_wellbeing_sem = sd(overall_pmh_correlation[grepl("Wellbeing", overall_pmh_correlation$Dimension),]$rho)/sqrt(length(sum(grepl("Wellbeing", overall_pmh_correlation$Dimension))))
```

```{r}
#| label: age
#| echo: false
#| include: true
age_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "original", "results", "age.csv"))
age_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(age_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
age_mila$Dimension <- factor(age_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
age_mila$Type <- factor(age_mila$Type, levels = c("True","Predicted"))


errorbar_age = ggplot(age_mila, aes(x = Age, y = Mean, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_ribbon(
    aes(ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95, fill = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.1, colour = NA, show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 0.25, aes(group = Dimension), alpha = 0.8) +
  facet_wrap(~ Type, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  labs(
    x = "Age",
    y = "Mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    title = "A",
    subtitle = "MAILA recovers improved mental health in older adults"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  guides(
    fill = "none",
    color = guide_legend(
      ncol = 1,
      override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.0, alpha = 1)  # <- thicker legend lines
    )
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "left",
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.6, "lines"),  # <- give the thicker line a bit more room
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )


# Wide format: True and Predicted in columns
scatter_age_df <- age_mila %>%
  select(Dimension, Age, Type, Mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Mean) %>%
  drop_na(True, Predicted)


scatter_age = ggplot(scatter_age_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, , linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
   stat_cor(
    aes(x = True, y = Predicted),          # re-specify without Dimension
    method = "spearman",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,                   # prevents using color=Dimension
    label.x.npc = "left",
    label.y.npc = "top",
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(
    x = "True mean (z)",
    y = "Predicted mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    title = " ",
    subtitle = " "
  ) + xlim(-0.4, 0.4) + ylim(-0.4, 0.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )
STAT.cor_age = cor.test(scatter_age_df$True, scatter_age_df$Predicted, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
#| label: employment

employment_mila = read.csv(file.path(root, "public_mental_health", "pred_demo", "original", "results", "employment.csv"))
employment_mila$Dimension <- str_replace_all(employment_mila$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
employment_mila$Dimension <- factor(employment_mila$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
employment_mila <- employment_mila %>% filter(!`Employment.status` %in% c("Starting next month"))
employment_mila$Employment.status <- dplyr::recode(
  employment_mila$Employment.status,
  "Full-Time"  = "Full",
  "Part-time"  = "Part",
  "Retired"    = "Retired",
  "Job seeking" = "None"
)
# order the employment categories (match your rename in Python)
emp_order <- c("Full", "Part", "Retired", "None")
employment_mila$`Employment.status` <- factor(employment_mila$Employment.status, levels = emp_order)

# ensure Type is a nice factor
employment_mila$Type <- factor(employment_mila$Type, levels = c("True","Predicted"))

pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.75)

errorbar_employment = ggplot(employment_mila, aes(x = Employment.status, y = Mean, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = Mean - CI95, ymax = Mean + CI95),
    width = 0.1, position = pd, linewidth = 0.2
  ) +
  geom_point(position = pd, size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ Type, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  labs(
    x = "Employment",
    y = "Mean (z)",
    color = NULL,
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "Human–computer interactions reflect lower distress in participants who are retired or employed full-time"
  ) + scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) + 
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  guides(fill = "none", color = guide_legend(ncol = 1), override.aes = list(linewidth = 5)) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )


# Wide format: True and Predicted in columns
scatter_employment_df <- employment_mila %>%
  select(Dimension, Employment.status, Type, Mean) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Mean) %>%
  drop_na(True, Predicted)

scatter_employment = ggplot(scatter_employment_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, , linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
   stat_cor(
    aes(x = True, y = Predicted),          # re-specify without Dimension
    method = "spearman",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,                   # prevents using color=Dimension
    label.x.npc = "left",
    label.y.npc = "top",
    size = 1.5
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(
    x = "True mean (z)",
    y = "Predicted mean (z)",
    color = "Dimension",
    title = " ",
    subtitle = " "
  ) + xlim(-0.4, 0.4) + ylim(-0.4, 0.4) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines")
  )
STAT.cor_employment = cor.test(scatter_employment_df$True, scatter_employment_df$Predicted, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
```

```{r}
scatter_gender = ggplot(scatter_gender_df, aes(x = True, y = Predicted, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 1) +

   stat_cor(

# --- NEW: bias/error plot (Predicted - True), i.e. over/underestimation ---
# Positive = MAILA predicts higher than the self-report mean; negative = lower.

error_gender_df <- gender_mila %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Sex, Type, Mean) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Mean) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(True, Predicted) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Error = Predicted - True,
    AbsError = abs(Error)
  )

# 1) compute Female − Male per Dimension x Type (using group means)
gender_diff <- gender_mila %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sex %in% c("Female", "Male")) %>%  # adapt if your labels differ
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Type, Sex, Mean, CI95) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Sex, values_from = c(Mean, CI95)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Diff = Mean_Female - Mean_Male,
    # conservative CI for difference (assumes independence)
    CI95_Diff = sqrt(CI95_Female^2 + CI95_Male^2),
    Lo = Diff - CI95_Diff,
    Hi = Diff + CI95_Diff
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Type, Diff, Lo, Hi)

# keep your ordering
gender_diff$Dimension <- factor(gender_diff$Dimension, levels = ordered_standard_dimensions)
gender_diff$Type <- factor(gender_diff$Type, levels = c("True", "Predicted"))

# 2) plot: stick from 0 to (Female−Male), dot on top, faceted by True/Predicted
p_gender_diff <- ggplot(gender_diff, aes(x = Dimension, y = Diff, color = Dimension)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  # stick from 0 to the dot
  # geom_linerange(
  #   aes(ymin = 0, ymax = Diff),
  #   linewidth = 0.5,
  #   alpha = 0.9
  # ) +
  # 95% CI around the dot (optional but usually helpful)
  geom_linerange(
    aes(ymin = Lo, ymax = Hi),
    linewidth = 0.2,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +

  # dot
  geom_point(size = 1, alpha = 0.7) +

  facet_wrap(~Type, nrow = 1, scales = "fixed") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Female (z) − Male (z)",
    title = " ",
    subtitle = "Gender differences: predicted < self-reported"
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = unit(1, "lines")
  )

p_gender_diff


gender_mila_distress <- gender_mila %>%
  dplyr::filter(!stringr::str_detect(Dimension, regex("Wellbeing", ignore_case = TRUE)))


gender_diff <- gender_mila_distress %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Type, Sex, Mean, CI95) %>%
  dplyr::filter(Sex %in% c("Male", "Female")) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    names_from  = Sex,
    values_from = c(Mean, CI95),
    names_sep   = "__"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Diff = Mean__Female - Mean__Male
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Type, Diff)

delta_under <- gender_diff %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = Type, values_from = Diff) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(True) & !is.na(Predicted)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Delta = True - Predicted)


STAT.under_gender_all <- t.test(delta_under$Delta, mu = 0)

```

Human–computer interactions can be collected continuously at zero marginal cost, enabling population estimates at arbitrary scale. In our data, individual-level prediction errors ranged from `r min(real_joint_df$NRMSE*100)` to `r max(real_joint_df$NRMSE*100)`%. In simulations calibrated to MAILA's empirical error, group-level estimates approach ceiling once sample sizes exceed ~100, suggesting that MAILA may be especially well suited for population-level mental-health monitoring (Figure S18).

From human–computer interactions alone, and without access to any demographic or temporal information, MAILA recovered established demographic and environmental effects on mental health at high fidelity. This included the effects of age, with older adults reporting lower distress and higher wellbeing [@charles_social_2010] (R = `r STAT.cor_age$estimate`, p = `r STAT.cor_age$p.value`; Figure 3A); of employment, with unemployed and part-time employed individuals reporting higher distress and lower wellbeing than retired or full-time employed participants [@arena_mental_2023] (R = `r STAT.cor_employment$estimate`, p = `r STAT.cor_employment$p.value`; Figure 3B); and of gender, with participants identifying as female reporting higher distress than those identifying as male [@kuehner_why_2017] (R = `r STAT.cor_gender$estimate`, p = `r STAT.cor_gender$p.value`; Figure 3C).

Prior genome-wide association studies have shown strong genetic correlations between clinician-assigned diagnosis and self-reported mental illness [@sanchez-roige_emerging_2020]. Consistent with self-identification, MAILA predicted higher depression-related distress in participants who identified as living with depression (b = `r STAT.depression_diagn$coefficients[2,1]` ± `r STAT.depression_diagn$coefficients[2,2]`, p = `r STAT.depression_diagn$coefficients[2,4]`), and higher obsessive–compulsive distress in those who reported living with OCD (b = `r STAT.ocd_diagn$coefficients[3,1]` ± `r STAT.ocd_diagn$coefficients[3,2]`, p = `r STAT.ocd_diagn$coefficients[3,4]`), with strong agreement between the ground-truth and the information encoded in human–computer interactions across all dimensions of mental health (R = `r STAT.corr_diagnosis$estimate`, p = `r STAT.corr_diagnosis$p.value`, Figure 3D).

Together, these findings benchmark MAILA against known population-level correlates of mental health and illustrate how routine human–computer interactions can support real-time public mental-health monitoring at scale.

```{r}
#| label: Figure_3
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 3. MAILA tracks group-level mental health.** Human–computer interactions enable scalable population estimates of mental health. For each demographic factor, we compare group-level self-reported means (True) to behavior-based estimates from MAILA (Predicted). Error bars and ribbons indicate 95% confidence intervals. Scatter plots show predicted versus true group means across all 13 dimensions for age (**A**), employment (**B**), gender (**C**), and self-identified mental illness (**D**)."

lay_combined = rbind(c(1,1,1,2), c(3,3,3,4), c(5,6, 7,8))
grid.arrange(errorbar_age, scatter_age, 
             errorbar_employment, scatter_employment,
             errorbar_gender, scatter_gender, errorbar_diagnosis, scatter_diagnosis,
  layout_matrix = lay_combined,
  heights = c(1,1,1),
  widths = c(0.8,0.6,1.2,0.6)
)

g <- arrangeGrob(errorbar_age, scatter_age, 
             errorbar_employment, scatter_employment,
             errorbar_gender, scatter_gender, errorbar_diagnosis, scatter_diagnosis,
  layout_matrix = lay_combined,
  heights = c(1,1,1),
  widths = c(0.8,0.6,1.2,0.6)
)

ggsave("cursor_mind_files/rev_Figure_3.svg", g, width = 9, height = 5, units = "in", device = "svg")
```

## Human–computer interactions encode 3 orthogonal dimensions of mental health

Language-based descriptions of mental health are typically interrelated [@caspi_all_2018]: low mood, for example, is frequently accompanied by social withdrawal and persistent worry. These covariations were also present in the MAILA dataset: participants who felt more distressed reported lower wellbeing (and vice versa, R = `r subset(domain_true_summary, Type == "Between Domains")$mean_r` ± `r subset(domain_true_summary, Type == "Between Domains")$error_r`). Higher scores on one dimension were accompanied by higher scores on others in the same domain (correlations within distress dimensions: R = `r subset(domain_true_summary, Type == "Within Distress")$mean_r` ± `r subset(domain_true_summary, Type == "Within Distress")$error_r`; wellbeing: `r subset(domain_true_summary, Type == "Within Wellbeing")$mean_r` ± `r subset(domain_true_summary, Type == "Within Wellbeing")$error_r`; Figure S6). Such shared variance is often taken to reflect a global factor capturing an individual’s propensity toward distress [@caspi_all_2018]. A key question that follows is whether patterns of human–computer interaction encode particular thoughts and emotions that shape the content of psychological distress and wellbeing, or whether they reflect only a general tendency toward poor or good mental health.

```{r}
#| label: pca_variance
#| include: false

variance_explained = read.csv(file.path(root, "dimensions/explained_variance.csv"))
```

```{r}
#| label: pca_projections
#| include: false

pca_projections <- read.csv(file.path(root, "dimensions", "dim_pca_projections.csv"))
# If you saved to "dimensions_pc_driven", switch the path above accordingly.

# --- Long format: columns not in {PC, t} are Dimensions ---
value_cols <- setdiff(names(pca_projections), c("PC", "t"))
pca_long_projections <- pca_projections %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = all_of(value_cols),
               names_to = "Dimension", values_to = "Projection")

# Clean names like before
pca_long_projections$Dimension <- gsub("[-\\.]", " ", pca_long_projections$Dimension)

# Order dimensions (use your preferred order if available)
if (exists("ordered_standard_dimensions")) {
  pca_long_projections$Dimension <- factor(
    pca_long_projections$Dimension,
    levels = ordered_standard_dimensions
  )
} else {
  pca_long_projections$Dimension <- factor(pca_long_projections$Dimension)
}

# Keep PC order
pca_long_projections$PC <- factor(pca_long_projections$PC, levels = c("PC1","PC2","PC3"))

# --- Alpha ramp per (PC, Dimension) using t-order (slow start -> fast end) ---
alpha_range <- c(0.1, 1)
pca_long_projections <- pca_long_projections %>%
  dplyr::arrange(PC, Dimension, t) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(PC, Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    idx = dplyr::row_number(),
    nx  = length(Projection),              # no n()
    w   = ifelse(nx > 1, (idx - 1)/(nx - 1), 0),
    alpha = alpha_range[1] + (alpha_range[2] - alpha_range[1]) * (w^3)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# --- Plot ---
p_projections_faceted <- ggplot(
  pca_long_projections,
  aes(x = Dimension, y = Projection, color = Dimension, alpha = alpha)
) +
  geom_point(size = 1) +
  # use your custom palette if provided
  { if (exists("custom_colors")) scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors)
    else scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") } +
  scale_alpha_continuous(range = alpha_range, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 1)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = c(0,0.5,1), linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Reconstructed score (original scale; 0–1)", title = "A", subtitle = "MAILA projects human–computer interactions into a low-dimensional representation of mental health", color = "") +
  facet_wrap(~ PC, ncol = 3) +  coord_flip()
```

```{r}
#| label: pca_scatter
#| include: false
pc_palette <- c(
  "PC1" = "#4B0082",  # indigo / deep purple
  "PC2" = "#800080",  # classic purple
  "PC3" = "#BA55D3",   # medium orchid
  ">PC3" = "#CDA4DE"   # medium orchid
)

files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "regression", "predicted_vs_true_values_pca.csv"), source = "baseline"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "retest", "pca", "predicted_vs_true_values_pca.csv"), source = "follow-up"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "beads", "pca", "predicted_vs_true_values_pca.csv"), source = "game"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "generalization", "questionnaire", "pca", "predicted_vs_true_values_pca.csv"), source = "survey"),
    list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "quest", "pca", "predicted_vs_true_values_pca.csv"), source = "touch"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "touch_regression", "general", "draw", "pca", "predicted_vs_true_values_pca.csv"), source = "touch-d")
)

predictions_df_all_pca <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()

p_scatter_overall_pca <- ggplot(
  predictions_df_all_pca |> dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
  aes(x = True, y = Predicted)
) +
  geom_hex(bins = 100) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  facet_wrap(~Dimension, ncol = 3, scales = 'free') +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  #scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  #scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "True scores", y = "Predicted scores", subtitle = "B")

##
## z-scored (absolute prediction, PCA components)
##
dims_to_show <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")

# 1) z-score within each PCA component
pred_all_pca_z <- predictions_df_all_pca %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_show) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    True_z      = as.numeric(scale(True)),
    Predicted_z = as.numeric(scale(Predicted))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 2) per-component OLS fit + 95% CI in z-space
x_grid_z_pca <- tibble::tibble(True_z = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 400))

fits_pca_z <- pred_all_pca_z %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    m  <- stats::lm(Predicted_z ~ True_z, data = .x)
    pr <- tibble::as_tibble(stats::predict(m, newdata = x_grid_z_pca,
                                           interval = "confidence", level = 0.95))
    dplyr::bind_cols(x_grid_z_pca, pr)  # cols: True_z, fit, lwr, upr
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 3) plot (same style as your z-scored version)
p_scatter_overall_pca_z <- ggplot() +
  # ultra-light point underlay
  geom_point(
    data = pred_all_pca_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = Predicted_z, color = Dimension),
    size = 0.01, alpha = 0.01
  ) +
  # 95% CI ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = fits_pca_z,
    aes(x = True_z, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.20, color = NA, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # mean OLS line
  geom_line(
    data = fits_pca_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = fit, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4, inherit.aes = FALSE, alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  # guides: zero axes + 45°
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  labs(x = "True (z)", y = "Predicted (z)", subtitle = "B")
```

```{r}
#| label: pca_regression
#| include: false
# Real metrics
pca_real_cursor <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"regression","pca_summary_regression.csv"), "baseline")
pca_real_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","retest","pca","pca_summary_regression.csv"), "follow-up")
pca_real_survey <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","questionnaire","pca","pca_summary_regression.csv"), "survey")
pca_real_game <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","beads","pca","pca_summary_regression.csv"), "game") 
pca_real_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "quest", "pca", "pca_summary_regression.csv"), "touch")
pca_real_touch_draw <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "draw", "pca", "pca_summary_regression.csv"), "touch-d")
pca_real_joint_df <- bind_rows(pca_real_cursor, pca_real_retest, pca_real_game, pca_real_survey, pca_real_touch_quest, pca_real_touch_draw)

pca_perm_rho_cursor  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"regression","summary_permuted_rhos_pca.csv"),  "baseline")
pca_perm_rho_retest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","retest","pca","summary_permuted_rhos_pca.csv"),  "follow-up")
pca_perm_rho_game  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","beads","pca","summary_permuted_rhos_pca.csv"),  "game")
pca_perm_rho_survey  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","questionnaire","pca","summary_permuted_rhos_pca.csv"),  "survey")
pca_perm_rho_touch_quest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "quest", "pca","summary_permuted_rhos_pca.csv"), "touch")
pca_perm_rho_touch_draw  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "draw", "pca","summary_permuted_rhos_pca.csv"), "touch-d")
pca_permuted_rhos_df <- bind_rows(pca_perm_rho_cursor, perm_rho_retest, pca_perm_rho_game, pca_perm_rho_survey, pca_perm_rho_touch_draw)

## Long format for metrics
pca_perm_rho_long <- pca_permuted_rhos_df |>
  dplyr::rename(Value = Permuted_R) |>
  mutate(Metric = "r") |>
  select(Dimension, Value, Metric, Source)

pca_permuted_combined <- bind_rows(pca_perm_rho_long) |>
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r"), ordered = TRUE),
    Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                           levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing"))
  )

# Real (KEEP Source → different shapes)
pca_joint_long <- pca_real_joint_df |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c("R", "AUC_ROC_50", "NRMSE"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
  mutate(Metric = recode(Metric, "R" = "r", "AUC_ROC_50" = "AUC", "NRMSE" = "e"),
         Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r","e"), ordered = TRUE),
         Questionnaire = factor(ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
                                levels = c("Distress","Wellbeing")))

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
##
p_metrics_permuted_pca <- ggplot() +
  # pooled null violins (transparent outline)
  geom_violin(
    data = pca_permuted_combined %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r", Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 1, bw = 0.005, color = NA
  ) +

  # 1) BASELINE — solid markers (fill matches outline color)
  geom_point(
    data = pca_joint_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r", Source %in% c( "baseline", "touch", "touch-d"), Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, fill = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6
  ) +

  # 2) FOLLOW-UP & GAME — hollow markers (white fill, colored outline)
  geom_point(
    data = pca_joint_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r", Source %in% c("follow-up", "game", "survey"), Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  geom_violin(
    data = pca_joint_long %>%
      dplyr::filter(Metric == "r",
                    !Dimension %in% c("PC1","PC2","PC3")) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(DimColl = ">PC3"),
    aes(x = DimColl, y = Value, color = DimColl, fill = DimColl),
    size = 0.25, alpha = 0.3, bw = 0.005
    #position = position_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0)
  ) +
  # Make sure the new column appears after PC3
  scale_x_discrete(limits = c("PC1","PC2","PC3",">PC3")) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  # Shape mapping (fillable shapes)
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline" = 21, "follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23, "touch" = 24, "touch-d" = 25)) +
  # Dimension colors (outline + baseline fill)
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "r", shape = NULL, title = "B", subtitle = "MAILA predicts inter-individual \ndifferences along PC1-3") +
  guides(
    shape = guide_legend(ncol = 1, byrow = TRUE),
  ) +
theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.direction = "vertical",
    legend.box = "vertical",
    legend.box.just = "left",
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.15, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.05, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.05, 0.3))
```

```{r}
#| label: pca_auc
#| include: false
auc_cols <- grep("^AUC_ROC_\\d+$", names(pca_real_joint_df), value = TRUE)

pca_joint_auc_long <- pca_real_joint_df |>
  dplyr::select(Dimension, Source, dplyr::all_of(auc_cols)) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = dplyr::all_of(auc_cols),
    names_to = "Percentile",
    values_to = "AUC"
  ) |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    Percentile = as.integer(sub("^AUC_ROC_", "", Percentile)),
    Questionnaire = factor(
      ifelse(grepl("Wellbeing", Dimension), "Wellbeing", "Distress"),
      levels = c("Distress", "Wellbeing")
    )
  )
pca_joint_auc_long = subset(pca_joint_auc_long, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))


pca_joint_auc_long2 <- pca_joint_auc_long %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    FillKey = ifelse(Source %in% c("follow-up","game","survey"),
                     "white", as.character(Dimension))
  )

pd_src<-  position_dodge(width = 0.8)  # real dodge
pca_p_auc_jitter <- ggplot(subset(pca_joint_auc_long2),
       aes(x = factor(Percentile), y = AUC,
           color = Dimension, shape = Source, group = Source)) +   # <-- dodge by Source
  geom_point(aes(fill = FillKey), position = pd_src, size = 1, stroke = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.5, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("baseline"=21,"follow-up"=21,"game"=22,"survey"=23,"touch"=24,"touch-d"=25)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("white"="white", pc_palette), guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = "Percentile cutoff (%)", y = "AUC", subtitle = "C") +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0.4, 0.9)) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",   # shows only Source legend (shapes)
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"))

# Real metrics
pca_real_roc_cursor <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"regression","summary_roc_curves_pca.csv"), "baseline") 
pca_real_roc_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","retest","pca","summary_roc_curves_pca.csv"), "follow-up") 
pca_real_roc_game <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","beads","pca","summary_roc_curves_pca.csv"), "game") 
pca_real_roc_survey <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"generalization","questionnaire","pca","summary_roc_curves_pca.csv"), "survey") 
pca_real_roc_touch_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "quest", "pca", "summary_roc_curves_pca.csv"), "touch")
pca_real_roc_touch_draw <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"touch_regression", "general", "draw", "pca", "summary_roc_curves_pca.csv"), "touch-d") 

pca_real_joint_roc_df <- bind_rows(pca_real_roc_cursor, pca_real_roc_retest, pca_real_roc_game, pca_real_roc_survey, pca_real_roc_touch_quest, pca_real_roc_touch_draw)
pca_real_joint_roc_df = subset(pca_real_joint_roc_df, Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))


grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.001)

pca_loess_by_dim_src <- pca_real_joint_roc_df %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fpr) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, Source) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    df <- dplyr::distinct(.x, fpr, tpr)  # avoid dup fpr rows
    fit <- stats::loess(
      tpr ~ fpr, data = df,
      span = 0.25, degree = 1, surface = "direct",
      control = loess.control(trace.hat = "approx")
    )
    t_hat <- predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(fpr = grid))
    t_hat <- pmin(pmax(t_hat, 0), 1)
    t_hat <- cummax(replace(t_hat, is.na(t_hat), 0))
    tibble::tibble(fpr = grid, tpr_hat = t_hat)
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

pca_loess_mean_ci <- pca_loess_by_dim_src %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, fpr) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n        = dplyr::n(),
    mean_tpr = mean(tpr_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tpr   = sd(tpr_hat,   na.rm = TRUE),
    se_tpr   = sd_tpr / sqrt(n),
    ci_low   = pmax(0, mean_tpr - 1.96 * se_tpr),
    ci_high  = pmin(1, mean_tpr + 1.96 * se_tpr),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

pca_p_roc_loess <- ggplot() +
  # 95% CI ribbon
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pca_loess_mean_ci,
    aes(x = fpr, ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.2, color = NA
  ) +
  # mean curve
  geom_line(
    data = pca_loess_mean_ci,
    aes(x = fpr, y = mean_tpr, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~Dimension, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  labs(
    x        = "False positive rate",
    y        = "True positive rate",
    subtitle = "C"
  )
pca_p_roc_loess
```

```{r}
#| label: pca_change_visuals
#| include: false
#| 
files_info <- list(
  list(path = file.path(root, "changes", 'retest', "pca", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "follow-up"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "changes", 'questionnaire', "pca", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "survey"),
  list(path = file.path(root, "changes", 'beads', "pca", "summary_predicted_vs_true_values.csv"), source = "game")
)

pca_predictions_df_change <- lapply(files_info, function(info) {
  df <- read.csv(info$path)
  df$Dimension <- str_replace_all(df$Dimension, "\\-", " ")
  df$Source <- info$source
  df
}) |> bind_rows()

# Real metrics
pca_real_change_retest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "retest", "pca","summary_regression.csv"), "follow-up") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
pca_real_change_quest <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "questionnaire", "pca","summary_regression.csv"), "survey") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
pca_real_change_beads <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "beads", "pca","summary_regression.csv"), "game") |>
  select(Dimension, NRMSE, R, AUC_ROC, Source) |> distinct()
pca_real_change = rbind(pca_real_change_retest, pca_real_change_quest, pca_real_change_beads)

# Permuted
pca_perm_rho_change_retest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "retest", "pca", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "follow-up")
pca_perm_rho_change_quest  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "questionnaire", "pca", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "survey")
pca_perm_rho_change_beads  <- read_with_source(file.path(root,"changes", "beads", "pca", "summary_permuted_rhos.csv"),  "game")
pca_perm_rho_change = rbind(pca_perm_rho_change_retest, pca_perm_rho_change_quest, pca_perm_rho_change_beads)


pca_p_scatter_change <- ggplot(
  pca_predictions_df_change,
  aes(x = True, y = Predicted)
) +
  geom_hex(bins = 75) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "D") +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, ncol = 3, scales = 'free') +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(x = "True change", y = "Predicted change", subtitle = "E")

##
## z-scored (PCA changes)
##
dims_to_show <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")

# 1) z-score ΔTrue / ΔPredicted within each PC
pca_change_z <- pca_predictions_df_change %>%
  dplyr::filter(Dimension %in% dims_to_show) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    True_z      = as.numeric(scale(True)),
    Predicted_z = as.numeric(scale(Predicted))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 2) common x-grid and per-PC OLS + 95% CI (mean) in z-space
x_grid_z_change <- tibble::tibble(True_z = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 400))

fits_pca_change_z <- pca_change_z %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    m  <- stats::lm(Predicted_z ~ True_z, data = .x)
    pr <- tibble::as_tibble(stats::predict(m, newdata = x_grid_z_change,
                                           interval = "confidence", level = 0.95))
    dplyr::bind_cols(x_grid_z_change, pr)  # cols: True_z, fit, lwr, upr
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

# 3) plot (z-scored PCA changes)
p_scatter_change_pca_z <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = pca_change_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = Predicted_z, color = Dimension),
    size = 0.01, alpha = 0.01
  ) +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = fits_pca_change_z,
    aes(x = True_z, ymin = lwr, ymax = upr, fill = Dimension, group = Dimension),
```
