```{r}
    alpha = 0.20, color = NA, inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = fits_pca_change_z,
    aes(x = True_z, y = fit, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4, inherit.aes = FALSE, alpha = 0.75
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  facet_wrap(~ Dimension, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = grid::unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(-3, 3), breaks = c(-2, 0, 2)) +
  labs(
    x = "True change (z)",
    y = "Predicted change (z)",
    subtitle = "E"
  )

pca_perm_rho_change_long <- pca_perm_rho_change |>
  dplyr::rename(Value = Permuted_R) |>
  mutate(Metric = "r") |>
  select(Dimension, Value, Metric, Source)

pca_permuted_rho_change_combined <- bind_rows(pca_perm_rho_change_long) |>
  mutate(
    Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r"), ordered = TRUE)
  )

pca_real_change_long <- pca_real_change |>
  pivot_longer(cols = c("R", "AUC_ROC", "NRMSE"), names_to = "Metric", values_to = "Value") |>
  mutate(Metric = recode(Metric, "R" = "r", "AUC_ROC" = "AUC", "NRMSE" = "e"),
         Metric = factor(Metric, levels = c("r","e"), ordered = TRUE))

pd <- position_dodge2(width = 0.2)
pca_p_change_permuted <- ggplot() +
  # pooled null violins (transparent outline)
  geom_violin(
    data = pca_permuted_rho_change_combined %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.35, width = 1, bw = 0.001, color = NA
  ) +
  # 2) FOLLOW-UP & GAME — hollow markers (white fill, colored outline)
  geom_point(
    data = pca_real_change_long %>% dplyr::filter(Metric == "r"),
    aes(x = Dimension, y = Value, color = Dimension, shape = Source),
    #position = pd,
    size = 1, stroke = 0.6, fill = "white"
  ) +
  # Shape mapping (fillable shapes)
  scale_shape_manual(values = c("follow-up" = 21, "game" = 22, "survey" = 23)) +
  # Dimension colors (outline + baseline fill)
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
 geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  labs(x = NULL, y = 'r', subtitle = "G") +
  ylim(-0.1,0.7) +
  theme(
    legend.position   = "none",
    legend.text       = element_text(size = base_font_size * 0.8), # slightly smaller text
    axis.text.x       = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major  = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor  = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y   = unit(1, "lines")
  )
  
## PCA change stats
  
STAT.pca_changes = t.test(subset(pca_real_change_long, Metric == "r" & Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))$Value)
```

```{r}
#| label: pca_change_rocs
#| include: false
#| 
# Real metrics
pca_real_roc_retest_change     <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "changes", "retest", "pca", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "follow-up")
pca_real_roc_game_change        <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "changes", "beads", "pca", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "game")
pca_real_roc_survey_change      <- read_with_source(file.path(root, "changes", "questionnaire", "pca", "summary_roc_curves.csv"),     "survey")

pca_real_joint_roc_change_df <- bind_rows(
  pca_real_roc_retest_change,
  pca_real_roc_game_change,
  pca_real_roc_survey_change,
)

grid <- seq(0, 1, by = 0.001)

pca_loess_by_dim_src_change <- pca_real_joint_roc_change_df %>%
  dplyr::arrange(fpr) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, Source) %>%
  dplyr::group_modify(~{
    df <- dplyr::distinct(.x, fpr, tpr)  # avoid dup fpr rows
    fit <- stats::loess(
      tpr ~ fpr, data = df,
      span = 0.25, degree = 1, surface = "direct",
      control = loess.control(trace.hat = "approx")
    )
    t_hat <- predict(fit, newdata = data.frame(fpr = grid))
    t_hat <- pmin(pmax(t_hat, 0), 1)
    t_hat <- cummax(replace(t_hat, is.na(t_hat), 0))
    tibble::tibble(fpr = grid, tpr_hat = t_hat)
  }) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

pca_loess_mean_ci_change <- pca_loess_by_dim_src_change %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, fpr) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n        = dplyr::n(),
    mean_tpr = mean(tpr_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_tpr   = sd(tpr_hat,   na.rm = TRUE),
    se_tpr   = sd_tpr / sqrt(n),
    ci_low   = pmax(0, mean_tpr - 1.96 * se_tpr),
    ci_high  = pmin(1, mean_tpr + 1.96 * se_tpr),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

pca_p_roc_loess_change <- ggplot() +
  geom_ribbon(
    data = pca_loess_mean_ci_change,
    aes(x = fpr, ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high, fill = Dimension),
    alpha = 0.2, color = NA
  ) +
  geom_line(
    data = pca_loess_mean_ci_change,
    aes(x = fpr, y = mean_tpr, color = Dimension, group = Dimension),
    linewidth = 0.4
  ) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
              color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  #facet_wrap(~ Dimension, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), breaks = c(0.25, 0.75)) +
  theme(
    legend.position  = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y  = unit(1, "lines")
  ) +
  labs(
    x        = "False positive rate",
    y        = "True positive rate",
    title = "C",
    subtitle = "MAILA tracks changes \nalong PC1-3"
  )

```

To test whether human–computer interactions encode symptom-specific markers beyond a one-dimensional scalar of distress, we transformed the $\mathrm{Y^{N \times Q}}$ mental health matrix into a set of orthogonal principal components (PC). We then trained independent SVR models to predict each PC, treating PCs as orthogonal sources of variation in self-reported mental health. Based on the recorded human–computer interactions alone, MAILA successfully predicted the location of held-out participants on the first 3 PCs of mental health, which together explained `r 100 * variance_explained$Cumulative_Variance[3]`% of the variance across all datasets (Figure 4A). 

PC1 reflected a general distress-to-wellbeing axis and was decoded at R = `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC1")$R)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC1")$R)$error` (p = `r subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC1")$p_r[1]`). PC2 separated depression and interpersonal sensitivity from other types of distress (R = `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC2")$R)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC2")$R)$error`, p = `r subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC2")$p_r[1]`). PC3 placed somatization and hostility on one end, obsessive-compulsive symptoms and interpersonal sensitivity on the other, and anxiety, depression, psychoticism, and paranoid ideation in between (R = `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC3")$R)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC3")$R)$error`, p = `r subset(pca_real_joint_df, Dimension == "PC3")$p_r[1]`; Figure 4B). On all 3 axes, MAILA remained highly sensitive to within-participant changes (direction of change across PC1-3: AUC = `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_change_long, Metric == "AUC" & Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))$Value)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_change_long, Metric == "AUC" & Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))$Value)$error`; Figure 4C). We found weaker predictions beyond PC3 (R = `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, !Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))$R)$mean` ± `r mean_ci_95(subset(pca_real_joint_df, !Dimension %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))$R)$error`), suggesting that human–computer interactions capture the most dominant axes of variation in self-reported mental health (Figure S19).

```{r}
#| label: pc_slopes_original
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"
# slopes of reconstructed Value ~ t within each single-PC sweep
slopes <- pca_projections %>%
  dplyr::select(PC, t, Depression, Emotional.Wellbeing) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_longer(
    cols = c(Depression, Emotional.Wellbeing),
    names_to = "Dimension",
    values_to = "Value"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Dimension, PC) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    slope = coef(lm(Value ~ t))[2],
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PC = factor(PC, levels = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = PC, values_from = slope) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PC1 = ifelse(is.na(PC1), 0, PC1),
    PC2 = ifelse(is.na(PC2), 0, PC2),
    PC3 = ifelse(is.na(PC3), 0, PC3),
    # match custom_colors keys
    Dimension = gsub("[-\\.]", " ", Dimension),
    label = Dimension
  )
```

```{r}
#| label: pc_naturalistic_projection
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"

traj_df <- read.csv(file.path(root, "generalization", "naturalistic", "PC_space", "pc_traj_user_curves.csv"))
centroids <- read.csv(file.path(root, "generalization", "naturalistic", "PC_space", "pc_traj_centroids.csv"))
group_df  <- read.csv(file.path(root, "generalization", "naturalistic", "PC_space", "pc_traj_group_curve.csv"))

traj_df <- traj_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PC1 = as.numeric(PC1),
    PC2 = as.numeric(PC2),
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour),
    user = as.character(user)
  )

group_df <- group_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PC1 = as.numeric(PC1),
    PC2 = as.numeric(PC2),
    LocalHour = as.numeric(LocalHour)
  )

centroids <- centroids %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PC1 = as.numeric(PC1),
    PC2 = as.numeric(PC2),
    PC3 = as.numeric(PC3),
    n   = as.numeric(n),
    user = as.character(user)
  )

arrow_center_x <- 0
arrow_center_y <- 0
arrow_scale <- 0.4 

xr <- range(c(traj_df$PC1, group_df$PC1, centroids$PC1), na.rm = TRUE)
yr <- range(c(traj_df$PC2, group_df$PC2, centroids$PC2), na.rm = TRUE)
x_span <- diff(xr); y_span <- diff(yr)

slopes_plot <- slopes %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    norm12 = sqrt(PC1^2 + PC2^2),
    ux = ifelse(norm12 > 0, PC1 / norm12, 0),
    uy = ifelse(norm12 > 0, PC2 / norm12, 0),
    x0 = arrow_center_x,
    y0 = arrow_center_y,
    xend = arrow_center_x + ux * arrow_scale * x_span,
    yend = arrow_center_y + uy * arrow_scale * y_span
  )

size_min <- 1
size_max <- 1
use_abs_pc3 <- FALSE

centroids <- centroids %>%
  dplyr::mutate(PC3_for_size = if (use_abs_pc3) abs(PC3) else PC3)

rng <- range(centroids$PC3_for_size, na.rm = TRUE)
if (!is.finite(rng[1]) || !is.finite(rng[2]) || rng[2] <= rng[1]) {
  centroids$Size <- (size_min + size_max) / 2
} else {
  centroids$Size <- size_min + (size_max - size_min) *
    (centroids$PC3_for_size - rng[1]) / (rng[2] - rng[1])
}

alpha_min <- 0.6
alpha_max <- 1

pc3_abs_rng <- range(abs(centroids$PC3), na.rm = TRUE)
if (!is.finite(pc3_abs_rng[1]) || !is.finite(pc3_abs_rng[2]) || pc3_abs_rng[2] <= pc3_abs_rng[1]) {
  centroids <- centroids %>% dplyr::mutate(Alpha = (alpha_min + alpha_max) / 2)
} else {
  centroids <- centroids %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Alpha = alpha_min + (alpha_max - alpha_min) *
                    (abs(PC3) - pc3_abs_rng[1]) / (pc3_abs_rng[2] - pc3_abs_rng[1]))
}
centroids$Alpha <- as.numeric(centroids$Alpha)

traj_df <- traj_df %>%
  dplyr::left_join(centroids %>% dplyr::select(user, Alpha), by = "user") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Alpha = as.numeric(ifelse(is.na(Alpha), alpha_min, Alpha)))

label_hours <- c(8, 16, 24)
label_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(label_hours, function(h) {
  i <- which.min(abs(group_df$LocalHour - h))
  data.frame(
    LocalHour = group_df$LocalHour[i],
    PC1 = group_df$PC1[i],
    PC2 = group_df$PC2[i],
    lab = paste0(h, "h")
  )
}))

p_pc_traj <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = slopes_plot,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = xend, yend = yend, color = Dimension),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.5,
    lineend = "round",
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.1, "in")),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = slopes_plot,
    aes(x = xend, y = yend, label = label, color = Dimension),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = -0.6,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +

  geom_path(
    data = traj_df,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, group = user, alpha = Alpha),
    linewidth = 0.20,
    color = pc_palette[4]
  ) +
  geom_path(
    data = group_df,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2),
    linewidth = 0.6,
    alpha = 0.95,
    color = pc_palette[1]
  ) +
  geom_path(
    data = group_df,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2),
    linewidth = 2,
    alpha = 0.2,
    color = pc_palette[1]
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = label_df,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2),
    size = 1,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = label_df,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, label = lab),
    size = 2,
    vjust = -0.8,
    color = "black"
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = centroids,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, alpha = Alpha),
    color = pc_palette[3],
    size = 1,
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  scale_alpha_continuous(
    limits = c(alpha_min, alpha_max),
    breaks = c(alpha_min, (alpha_min + alpha_max) / 2, alpha_max),
    labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High"),
    name = "PC3"
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2",
    title = "D",
    subtitle = "MAILA tracks trajectories in PC space"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    legend.justification = c(0, 1),
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  )

p_pc_traj
```

```{r}
#| label: iet_stats
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"

base_dir <- "/Users/veithweilnhammer/Github/cursor_mind/iet"  # <-- change if needed
datasets <- c("bayes_dataset", "beat_dataset", "context_dataset")

pattern <- "^merged_(og|pca)_(arousal|valence)_df\\.csv$"

all_files <- unlist(lapply(datasets, function(ds) {
  ds_dir <- file.path(base_dir, ds, "summary")
  if (!dir.exists(ds_dir)) return(character(0))
  list.files(ds_dir, pattern = pattern, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
}), use.names = FALSE)

stopifnot(length(all_files) > 0)

parse_meta <- function(path) {
  parts <- strsplit(normalizePath(path, winslash = "/"), "/")[[1]]
  ds_idx <- which(parts %in% datasets)
  dataset <- if (length(ds_idx) > 0) parts[ds_idx[1]] else NA_character_

  # model = first folder after dataset (common layout)
  model <- if (!is.na(dataset) && (ds_idx[1] + 1) <= length(parts)) parts[ds_idx[1] + 1] else "unknown_model"

  fname <- basename(path)
  m <- stringr::str_match(fname, "^merged_(og|pca)_(arousal|valence)_df\\.csv$")
  feature_type <- m[,2]  # og / pca
  contrast     <- m[,3]  # arousal / valence

  tibble::tibble(
    file = path,
    dataset = dataset,
    model = model,
    feature_type = feature_type,
    contrast = contrast
  )
}

meta_df <- purrr::map_dfr(all_files, parse_meta)

raw_df <- meta_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(data = purrr::map(file, ~ readr::read_csv(.x, show_col_types = FALSE))) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(data) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    dataset = factor(dataset, levels = datasets),
    feature_type = factor(feature_type, levels = c("og", "pca")),
    contrast = factor(contrast, levels = c("arousal", "valence"))
  )

raw_df$Dimension <- gsub("[-\\.]", " ", raw_df$Dimension)

palette_all <- c(pc_palette, custom_colors)
pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.75)

summary_diff <- raw_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(model, feature_type, contrast, Dimension) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    ci = list(mean_ci_95(Difference)),
    p  = tryCatch(t.test(Difference, mu = 0)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$mean),
    lo   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$lower),
    hi   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$upper),
    se   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$se),
    err  = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$error)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-ci) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(model, feature_type, contrast) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    p_bonf = p.adjust(p, method = "bonferroni"),
    p_fdr  = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup()

raw_og_long <- raw_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(feature_type == "og") %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Contrast = stringr::str_to_title(as.character(contrast)),
    Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("Arousal", "Valence")),
    Dimension = as.character(Dimension),
    Dimension_display = factor(Dimension, levels = unique(ordered_standard_dimensions)),
    Model = factor(as.character(model), levels = unique(as.character(model)))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Contrast, Model,
    Dimension, Dimension_display,
    participant = Participant_idx,
    group = "High",
    value = Prediction_high
  ) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    raw_df %>%
      dplyr::filter(feature_type == "og") %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        Contrast = stringr::str_to_title(as.character(contrast)),
        Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("Arousal", "Valence")),
        Dimension = as.character(Dimension),
        Dimension_display = factor(Dimension, levels = unique(ordered_standard_dimensions)),
        Model = factor(as.character(model), levels = unique(as.character(model)))
      ) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Contrast, Model,
        Dimension, Dimension_display,
        participant = Participant_idx,
        group = "Low",
        value = Prediction_low
      )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(group = factor(group, levels = c("Low", "High"))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(value))

sum_og <- raw_og_long %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Model, Contrast, Dimension, Dimension_display, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    ci = list(mean_ci_95(value)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$mean),
    lo   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$lower),
    hi   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$upper)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-ci)

pc_keep <- c("PC1","PC2","PC3")

raw_pca_diff <- raw_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(feature_type == "pca", Dimension %in% pc_keep) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Contrast = stringr::str_to_title(as.character(contrast)),
    Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("Arousal", "Valence")),
    Dimension = as.character(Dimension),
    Dimension_display = factor(Dimension, levels = pc_keep),
    Model = factor(as.character(model), levels = unique(as.character(model)))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(Difference))

sum_pca_diff <- raw_pca_diff %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Model, Contrast, Dimension, Dimension_display) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    ci = list(mean_ci_95(Difference)),
    p  = tryCatch(t.test(Difference, mu = 0)$p.value, error = function(e) NA_real_),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$mean),
    lo   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$lower),
    hi   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$upper)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-ci)

raw_pca_long <- raw_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(feature_type == "pca", Dimension %in% pc_keep) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Contrast = stringr::str_to_title(as.character(contrast)),
    Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("Arousal", "Valence")),
    Dimension = as.character(Dimension),
    Dimension_display = factor(Dimension, levels = pc_keep),
    Model = factor(as.character(model), levels = unique(as.character(model)))
  ) %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    Contrast, Model,
    Dimension, Dimension_display,
    participant = Participant_idx,
    group = "High",
    value = Prediction_high
  ) %>%
  dplyr::bind_rows(
    raw_df %>%
      dplyr::filter(feature_type == "pca", Dimension %in% pc_keep) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        Contrast = stringr::str_to_title(as.character(contrast)),
        Contrast = factor(Contrast, levels = c("Arousal", "Valence")),
        Dimension = as.character(Dimension),
        Dimension_display = factor(Dimension, levels = pc_keep),
        Model = factor(as.character(model), levels = unique(as.character(model)))
      ) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Contrast, Model,
        Dimension, Dimension_display,
        participant = Participant_idx,
        group = "Low",
        value = Prediction_low
      )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(group = factor(group, levels = c("Low", "High"))) %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(value))

sum_pca_values <- raw_pca_long %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Model, Contrast, Dimension, Dimension_display, group) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    ci = list(mean_ci_95(value)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    mean = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$mean),
    lo   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$lower),
    hi   = purrr::map_dbl(ci, ~ .x$upper)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-ci)
```

```{r}
#| label: iet_summary
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"
#| 
library(purrr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(tibble)

pattern_counts <- "^behavior_counts_summary\\.csv$"

count_files <- unlist(lapply(datasets, function(ds) {
  ds_dir <- file.path(base_dir, ds, "summary")
  if (!dir.exists(ds_dir)) return(character(0))
  list.files(ds_dir, pattern = pattern_counts, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
}), use.names = FALSE)

stopifnot(length(count_files) > 0)

parse_ds <- function(path) {
  parts <- strsplit(normalizePath(path, winslash = "/"), "/")[[1]]
  ds_idx <- which(parts %in% datasets)
  dataset <- if (length(ds_idx) > 0) parts[ds_idx[1]] else NA_character_
  tibble(file = path, dataset = dataset)
}

meta_counts <- purrr::map_dfr(count_files, parse_ds)

counts_df <- meta_counts %>%
  mutate(data = purrr::map(file, ~ readr::read_csv(.x, show_col_types = FALSE))) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(data) %>%
  mutate(dataset = factor(dataset, levels = datasets)) %>%
  arrange(dataset)

counts_total <- counts_df %>%
  summarise(
    dataset = "TOTAL",
    n_participants = sum(n_participants, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_videos       = sum(n_videos, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

counts_df_all_iet <- dplyr::bind_rows(counts_df, counts_total)
```

```{r}
#| label: iet_plots
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"
pd <- position_dodge(width = 0.3)

p_og_values <- ggplot() +
  geom_point(
    data = raw_og_long,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = value,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = group,
      group = group
    ),
    position = position_jitterdodge(
      jitter.width = 0.12,
      jitter.height = 0,
      dodge.width = 0.75,
      seed = 1
    ),
    alpha = 0.10,
    size = 0.6,
    stroke = 0
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = sum_og,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = mean,
      ymin = lo,
      ymax = hi,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = group,
      group = group
    ),
    position = pd,
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = sum_og,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = mean,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = group,
      group = group
    ),
    position = pd,
    size = 1.4,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = palette_all, guide = "none") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(Low = 15, High = 16)) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(Contrast), scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = "Predicted score (High vs Low)",
    x = NULL,
    shape = NULL,
    subtitle = "Original dimensions: High vs Low"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  coord_flip()

p_pca_values <- ggplot() +
  geom_linerange(
    data = sum_pca_values,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = mean,
      ymin = lo,
      ymax = hi,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = group,
      group = group
    ),
    position = pd,
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.5,
    alpha = 0.85
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = sum_pca_values,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = mean,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = group,
      group = group
    ),
    position = pd,
    size = 1,
    alpha = 0.95,
    fill = "white",          # <-- filled white
    stroke = 0.6            # <-- visible outline
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = palette_all, guide = "none") +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(`Low` = 25, `High` = 24)) +  # 25=down triangle, 24=up triangle (both fillable)
  facet_grid(cols = vars(Contrast), scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = "Projection",
    x = "Components",
    shape = NULL,
    title = "E",
    subtitle = "PC1-3 track dynamic changes in arousal and valence"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),

    # legend inside plot: top-left (shape only)
    legend.position = c(0.02, 0.98),
    legend.justification = c(0, 1),
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = scales::alpha("white", 0.75), color = NA),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2)
  )

p_pca_values

p_pca_values

p_pca_diff <- ggplot() +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = sum_pca_diff,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = mean,
      ymin = lo,
      ymax = hi,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = Model,
      linetype = Model
    ),
    position = pd,
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 0.6,
    alpha = 0.90
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = sum_pca_diff,
    aes(
      x = Dimension_display,
      y = mean,
      color = Dimension,
      shape = Model
    ),
    position = pd,
    size = 1.6,
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.25) +
  scale_color_manual(values = palette_all, guide = "none") +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(Contrast), scales = "free_y", space = "free_y") +
  labs(
    y = "Difference (High/Positive − Low/Negative) ± 95% CI",
    x = NULL,
    shape = NULL,
    linetype = NULL,
    subtitle = "B"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    legend.position = "top"
  ) +
  coord_flip()

p_pca_diff
```

```{r}
#| label: iet_pca_joint_3d
#| echo: false
#| include: false
#| fig.align: "center"
#| out.width: "100%"

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(purrr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

base_dir <- "/Users/veithweilnhammer/Github/cursor_mind/iet"
datasets <- c("bayes_dataset", "beat_dataset", "context_dataset")

pattern_joint <- "pc_joint_pred_wide_participants"  # your saved pred_wide files

joint_files <- unlist(lapply(datasets, function(ds) {
  ds_dir <- file.path(base_dir, ds, "summary")
  if (!dir.exists(ds_dir)) return(character(0))
  list.files(ds_dir, pattern = pattern_joint, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
}), use.names = FALSE)

stopifnot(length(joint_files) > 0)

parse_meta_joint <- function(path) {
  parts <- strsplit(normalizePath(path, winslash = "/"), "/")[[1]]
  ds_idx <- which(parts %in% datasets)
  dataset <- if (length(ds_idx) > 0) parts[ds_idx[1]] else NA_character_
  model <- if (!is.na(dataset) && (ds_idx[1] + 1) <= length(parts)) parts[ds_idx[1] + 1] else "unknown_model"
  tibble::tibble(file = path, dataset = dataset, model = model)
}

meta_joint <- purrr::map_dfr(joint_files, parse_meta_joint)

pred_joint <- meta_joint %>%
  dplyr::mutate(data = purrr::map(file, ~ readr::read_csv(.x, show_col_types = FALSE))) %>%
  tidyr::unnest(data) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dataset = factor(dataset, levels = datasets),
    Model   = factor(as.character(model), levels = unique(as.character(model))),
    Group   = as.character(Group),
    PC1 = as.numeric(PC1),
    PC2 = as.numeric(PC2),
    PC3 = as.numeric(PC3)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(PC1), is.finite(PC2), is.finite(PC3))

summary_iet_participants_by_group <- pred_joint %>%
  dplyr::group_by(dataset) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows = n(),
    n_participants = dplyr::n_distinct(Participant_idx, na.rm = TRUE),
    has_ids = any(!is.na(Participant_idx)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(dataset)

label_group <- function(g) {
  g <- as.character(g)
  if (g == "Center") return("Center")
  g2 <- g
  g2 <- gsub("^HighA", "High Arousal", g2)
  g2 <- gsub("^LowA",  "Low Arousal",  g2)
  g2 <- gsub("HighV$", "Positive Valence", g2)
  g2 <- gsub("LowV$",  "Negative Valence", g2)
  g2 <- gsub(" \\+ ", " | ", g2)
  g2
}

pred_joint <- pred_joint %>%
  dplyr::mutate(Group_label = vapply(Group, label_group, character(1)))

centroids <- pred_joint %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Group, Group_label) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    ci1 = list(mean_ci_95(PC1)),
    ci2 = list(mean_ci_95(PC2)),
    x   = purrr::map_dbl(ci1, ~ .x$mean),
    xlo = purrr::map_dbl(ci1, ~ .x$lower),
    xhi = purrr::map_dbl(ci1, ~ .x$upper),
    y   = purrr::map_dbl(ci2, ~ .x$mean),
    ylo = purrr::map_dbl(ci2, ~ .x$lower),
    yhi = purrr::map_dbl(ci2, ~ .x$upper),
    n   = dplyr::n_distinct(Participant_idx),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-ci1, -ci2)

center_df <- pred_joint %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Group = "Center",
    Group_label = "Center",
    ci1 = list(mean_ci_95(PC1)),
    ci2 = list(mean_ci_95(PC2)),
    x   = purrr::map_dbl(ci1, ~ .x$mean),
    xlo = purrr::map_dbl(ci1, ~ .x$lower),
    xhi = purrr::map_dbl(ci1, ~ .x$upper),
    y   = purrr::map_dbl(ci2, ~ .x$mean),
    ylo = purrr::map_dbl(ci2, ~ .x$lower),
    yhi = purrr::map_dbl(ci2, ~ .x$upper),
    n   = dplyr::n_distinct(Participant_idx)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(-ci1, -ci2)

centroids_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(centroids, center_df) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Group = factor(Group, levels = c("LowA + LowV", "LowA + HighV", "HighA + LowV", "HighA + HighV", "Center"))
  )

if (!exists("slopes")) {
  stop("Object `slopes` not found. Please compute `slopes` from pca_projections before this chunk.")
}

# center arrows on the "Center" centroid
center_xy <- centroids_all %>%
  dplyr::filter(as.character(Group) == "Center" | Group_label == "Center") %>%
  dplyr::summarise(cx = mean(x, na.rm = TRUE), cy = mean(y, na.rm = TRUE))

arrow_center_x <- center_xy$cx
arrow_center_y <- center_xy$cy

# use the same axis limits you apply at the end for consistent arrow scaling
xlim_joint <- c(-0.2, 2)
ylim_joint <- c(0, 1)
x_span <- diff(xlim_joint)
y_span <- diff(ylim_joint)

arrow_length <- 0.4  # shorter arrows for this plot (fraction of span)

slopes_plot_joint <- slopes %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Dimension = gsub("[-\\.]", " ", Dimension),  # match custom_colors keys
    label = Dimension,

    norm12 = sqrt(PC1^2 + PC2^2),
    ux = ifelse(norm12 > 0, PC1 / norm12, 0),
    uy = ifelse(norm12 > 0, PC2 / norm12, 0),

    x0 = arrow_center_x,
    y0 = arrow_center_y,
    xend = arrow_center_x + ux * arrow_length * x_span,
    yend = arrow_center_y + uy * arrow_length * y_span
  )

alpha_min <- 0.001  # <- barely visible floor; adjust (e.g., 0.10–0.25)
alpha_max <- 0.01   # <- keep dots subtle even at max; adjust (e.g., 0.5–1.0)

pc3_abs_rng <- range(abs(pred_joint$PC3), na.rm = TRUE)
if (!is.finite(pc3_abs_rng[1]) || !is.finite(pc3_abs_rng[2]) || pc3_abs_rng[2] <= pc3_abs_rng[1]) {
  pred_joint <- pred_joint %>% dplyr::mutate(Alpha = (alpha_min + alpha_max) / 2)
} else {
  pred_joint <- pred_joint %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Alpha = alpha_min + (alpha_max - alpha_min) *
        (abs(PC3) - pc3_abs_rng[1]) / (pc3_abs_rng[2] - pc3_abs_rng[1])
    )
}
pred_joint$Alpha <- as.numeric(pred_joint$Alpha)

p_pc_joint <- ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = slopes_plot_joint,
    aes(x = x0, y = y0, xend = xend, yend = yend, color = Dimension),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    linewidth = 0.45,
    alpha = 0.5,
    lineend = "round",
    arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.08, "in")),
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_text(
    data = slopes_plot_joint,
    aes(x = xend, y = yend, label = label, color = Dimension),
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    size = 2.0,
    hjust = 0,
    vjust = -0.6,
    alpha = 1,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = custom_colors) +
  geom_point(
    data = pred_joint,
    aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, alpha = Alpha),
    size = 0.5,
    color = pc_palette[3],
    stroke = 0
  ) +

  geom_segment(
    data = centroids_all,
    aes(x = xlo, xend = xhi, y = y, yend = y),
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = pc_palette[1]
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = centroids_all,
    aes(x = x, xend = x, y = ylo, yend = yhi),
    linewidth = 0.5,
    color = pc_palette[1]
  ) +

  geom_point(
    data = centroids_all,
    aes(x = x, y = y),
    size = 1,
    color = pc_palette[1],
    stroke = 0.25
  ) +

  ggrepel::geom_text_repel(
    data = centroids_all,
    aes(x = x, y = y, label = Group_label),
    size = 2,
    color = "black",
    box.padding   = 0.25,
    point.padding = 0.25,
    min.segment.length = 0,
    segment.color = "black",
    segment.size  = 0.5,
    force = 6,
    force_pull = 0.1,
    max.iter = 5000,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    seed = 1
  ) +

  scale_alpha_continuous(
    limits = c(alpha_min, alpha_max),
    breaks = c(alpha_min, (alpha_min + alpha_max)/2, alpha_max),
    labels = c("Low", "Mid", "High"),
    name = "PC3"
  ) +

  labs(
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2",
    title = "E",
    subtitle = "MAILA tracks experiences of arousal and valence \nduring emotionally evocative videos"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    legend.justification = c(0, 1),
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    legend.key = element_rect(fill = NA, color = NA),
    legend.margin = margin(2, 2, 2, 2),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2)
  ) +
  xlim(xlim_joint[1], xlim_joint[2]) +
  ylim(ylim_joint[1], ylim_joint[2])

p_pc_joint
```

Having established that PC1–PC3 organize inter-individual differences in self-reported mental health, we next asked whether the same axes also track moment-to-moment changes in subjective experience within a person [@rutledge_computational_2014]. To this end, we applied pretrained, frozen MAILA models to four external datasets: naturalistic, open-ended human–computer interactions from 19 users performing everyday computer activities [@kilic_bogazici_2021] (Figure 4D), and three independent emotion-tracking datasets in which a total of `r subset(counts_df_all_iet, dataset == "TOTAL")$n_participants` participants used cursor movements to continuously report the arousal and valence they experienced while watching `r subset(counts_df_all_iet, dataset == "TOTAL")$n_videos` emotionally evocative videos [@ortega_inferential_2023; @ren_veatic_2024; @ortega_integration_2025] (Figure 4E).

With only naturalistic cursor movements as input, MAILA recovered systematic within-day trajectories in mental-health PC space. Across the day, decoded states shifted toward higher PC1 scores, consistent with increasing global distress-linked signatures, and toward lower PC2 scores, consistent with a shift away from emotional wellbeing and toward depression-linked distress (Figure 4D). 

During the video tasks, MAILA also tracked moment-to-moment fluctuations in affective experience. Periods of higher experienced arousal were accompanied by higher PC1 scores (shift toward distress; p = `r subset(sum_pca_diff, Contrast == "Arousal" & Dimension == "PC1")$p`) and higher PC2 scores (shift away from depression-linked distress; p = `r subset(sum_pca_diff, Contrast == "Arousal" & Dimension == "PC2")$p`), as well as lower PC3 scores (shift toward somatization/hostility; p = `r subset(sum_pca_diff, Contrast == "Arousal" & Dimension == "PC3")$p`; Figure 4E). More positive valence was associated with lower PC1 and higher PC2 scores (p = `r subset(sum_pca_diff, Contrast == "Valence" & Dimension == "PC1")$p`; p = `r subset(sum_pca_diff, Contrast == "Valence" & Dimension == "PC2")$p`), consistent with a shift toward wellbeing and away from depression-linked distress, with no additional effect on PC3 (p = `r subset(sum_pca_diff, Contrast == "Valence" & Dimension == "PC3")$p`).

Together, these results show that human–computer interactions encode not only higher-order variation in mental health across individuals, but also within-person dynamics in subjective experience. Moment-to-moment changes in arousal and valence mapped onto the same low-dimensional PC space that organized inter-individual differences in mental health, with arousal expressed most consistently along PC1 and valence along PC2.

```{r}
#| label: Figure_4
#| include: true
#| out.width: "100%"
#| fig-cap: "**Figure 4. Human–computer interactions predict 3 orthogonal dimensions of mental health.** MAILA predicts the level (PC1) and the origins (PC2-3) of distress. **A.** Reconstructed dimension scores (0–1) while sweeping each PC score from its empirical minimum (low opacity) to maximum (high opacity), holding the remaining PCs at 0. **B.** Predictive performance across PCs. Violins show null distributions; points mark observed correlation from baseline, follow-up, survey, game, and touch datasets (filled: five-fold cross-validation; unfilled: frozen models applied to another dataset). **C.** ROC curves for classifying higher versus lower PC scores at follow-up relative to baseline. **D.** Thin lines show predicted within-user trajectories in PC space during naturalistic human–computer interactions across time of day (black dots); the thick line shows the population mean trajectory. Purple points mark each participant’s centroid, with opacity indicating depth along PC3 (higher opacity = larger PC3). **E.** Participant-level PC1 and PC2 predictions are shown as points (opacity scales with absolute PC3), overlaid with group centroids (mean ± 95% CI) for the four arousal x valence states defined by within-participant median splits (high/low arousal; positive/negative valence). Centroids were computed across participants after applying frozen MAILA models to three external emotion-tracking datasets."

lay_combined = rbind(c(1,1,1),c(2,6,5), c(3,6,5))
grid.arrange(p_projections_faceted, p_metrics_permuted_pca, pca_p_roc_loess_change, p_pc_joint, p_pc_traj,
  layout_matrix = lay_combined,
  heights = c(1.5,1,1),
  widths = c(0.4,0.7,0.7)
)

g <- arrangeGrob(p_projections_faceted, p_metrics_permuted_pca, pca_p_roc_loess_change, p_pc_joint, p_pc_traj,
  layout_matrix = lay_combined,
  heights = c(1.5,1,1),
  widths = c(0.4,0.7,0.7)
)

ggsave("cursor_mind_files/rev_Figure_4.svg", g, width = 9, height = 5, units = "in", device = "svg")
```

```{r}
#| label: causal_intervention
#| include: false
#| 
rt_valence_arousal = read.csv('/Users/veithweilnhammer/Github/iet_tracking/Data/arousal_valence_prediction.csv')

Distress_Arousal_Valence <- lmer(Prediction ~ Arousal * Valence + (1 | Participant_idx), data = rt_valence_arousal[rt_valence_arousal$Dimension == "Overall Distress",])
STAT.Distress_Arousal_Valence = summary(Distress_Arousal_Valence)

Wellbeing_Arousal_Valence <- lmer(Prediction ~ Arousal * Valence + (1 | Participant_idx), data = rt_valence_arousal[rt_valence_arousal$Dimension == "Overall Wellbeing",])
STAT.Wellbeing_Arousal_Valence = summary(Wellbeing_Arousal_Valence)
```

<!-- To test how swiftly cursor-based predictions would adjust to changes in emotion, we analyzed data from a second independent experiment that manipulated affective states in real time. Participants watched a series of emotionally evocative videos and continuously reported their experience using a valence-by-arousal response grid (counter-balanced response orientation and mapping across the sample). Cursor-based distress predictions increased significantly when participants experienced negative valence. Cursor dynamics are thus sensitive to real-time affective states. -->

```{r}
#| label: pca_shap_values
#| include: false
library(reshape2)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(lsa)  # For cosine similarity

# Load SHAP values
shap_full_df <- read.csv(file.path(root, "shap/shap_full_PCA.csv"))

# Drop Participant_ID (not needed for aggregation)
shap_full_df <- shap_full_df[, -1]

# Convert to long format
shap_long_df <- gather(shap_full_df, key = "Feature", value = "SHAP_Value", -PCA_Component)

# Compute mean SHAP values per feature and PCA component
mean_shap_df <- ddply(shap_long_df, .(PCA_Component, Feature), summarise, 
                      Mean_SHAP = mean(SHAP_Value, na.rm = TRUE),
                      Abs_Mean_SHAP = mean(abs(SHAP_Value), na.rm = TRUE))

# Extract SHAP vectors for each PCA component
shap_pc1 <- mean_shap_df %>% filter(PCA_Component == "PC1") %>% arrange(Feature) %>% pull(Mean_SHAP)
shap_pc2 <- mean_shap_df %>% filter(PCA_Component == "PC2") %>% arrange(Feature) %>% pull(Mean_SHAP)
shap_pc3 <- mean_shap_df %>% filter(PCA_Component == "PC3") %>% arrange(Feature) %>% pull(Mean_SHAP)

# Combine into a matrix
shap_matrix <- cbind(shap_pc1, shap_pc2, shap_pc3)

# Compute cosine similarity matrix
cosine_similarity_matrix <- cosine(shap_matrix)


# Compute mean off-diagonal similarity
off_diag_values <- mean(cosine_similarity_matrix[upper.tri(cosine_similarity_matrix)], na.rm = TRUE)


off_diag_cosine_shap = cosine_similarity_matrix[upper.tri(cosine_similarity_matrix)]
```

<!-- SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP) indicated substantial differentiation in the $N \times C$ movement features that were relevant for predicting PC1-3, as indicated by an average cosine similarity `r mean(off_diag_cosine_shap)` ± `r mean_ci_95(off_diag_cosine_shap)$error` between the PC-specific SHAP vectors.  -->

```{r}
#| label: dimensionsal_regression_scores
#| include: false

# Load the full participant-level error data
final_participant_errors_df <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'regression', 'cross_question_performance_participant_errors.csv'))

# Trim unnecessary columns to reduce file size for GitHub
columns_to_keep <- c(
  "Participant_ID",
  "Test_Question_ID",
  "Train_Dimension",
  "Test_Dimension",
  "Residuals",
  "True_Values"
)
final_participant_errors_df <- final_participant_errors_df[, columns_to_keep]

# Overwrite the original CSV file with the trimmed version
write.csv(final_participant_errors_df, 
          file.path(root, 'regression', 'cross_question_performance_participant_errors.csv'), 
          row.names = FALSE)

# Add derived column: Index_Dimension
final_participant_errors_df$Index_Dimension <- "Non-index"

# Define wellbeing and distress dimensions
wellbeing_dimensions <- c("Emotional Wellbeing", "Social Wellbeing", "Psychological Wellbeing", "Overall Wellbeing")
distress_dimensions <- setdiff(unique(final_participant_errors_df$Train_Dimension), c(wellbeing_dimensions))

# Mark Congruent cases
final_participant_errors_df$Index_Dimension[
  (final_participant_errors_df$Train_Dimension == "Overall Wellbeing" & 
   final_participant_errors_df$Test_Dimension %in% wellbeing_dimensions) |
  (final_participant_errors_df$Train_Dimension == "Overall Distress" & 
   final_participant_errors_df$Test_Dimension %in% distress_dimensions)
] <- "Congruent"

# Mark Index (same) cases
final_participant_errors_df$Index_Dimension[
  final_participant_errors_df$Train_Dimension == final_participant_errors_df$Test_Dimension
] <- "Index"

# Compute range of true values per Test_Question_ID
range_by_question <- aggregate(True_Values ~ Test_Question_ID, data = final_participant_errors_df, 
                               FUN = function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE))
colnames(range_by_question)[2] <- "Value_Range"

# Merge ranges back
final_participant_errors_df <- merge(final_participant_errors_df, range_by_question, 
                                     by = "Test_Question_ID", all.x = TRUE)

# Compute normalized squared residuals
final_participant_errors_df$Normalized_Squared_Residual <- 
  (final_participant_errors_df$Residuals / final_participant_errors_df$Value_Range)^2

# Run mixed models if not loading summary data
if (!load_summary_data) {

  error_same_versus_congruent <- lmer(
    sqrt(Normalized_Squared_Residual) ~ Index_Dimension + (1 | Participant_ID), 
    data = subset(final_participant_errors_df, Index_Dimension != "Non-index")
  )
  STAT.error_same_versus_congruent <- summary(error_same_versus_congruent)

  error_congruent_versus_different <- lmer(
    sqrt(Normalized_Squared_Residual) ~ Index_Dimension + (1 | Participant_ID), 
    data = subset(final_participant_errors_df, Index_Dimension != "Index")
  )
  STAT.error_congruent_versus_different <- summary(error_congruent_versus_different)

  if (save_summary_data) {
    save(STAT.error_congruent_versus_different, 
         STAT.error_same_versus_congruent,
         file = "./Results/R_summary_dimensional_mse.Rdata")
  }

} else {
  load("./Results/R_summary_dimensional_mse.Rdata")
}

# Summary table for plotting
summary_dimensional_errors_index <- ddply(
  final_participant_errors_df,
  .(Index_Dimension),
  summarise,
  mean_error = mean_ci_95(sqrt(Normalized_Squared_Residual) * 100)$mean,
  ci_error = mean_ci_95(sqrt(Normalized_Squared_Residual) * 100)$error
)
```

<!-- To test MAILA’s specificity to the human-defined subdimensions of distress and wellbeing, we trained SVR models on individual subscores and evaluated their performance on the trained subscore, the global score of the corresponding domain, and non-target subscores. Prediction errors were lowest when models were evaluated on the subscore they were trained on (e = `r summary_dimensional_errors_index[summary_dimensional_errors_index$Index_Dimension == "Index",]$mean_error` ± `r summary_dimensional_errors_index[summary_dimensional_errors_index$Index_Dimension == "Index",]$ci_error`%), significantly higher when tested on the respective global score (e = `r summary_dimensional_errors_index[summary_dimensional_errors_index$Index_Dimension == "Congruent",]$mean_error` ± `r summary_dimensional_errors_index[summary_dimensional_errors_index$Index_Dimension == "Congruent",]$ci_error`%, p = `r STAT.error_same_versus_congruent$coefficients[2,5]`), and again significantly higher when tested on non-target subscores (e = `r summary_dimensional_errors_index[summary_dimensional_errors_index$Index_Dimension == "Non-index",]$mean_error` ± `r summary_dimensional_errors_index[summary_dimensional_errors_index$Index_Dimension == "Non-index",]$ci_error`%, p = `r STAT.error_congruent_versus_different$coefficients[2,5]`). -->

<!-- Movement features at baseline did not predict future changes along PC1 (R = `r trait_prediction[1,]$SpearmanR`, e = `r trait_prediction[1,]$NMRSE*100`%, p = `r trait_prediction[1,]$Permutation_p`), PC2 (R = `r trait_prediction[2,]$SpearmanR`, e = `r trait_prediction[2,]$NMRSE*100`%, p = `r trait_prediction[2,]$Permutation_p`) or PC3 (R = `r trait_prediction[3,]$SpearmanR`, e = `r trait_prediction[3,]$NMRSE*100`%, p = `r trait_prediction[3,]$Permutation_p`). This suggests that MAILA does not capture stable prognostic traits, but instead tracks dynamic, state-dependent fluctuations in mental health over time. -->

## MAILA extends cognitive phenotypes of mental health

Conventional mental health assessments rely on predefined indicators of mental health, such as responses to structured interviews and questionnaire items [@insel_research_2010]. MAILA, by contrast, learns an end-to-end mapping from human–computer interactions to mental health without specifying the underlying signals. This creates a new framework for discovering latent cognitive phenotypes that may not be exhaustively queried by self-report, yet shape everyday behavior in clinically meaningful ways [@mirea_cognitive_2025] (Figure 5A). 

```{r}
#| label: phq_gad_maila_joint_plots
#| include: false

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(readr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

gen_dir <- file.path(
  root,
  "generalization",
  "generalize_to_phq9_gad7",
  "pca_compare_maila_vs_symptoms_gen",
  "aggregate_mean_recomputed"
)

res_gen <- read_csv(
  file.path(gen_dir, "results_summary.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    PC = factor(PC, levels = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
    Model = as.character(Model)
  )

perm_rho_gen <- read_csv(
  file.path(gen_dir, "permuted_rhos.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  mutate(
    PC = factor(PC, levels = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
    Model = as.character(Model)
  )

questionnaire_map <- c(
  "GAD"  = "GAD-7",
  "PHQ"  = "PHQ-9",
  "PHQ2" = "PHQ-2"
)

combined_map <- c(
  "GAD"  = "MAILA+GAD",
  "PHQ"  = "MAILA+PHQ",
  "PHQ2" = "MAILA+PHQ2"
)

questionnaire_levels <- c("GAD-7", "PHQ-9", "PHQ-2")
pc_levels <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")
source_levels <- c("MAILA", "Questionnaire", "Questionnaire + MAILA")


panel_b_df <- bind_rows(lapply(names(questionnaire_map), function(q) {
  q_label <- questionnaire_map[[q]]

  q_df <- res_gen %>%
    filter(Model == q) %>%
    select(PC, q_r = Real_Rho)

  maila_df <- res_gen %>%
    filter(Model == "MAILA") %>%
    select(PC, maila_r = Real_Rho)

  q_df %>%
    inner_join(maila_df, by = "PC") %>%
    mutate(Questionnaire = q_label)
})) %>%
  mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(Questionnaire, levels = questionnaire_levels),
    PC = factor(PC, levels = pc_levels)
  )

lims_b <- range(c(panel_b_df$q_r, panel_b_df$maila_r), na.rm = TRUE)
lims_b[1] <- min(lims_b[1], -0.02)
lims_b[2] <- lims_b[2] + 0.01

p_symptom_vs_maila <- ggplot(
  panel_b_df,
  aes(x = q_r, y = maila_r, fill = PC, color = PC)
) +
  geom_abline(
    slope = 1, intercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.35, color = "black"
  ) +
  geom_point(
    shape = 21,
    size = 1,
    stroke = 0.6,
    alpha = 1
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~Questionnaire, nrow = 3) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette) +
  labs(
    x = "R: Questionnaire to ground-truth PC",
    y = "R: MAILA to ground-truth PC",
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "MAILA versus standard self-report scales",
    color = NULL,
    fill = NULL
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.15, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.05, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)
  ) +
  coord_equal(xlim = lims_b, ylim = lims_b)

p_symptom_vs_maila


perm_rho_gen <- readr::read_csv(
  file.path(gen_dir, "permuted_rhos.csv"),
  show_col_types = FALSE
) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    PC = factor(PC, levels = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")),
    Model = as.character(Model)
  )

questionnaire_levels <- c("GAD-7", "PHQ-9", "PHQ-2")
pc_levels <- c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")
source_levels <- c("MAILA", "Self-report", "Self-report + MAILA")

panel_c_long <- bind_rows(lapply(names(questionnaire_map), function(q) {
  q_label <- questionnaire_map[[q]]
  comb_model <- combined_map[[q]]

  bind_rows(
    res_gen %>%
      dplyr::filter(Model == "MAILA") %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Questionnaire = q_label,
        PC,
        Source = "MAILA",
        R = Real_Rho
      ),
    res_gen %>%
      dplyr::filter(Model == q) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Questionnaire = q_label,
        PC,
        Source = "Self-report",
        R = Real_Rho
      ),
    res_gen %>%
      dplyr::filter(Model == comb_model) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Questionnaire = q_label,
        PC,
        Source = "Self-report + MAILA",
        R = Real_Rho
      )
  )
})) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(Questionnaire, levels = questionnaire_levels),
    PC = factor(PC, levels = pc_levels),
    Source = factor(Source, levels = source_levels)
  )

# pooled null within each Questionnaire x PC row
panel_c_perm <- bind_rows(lapply(names(questionnaire_map), function(q) {
  q_label <- questionnaire_map[[q]]
  comb_model <- combined_map[[q]]

  bind_rows(
    perm_rho_gen %>%
      dplyr::filter(Model == "MAILA") %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Questionnaire = q_label,
        PC,
        Source = "MAILA",
        Iter,
        Value = Permuted_Rho
      ),
    perm_rho_gen %>%
      dplyr::filter(Model == q) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Questionnaire = q_label,
        PC,
        Source = "Self-report",
        Iter,
        Value = Permuted_Rho
      ),
    perm_rho_gen %>%
      dplyr::filter(Model == comb_model) %>%
      dplyr::transmute(
        Questionnaire = q_label,
        PC,
        Source = "Self-report + MAILA",
        Iter,
        Value = Permuted_Rho
      )
  )
})) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(Questionnaire, levels = questionnaire_levels),
    PC = factor(PC, levels = pc_levels),
    Source = factor(Source, levels = source_levels)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(is.finite(Value))

panel_c_seg <- panel_c_long %>%
  dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("Self-report", "Self-report + MAILA")) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols = c(Questionnaire, PC),
    names_from = Source,
    values_from = R
  ) %>%
  dplyr::rename(
    r_questionnaire = `Self-report`,
    r_combined = `Self-report + MAILA`
  )

y_lims_c <- range(c(panel_c_perm$Value, panel_c_long$R), na.rm = TRUE)
y_lims_c[1] <- min(y_lims_c[1], -0.05)
y_lims_c[2] <- y_lims_c[2] + 0.02

p_add_maila <- ggplot() +
  geom_violin(
    data = panel_c_perm,
    aes(x = Questionnaire, y = Value, fill = PC),
    alpha = 0.35,
    width = 0.95,
    bw = 0.005,
    color = NA,
    trim = TRUE
  ) +
  geom_segment(
    data = panel_c_seg,
    aes(
      x = Questionnaire,
      xend = Questionnaire,
      y = r_questionnaire,
      yend = r_combined,
      color = PC
    ),
    linewidth = 0.45,
    alpha = 0.9
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = panel_c_long %>%
      dplyr::filter(Source %in% c("Self-report", "Self-report + MAILA")),
    aes(
      x = Questionnaire,
      y = R,
      color = PC,
      fill = PC,
      shape = Source
    ),
    size = 1,
    stroke = 0.6,
    alpha = 0.5
  ) +
  geom_point(
    data = panel_c_long %>%
      dplyr::filter(Source == "MAILA"),
    aes(
      x = Questionnaire,
      y = R,
      color = PC,
      shape = Source
    ),
    fill = "white",
    size = 1,
    stroke = 0.6
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 0.25
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(PC)) +
  scale_shape_manual(
    breaks = c("MAILA", "Self-report", "Self-report + MAILA"),
    values = c(
      "MAILA" = 21,
      "Self-report" = 22,
      "Self-report + MAILA" = 23
    )
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pc_palette, guide = "none") +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "R",
    shape = NULL,
    title = "B",
    subtitle = "MAILA improves predictions from verbal self-reports"
  ) +
  guides(
    shape = guide_legend(
      nrow = 1,
      byrow = TRUE,
      override.aes = list(
        color = c("grey40", "grey40", "grey40"),
        fill = c("white", "grey40", "grey40"),
        size = c(1, 1, 1),
        stroke = c(0.6, 0.6, 0.6),
        alpha = c(1, 1, 1)
      )
    )
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = base_font_size) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "grey95", linewidth = 0.2),
    panel.spacing.y = unit(1, "lines"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.box = "horizontal",
    legend.justification = "center",
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.15, "lines"),
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.05, "lines"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.2, "lines"),
    legend.key.width = unit(0.4, "lines"),
    legend.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0),
    legend.box.margin = margin(t = 0, r = 0, b = 0, l = 0)
  ) +
  coord_flip(ylim = y_lims_c)

p_add_maila
```

```{r}
#| label: phq_gad_maila_summary_stats
#| include: false

# MAILA alone
maila_stats <- res_gen %>%
  dplyr::filter(Model == "MAILA", PC %in% c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")) %>%
  dplyr::select(PC, Real_Rho, Real_Pval, N) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(match(PC, c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3")))

maila_PC1_r <- subset(maila_stats, PC == "PC1")$Real_Rho
maila_PC1_p <- subset(maila_stats, PC == "PC1")$Real_Pval

maila_PC2_r <- subset(maila_stats, PC == "PC2")$Real_Rho
maila_PC2_p <- subset(maila_stats, PC == "PC2")$Real_Pval

maila_PC3_r <- subset(maila_stats, PC == "PC3")$Real_Rho
maila_PC3_p <- subset(maila_stats, PC == "PC3")$Real_Pval

n_validation <- max(maila_stats$N, na.rm = TRUE)

# questionnaire-only vs MAILA
report_b <- panel_b_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    delta_r = maila_r - q_r
  )

report_b_summary <- report_b %>%
  dplyr::group_by(PC) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    maila_r = dplyr::first(maila_r),
    mean_q_r = mean(q_r, na.rm = TRUE),
    maila_pct_of_mean_q = 100 * dplyr::first(maila_r) / mean(q_r, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_delta_r = mean(delta_r, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

maila_pct_PC1 <- subset(report_b_summary, PC == "PC1")$maila_pct_of_mean_q
mean_q_PC1    <- subset(report_b_summary, PC == "PC1")$mean_q_r

maila_delta_PC2 <- subset(report_b_summary, PC == "PC2")$mean_delta_r
maila_delta_PC3 <- subset(report_b_summary, PC == "PC3")$mean_delta_r

# rebuild panel_c_df for questionnaire-only vs questionnaire + MAILA
panel_c_df <- bind_rows(lapply(names(questionnaire_map), function(q) {
  q_label <- questionnaire_map[[q]]
  comb_model <- combined_map[[q]]

  base_df <- res_gen %>%
    dplyr::filter(Model == q) %>%
    dplyr::select(PC, r_base = Real_Rho)

  comb_df <- res_gen %>%
    dplyr::filter(Model == comb_model) %>%
    dplyr::select(PC, r_combined = Real_Rho)

  base_df %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(comb_df, by = "PC") %>%
    dplyr::mutate(Questionnaire = q_label)
})) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Questionnaire = factor(Questionnaire, levels = questionnaire_levels),
    PC = factor(PC, levels = c("PC1", "PC2", "PC3"))
  )

# questionnaire-only vs questionnaire + MAILA
report_c <- panel_c_df %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    delta_r = r_combined - r_base
  )

report_c_summary <- report_c %>%
  dplyr::group_by(PC) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    mean_delta_r = mean(delta_r, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

add_delta_PC1 <- subset(report_c_summary, PC == "PC1")$mean_delta_r
add_delta_PC2 <- subset(report_c_summary, PC == "PC2")$mean_delta_r
add_delta_PC3 <- subset(report_c_summary, PC == "PC3")$mean_delta_r
```

We first tested whether MAILA preserved clinically meaningful structure beyond the questionnaire space on which it was trained (BSI and MHC-SF). Frozen MAILA models were applied to touch-based interactions from an independent validation sample of 500 participants who also completed the PHQ-9 and GAD-7. MAILA predictions remained aligned with PC1 (R = `r maila_PC1_r`, p = `r maila_PC1_p`), PC2 (R = `r maila_PC2_r`, p = `r maila_PC2_p`), and PC3 (R = `r maila_PC3_r`, p = `r maila_PC3_p`; averaged across touch-based interface and drawing tasks; Figure 5B).

To evaluate MAILA relative to standard symptom scales, we compared frozen MAILA predictions with questionnaire-only models based on GAD-7, PHQ-9, and PHQ-2 summary scores. GAD-7, PHQ-9, and PHQ-2 primarily measure depression and anxiety, providing a strong benchmark for the global distress axis (PC1) but a weaker benchmark for orthogonal components capturing other sources of mental-health variation. Consistent with this, MAILA reached `r maila_pct_PC1`% of the mean questionnaire-only benchmark on PC1 (`r maila_PC1_r` versus `r mean_q_PC1`). For PC2 (depression and interpersonal sensitivity vs. emotional wellbeing) and PC3 (somatization and hostility vs. obsession-compulsion), MAILA outperformed GAD-7, PHQ-9, and PHQ-2 by ΔR = `r maila_delta_PC2` and ΔR = `r maila_delta_PC3`, respectively (Figure 5B). Adding MAILA to questionnaire-only models further improved prediction by ΔR = `r add_delta_PC1`, `r add_delta_PC2`, and `r add_delta_PC3` for PC1, PC2, and PC3, respectively. These findings suggest that human–computer interactions capture mental-health information that is only partially reflected in conventional language-based measures of depression and anxiety.

```{r}
#| label: dimensional_scores
#| include: false

euclidean_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'euclidean_observed_correlation.csv')
euclidean_permuted_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'euclidean_permuted_rhos.csv')

cca_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'cca_observed_redundancy.csv')
cca_permuted_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'cca_permuted_redundancy.csv')

pls_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'pls_observed_r2.csv')
pls_permuted_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'pls_permuted_r2.csv')

procrustes_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'procrustes_observed_similarity.csv')
procrustes_permuted_path <- file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'procrustes_permuted_similarity.csv')

euclidean_observed <- read.csv(euclidean_path)
euclidean_permuted <- read.csv(euclidean_permuted_path)

observed_rho_euclidean <- euclidean_observed$Observed_Rho[1]
empirical_pval_euclidean <- euclidean_observed$Empirical_Pval[1]


cca_observed <- read.csv(cca_path)
cca_permuted <- read.csv(cca_permuted_path)


total_redundancy_Y_given_X <- cca_observed$Observed_Redundancy[1]
p_value_redundancy <- cca_observed$Empirical_Pval[1]


pls_observed <- read.csv(pls_path)
pls_permuted <- read.csv(pls_permuted_path)


observed_r2_pls <- pls_observed$Observed_R2[1]
p_empirical_pls <- pls_observed$Empirical_Pval[1]


procrustes_observed <- read.csv(procrustes_path)
procrustes_permuted <- read.csv(procrustes_permuted_path)


procrustes_similarity <- procrustes_observed$Observed_Procrustes_Similarity[1]
p_empirical_procrustes <- procrustes_observed$Empirical_Pval[1]

cca_correlations <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'cca_observed_correlations.csv'))
cca_loadings_mh <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'cca_loadings_mh.csv'), row.names = 1)
cca_loadings_mv <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'dimensions', 'cca_loadings_mv.csv'), row.names = 1)


significant_cca_components <- sum(cca_correlations$Empirical_Pval < 0.05)
observed_redundancy <- cca_observed$Observed_Redundancy[1]
p_value_redundancy <- cca_observed$Empirical_Pval[1]


cca_datasets <- read.csv(file.path(root, 'dimensions','cca', 'cca_datasets.csv'))
```
